awards.Description: GENOTYPING THE UNDERSTANDING AMERICA STUDY TO GENERATE NOVEL OPPORTUNITIES FOR RESEARCH ON COGNITIVE FUNCTIONING AND DEMENTIA - PROJECT SUMMARY / ABSTRACT COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT AND DEMENTIA ARE PREVALENT AND CAUSE SIGNIFICANT MORBIDITY AND SUBSTANTIAL FINANCIAL AND SOCIAL BURDEN. WITH THE RISING NUMBER OF CASES OF DEMENTIA IN THE U.S. AND WORLDWIDE, THERE IS AN URGENT NEED TO IDENTIFY OPPORTUNITIES FOR PREVENTING OR DELAYING ITS ONSET.  IN THIS INFRASTRUCTURE PROPOSAL, WE PROPOSE TO MAKE USE OF RECENT ADVANCES IN GENETICS BY GENOTYPING THE UNDERSTANDING AMERICA STUDY (UAS) AND CONSTRUCTING “POLYGENIC SCORES” (PGSS), INDEXES THAT AGGREGATE THE SMALL EFFECTS OF MILLIONS OF GENETIC VARIANTS FROM ACROSS THE GENOME, FOR USE IN SOCIAL-SCIENCE STUDIES OF FACTORS THAT INCREASE OR MITIGATE THE RISK OF ALZHEIMER’S DISEASE AND RELATED DEMENTIAS (ADRD). THE UAS, A PROBABILITY- BASED INTERNET PANEL HOUSED AT THE CENTER FOR ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL RESEARCH (CESR) AT THE UNIVERSITY OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA, LONGITUDINALLY TRACKS A SAMPLE OF APPROXIMATELY 10,000 ADULTS IN THE U.S. (GROWING TO AT LEAST 20,000 BY 2026). IT COMBINES SEVERAL SOURCES OF INFORMATION, INCLUDING FROM SURVEYS, WEARABLE DEVICES, ADMINISTRATIVE LINKAGES, AND CONTEXTUAL DATA, AND HAS SEVERAL UNIQUE FEATURES: IT PROVIDES THE OPPORTUNITY FOR ON- DEMAND DATA COLLECTION ON SHORT NOTICE; IT ALLOWS FOR THE COLLECTION OF DATA AT HIGHER FREQUENCIES AND FOR THE POSSIBILITY OF INITIATING NEW DATA COLLECTION IN RESPONSE TO MAJOR SOCIETAL EVENTS (SUCH AS THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC), OR TRIGGERED BY EVENTS IN THE LIVES OF RESPONDENTS (SUCH AS “BURST SURVEYS” FIELDED WHEN THERE IS AN IMPORTANT CHANGE IN THE LIFE OF A PANEL MEMBER); IT CAN BE USED TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF NATURAL EXPERIMENTS; IT ALLOWS FOR FREQUENT COLLECTION (ONCE OR TWICE A MONTH) OF PARADATA (COMPUTER USER-BEHAVIOR FROM SURVEYS, E.G., ERRORS AND PROCESSING SPEED GLEANED FROM KEYSTROKES) WHICH IS PREDICTIVE OF COGNITIVE FUNCTIONING.  OURS IS NOT A GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDY (GWAS). INSTEAD, WE WILL USE GENETIC VARIANTS (SNPS) IDENTIFIED FROM EXISTING LARGE, REPLICATED GWASS, TO CREATE POLYGENIC SCORES (PGSS), AND EXPLOIT UNIQUE UAS CAPABILITIES, AFFORDED BY ITS INTERNET MODE OF OPERATION, TO BETTER UNDERSTAND ADRD RISK IN A NATIONALLY REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE. WE WILL USE PGSS, AS WELL AS APOE-E4 STATUS, TOGETHER WITH LONGITUDINAL HEALTH, COGNITIVE, BEHAVIORAL, AND ENVIRONMENTAL MEASURES, TO: (I) IDENTIFY POPULATIONS AT RISK OF COGNITIVE DECLINE, (II) COLLECT NEW DATA FOR CAUSAL INFERENCES OF THE EFFECTS OF ADRD RISK/PROTECTIVE FACTORS ON COGNITION BY GENETIC ADRD RISK, AND III) STUDY THE ROLE OF GENETICS IN THE RESILIENCE TO ADVERSE LIFE EVENTS AFFECTING COGNITIVE FUNCTIONING.  BY MAKING PUBLICLY AVAILABLE A LARGE NUMBER OF GENETIC MEASURES FOR ADRD, COGNITIVE DECLINE, AND ASSOCIATED PROTECTIVE/RISK FACTORS (E.G., PHYSICAL ACTIVITY, CARDIOVASCULAR RISK [DIABETES, OBESITY, SMOKING AND HYPERTENSION], DIET, SLEEP, POLLUTION, AND EDUCATION, AMONG OTHERS), AND THROUGH OUR OWN RESEARCH, WE SEEK TO STIMULATE THE USE OF UNIQUE UAS CAPABILITIES IN ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL-SCIENCE RESEARCH OF ADRD, COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT, AND COGNITIVE DECLINE.

THE ALASKA VOLCANO HAZARD ASSESSMENT AND MONITORING COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE UNITED STATES GEOLOGICAL SURVEY (USGS) AND THE STATE OF ALASKA DIVISION OF GEOLOGICAL & GEOPHYSICAL SURVEYS (DGGS) SUPPORTS VOLCANO MONITORING, DATA COLLECTION, AND RESEARCH EFFORTS OF DGGS AS A PARTNER AGENCY OF THE ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY (AVO). WORK PERFORMED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS COORDINATED CLOSELY WITH THE OTHER TWO AVO PARTNERS: THE UNITED STATES GEOLOGICAL SURVEY (USGS) AND THE UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA FAIRBANKS GEOPHYSICAL INSTITUTE (UAF/GI).ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED INCLUDE: (1) MAINTENANCE AND POPULATION OF THE GEOLOGIC DATABASE OF INFORMATION ON VOLCANOES OF ALASKA (2) GEOLOGIC MAPPING, VOLCANO HAZARD STUDIES, AND ALASKA VOLCANO GEOSPATIAL DATA MANAGEMENT; VOLCANO TARGETS VARY BASED ON VOLCANIC ACTIVITY LEVEL BUT CURRENT STUDIES ARE FOCUSED ON OKMOK, SHISHALDIN, MAKUSHIN, GREAT SITKIN, PAVLOF, AND SEMISOPOCHNOI VOLCANOES; (3) ENGAGEMENT WITH THE PUBLIC THROUGH CONTENT CREATION AND DELIVERY OF INFORMATION THROUGH THE ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY WEBSITE, SOCIAL MEDIA PLATFORMS, WEEKLY RADIO PROGRAMS, AND DATA ACCESSIBILITY THROUGH ON-LINE QUERY PORTALS AT WWW.AVO.ALASKA.EDU, (4) CREATION OF DATABASE FIELDS AND WEB-BASED FORMS AND INTERFACES TO STORE AND ACCESS SEISMIC AND SATELLITE OBSERVATION DATA OF VOLCANIC ACTIVITY, (5) MAINTENANCE OF INTERAGENCY COMMUNICATION PLATFORMS THAT ALLOW RAPID ANALYSIS AND DISCUSSION OF VOLCANIC UNREST AMONGST ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY INTERAGENCY AND INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENTISTS AND TECHNICAL STAFF (6) MANAGEMENT OF AVIATION, MARINE, AND FUEL LOGISTICS FOR ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY FIELDWORK.DELIVERABLES INCLUDE: (1) THROUGH THE ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY WEBSITE: FREE, ON-LINE PUBLIC ACCESS TO VOLCANO MONITORING SEISMIC DATA STREAMS, CURRENT VOLCANIC ACTIVITY REPORTS, LIVE WEB CAMERA VIEWS OF VOLCANOES, AND ALASKA VOLCANO INFORMATION SUCH AS GEOCHEMICAL DATASETS, GEOLOGIC INVESTIGATION REPORTS, IMAGES, AND ERUPTIVE HISTORY NARRATIVES; (2) PUBLICATION OF WEEKLY VOLCANIC ACTIVITY REPORTS AND PEER-REVIEWED FREE-ACCESS GEOLOGIC MAPS OF SHISHALDIN AND OKMOK VOLCANOES; (3) WEEKLY RADIO PROGRAM BROADCAST THROUGHOUT ALASKA, HIGHLIGHTING VOLCANIC ACTIVITY IN ALASKA; (4) A DATABASE OF SEISMIC AND SATELLITE OBSERVATIONS; (5) A ROBUST WEB-ACCESSIBLE INTERNAL COMMUNICATIONS PLATFORM FOR INTERAGENCY SCIENTIST TO RESPOND TO VOLCANIC UNREST.INTENDED BENEFICIARIES OF THE ACTIVITIES AND DELIVERABLES SUPPORTED BY THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT GRANT ARE THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF ALASKA WHO MAY BE DIRECTLY IMPACTED BY VOLCANIC ACTIVITY SUCH AS ASH FALL EVENTS AND FOR THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND AVIATION COMMUNITY WHO RELY ON ALASKA VOLCANO OBSERVATORY SCIENTIFIC EXPERTISE AND VOLCANIC ACTIVITY ALERTS TO WARN OF DANGEROUS DRIFTING ASH CLOUDS THAT AFFECT BOTH LOCAL AIR TRAFFIC AND INTERNATIONAL NORTH PACIFIC AIR ROUTES. OTHER BENEFICIARIES ARE ACADEMIC RESEARCH SCIENTISTS WHO RELY ON FREELY ACCESSIBLE DATA PROVIDED BY THIS GRANT THAT HELP TO ADVANCE THE SCIENCE OF VOLCANOLOGY AND VOLCANO RISK ASSESSMENT.

awards.Description: CENTER FOR MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS IN CANCER (CMIC) - SUMMARY CANCER AFFECTS THE HEALTH OF MILLIONS OF AMERICANS. STUDYING MOLECULAR MECHANISMS THAT ENDOW CELLS WITH MALIGNANT PROPERTIES IS AN ESSENTIAL COMPONENT OF ADVANCING PRE-CLINICAL STUDIES AND A KEY PART OF EFFORTS TO IMPROVE PATIENT OUTCOMES. THE PURPOSE OF THIS COBRE SUBMISSION IS TO ESTABLISH THE CENTER FOR MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS IN CANCER (CMIC) AT THE UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS FOR MEDICAL SCIENCES (UAMS). THE MISSION OF THE CMIC IS TO STUDY MOLECULAR FEATURES AND FUNCTIONAL PROPERTIES OF BIOMOLECULES THAT DRIVE CANCER. THE UNIFYING THEME OF RESEARCH AMONG CENTER MEMBERS IS THE COUPLING OF STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY AND HIGH-RESOLUTION IMAGING WITH PRECISE, QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS OF BIOCHEMICAL AND CELLULAR PROCESSES TO UNDERSTAND HOW MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS GOVERN THE INITIATION, PROGRESSION AND TREATMENT OF CANCER. IN PHASE 1 OF THIS COBRE, WE WILL PURSUE THE FOLLOWING AIMS: (AIM 1) ESTABLISH THE CMICCREATING AN ORGANIZATIONAL FRAMEWORK THAT ENSURES THE SUCCESS OF JUNIOR INVESTIGATORS AS INDEPENDENT SCIENTISTS AND STRENGTHENS THE RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURE AT UAMS AND ITS AFFILIATE INSTITUTIONS, (AIM 2) BUILD A THEMATIC, MULTIDISCIPLINARY COBRE CENTER WITH UNIQUE COLLABORATIVE OPPORTUNITIES AND STRONG RESEARCH PROGRAMS FOCUSED ON UNDERSTANDING THE MOLECULAR BASIS OF CANCER, AND (AIM 3) IMPLEMENT A PLAN FOR LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF THE CMIC. THE ORGANIZATION OF THE CMIC WILL INCLUDE 5 RESEARCH PROJECTS, AN ADMINISTRATIVE CORE, AND 2 NEW RESEARCH CORESTHE STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY CORE AND THE BIOMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS CORE. THE ADMINISTRATIVE CORE WILL OVERSEE OPERATION OF THE CENTER, INCLUDING A PILOT PROJECT PROGRAM TO FUEL THE PIPELINE OF REPLACEMENT RESEARCH PROJECT LEADERS (RPLS) AND A FORMALIZED FACULTY DEVELOPMENT PLAN THAT INCLUDES INDIVIDUALIZED MENTORING. THE STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY CORE WILL HELP RPLS PLAN AND EXECUTE STRUCTURE-FUNCTION STUDIES BY ENSURING HIGH-QUALITY SAMPLE PREPARATION AND PROVIDING ACCESS TO WORLD-CLASS INSTRUMENTATION AND COMPUTATIONAL RESOURCES REQUIRED FOR HIGH-RESOLUTION STRUCTURAL STUDIES. THE BIOMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS CORE WILL ENABLE RESEARCHERS TO PERFORM QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS OF MACROMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS AND DYNAMICS DOWN TO THE LEVEL OF SINGLE-MOLECULES. BOTH RESEARCH CORES WILL ASSIST USERS IN EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN, DATA ANALYSIS AND INTERPRETATION OF RESULTS, WHILE ALSO PROVIDING TRAINING AND EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH. THE CMIC WILL ADDRESS AN INSTITUTIONAL PRIORITY AT UAMS AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE MISSION OF THE WINTHROP P. ROCKEFELLER CANCER INSTITUTE BY CREATING A CRITICAL MASS OF INVESTIGATORS WHO ARE ABLE TO GAIN MOLECULAR-LEVEL INSIGHTS INTO MECHANISMS THAT ARE CRUCIAL FOR A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF CANCER BIOLOGY. OUR LONG-TERM GOAL IS TO LEVERAGE FACULTY MENTORING, STRATEGIC RECRUITMENT, AND CUTTING-EDGE CORE RESOURCES TO DEVELOP A CRITICAL MASS OF INVESTIGATORS THAT WILL SUPPORT A SELF- SUSTAINING CENTER IN WHICH RESEARCH ADVANCES OUR KNOWLEDGE OF CANCER THROUGH PRECISE AND COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSES OF MOLECULAR EVENTS THAT IMPACT MALIGNANT PATHOGENESIS.

METHANE SNIFFER SMALL UNMANNED SERIAL SYSTEM (MS-SUAV)  THE CONTRACT IS THE SPONSORING AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE NATIONAL AERONAUTICS AND SPACE ADMINISTRATION (NASA) AND THE CALIFORNIA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (CONTRACTOR), A PRIVATE NONPROFIT EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION, WHICH ESTABLISHES THE RELATIONSHIP FOR THE OPERATION OF THE FEDERALLY FUNDED RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT CENTER (FFRDC) KNOWN AS THE JET PROPULSION LABORATORY. THE CONTRACTOR, JPL, IS REQUIRED TO PERFORM WORK THAT IS DESIGNATED IN TASK ORDERS ISSUED BY THE NASA MANAGEMENT OFFICE (NMO) CONTRACTING OFFICERS.   NASA - SPONSORED WORK: JPL'S PRIMARY MISSION IS TO SUPPORT THE NASA SCIENCE MISSION DIRECTORATE (SMD) IN CARRYING OUT THE SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES IDENTIFIED IN THE SMD SCIENCE PLAN.  THE FOUR BROAD SCIENTIFIC AREAS ARE: EARTH SCIENCE, PLANETARY SCIENCE, HELIOPHYSICS, AND ASTROPHYSICS.  IN PERFORMANCE OF THIS CONTRACT, JPL SHALL:   1) SUPPLY A BROAD BASE OF SCIENTIFIC AND TECHNICAL CAPABILITIES RELEVANT TO NASA PROGRAM AND PROJECT RESPONSIBILITIES AND ASSIGNMENTS. 2) FOSTER ITS UNIQUE RELATIONSHIP WITH A TOP-TIER UNIVERSITY TO FACILITATE THE INVOLVEMENT OF SCIENTISTS, ENGINEERS, AND STUDENTS FROM THE UNIVERSITY AND RESEARCH COMMUNITIES IN NASA MISSION AND IN SUPPORTING OTHER GOVERNMENT AGENCIES. 3) SUPPORT NASA IN ENABLING PROGRAM AND INSTITUTIONAL CAPABILITIES. 4) DEVELOP SPACECRAFT AND INSTRUMENTS THAT ARE SENT TO VARIOUS DESTINATIONS WITH OUR SOLAR SYSTEM, INCLUDING EARTH ORBIT, PLANETS, PLANETARY SATELLITES, ASTEROIDS, AND COMETS.  THESE MISSIONS MAY BE ORBITERS, LANDERS, OR ROVERS, AND TYPICALLY INCLUDE CUTTING-EDGE TECHNOLOGIES REQUIRED TO MEET SCIENTIFIC REQUIREMENTS. 5) PERFORM PROJECT TASKS INVOLVING: (I) AUTONOMOUS DEEP-SPACE, INNER-SPACE AND EARTH-ORBITING SPACECRAFT OR MAJOR SUBSYSTEMS, (II) EXPERIMENTS, INSTRUMENTS, OR OTHER DEVICES WHICH MAY BE CARRIED AS PAYLOAD ON SPACECRAFTS IN MISSIONS MANAGED BY OTHERS, AND/OR (III) GROUND-BASED SYSTEMS. 6) BEYOND ITS PRIMARY MISSION, JPL MAY PERFORM WORK FOR OTHER NASA MISSION DIRECTORATES OR OFFICES. 7) JPL IS ALSO RESPONSIBLE FOR THE OPERATION, RESEARCH, TECHNOLOGY INSERTION, AND MANAGEMENT OF NASA S DEEP SPACE NETWORK TO PROVIDE TELECOMMUNICATION AND OPERATION SERVICES, INCLUDING DATA ACQUISITION AND DATA DELIVERY REQUIRED TO MEET ESTABLISHED AGENCY OBJECTIVES. 8) JPL IS ALSO REQUIRED TO MAINTAIN AND CONDUCT AN EDUCATION PROGRAM IN CLOSE COORDINATION WITH THE NASA HEADQUARTERS EDUCATION OFFICE, THE ACADEMIC COMMUNITY AT LARGE, AND IN SUPPORT TO NASA STRATEGIC OBJECTIVES TO IMPROVE STUDENT RETENTION IN THE SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, ENGINEERING, AND MATHEMATICS (STEM) DISCIPLINES.   WORK FOR NON-NASA SPONSORS:  JPL MAY PERFORM WORK FOR NON-NASA SPONSORS.  THIS WORK WILL BE DESIGNATED IN TASK ORDERS ISSUED BY NMO CONTRACTING OFFICERS BASED ON TASK PLANS INITIATED BY THE CONTRACTOR.

PURPOSE: THE PURPOSE OF THIS GRANT IS TO ASSIST WITH THE DEVELOP AND TO VALIDATE STANDARD TEST METHODS FOR SMALL UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (AKA DRONES) USED BY PUBLIC SAFETY AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE ORGANIZATIONS IN CONFINED URBAN ENVIRONMENTS. ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED: THE GRANTEE WILL HELP DEVELOP AND VALIDATE STANDARD TEST METHODS FOR DRONES THAT CAN BE USED TO EVALUATE SYSTEM CAPABILITIES AND REMOTE PILOT PROFICIENCY. THEY WILL PROVIDE TECHNICAL EXPERTISE AND USE CASES FROM PUBLIC SAFETY AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE ORGANIZATIONS INTERESTED IN USING SUCH STANDARDS TO SUPPORT PURCHASING DECISIONS, FOCUS TRAINING, AND EVALUATE LEVELS OF REMOTE PILOT PROFICIENCY FOR CREDENTIALING. THIS WILL HELP PRIORITIZE TEST METHODS FOR THE STANDARDIZATION PROCESS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES: THIS GRANT WILL PRODUCE A SERIES OF DRAFT TEST METHODS FOR BALLOTING THROUGH THE ASTM INTERNATIONAL STANDARDS COMMITTEE ON HOMELAND SECURITY APPLICATIONS; RESPONSE ROBOTS (E54.09). ALSO, POTENTIALLY NEW USE CASES FROM PUBLIC SAFETY AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE ORGANIZATIONS WITH THRESHOLDS OF ACCEPTABLE PERFORMANCE IN EACH TEST METHOD FOR VARIOUS AIRCRAFT BASED ON THEIR INTENDED MISSIONS, AIRSPACE RESTRICTIONS, AND ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES: THE BENEFICIARIES WILL BE PUBLIC SAFETY AND EMERGENCY RESPONSE ORGANIZATIONS NATIONWIDE WHO WILL USE THE RESULTING TEST METHODS TO GUIDE PROCUREMENTS AND TRAIN WITH MEASURES OF REMOTE PILOT PROFICIENCY. THE PUBLIC WILL ALSO BENEFIT AS EMERGENCY RESPONSE ORGANIZATIONS BECOME EQUIPPED WITH BETTER LIFE-SAVING TOOLS AND TACTICS, ALONG WITH STANDARDS-BASED CREDENTIALING THAT WILL FACILITATE MUTUAL AID AT LARGE SCALE DISASTERS. SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES: THE RECIPIENT DOES NOT INTEND TO SUBAWARD FUNDS.

COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT FUNDING OF THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP, EQUIP AND DEPLOY A SMALL UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (SUAS) PROGRAM, INFORMALLY REFERRED TO AS A DRONE PROGRAM. THE ARIZONA DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE SUAS PROGRAM AS DESIGNED WILL INCREASE RESPONDER SAFETY, SAVE LIVES, ALLOW FOR A MORE EFFICIENT AND EFFECTIVE RESPONSE, PROTECT AND PRESERVE PROPERTY AND ENHANCE THE DEPARTMENT’S CAPABILITY TO DEVELOP SITE SPECIFIC BIOSECURITY PLANS IN PARTNERSHIP WITH STAKEHOLDERS USING REAL-TIME AND CURRENT DIGITAL AERIAL IMAGERY, RESPOND TO ANIMAL HEALTH DISEASE OUTBREAKS, AIDING RESPONSE TO LIVESTOCK IN DISASTER SCENARIOS, AND SUPPORT FOR ANIMAL CRUELTY INVESTIGATIONS WHICH FREQUENTLY HAVE COMPONENTS OF ILLEGAL ANIMAL IMPORTATION SUCH AS THE CASE OF COCKFIGHTING OPERATIONS AND ILLEGAL IMPORTATION OF BIRDS FROM MEXICO. ACTIVITIES CONDUCTED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT INCLUDE (1) PROGRAM POLICY DEVELOPMENT, (2A) MANAGEMENT OF STAFF ATTENDANCE AND TRAINING AS REQUIRED UNDER 14 CFR PART 61 AND FAA PART 107 TO OWN, OPERATE AND REMOTE PILOT SUAS, (2B) OBTAIN LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS (COA) AND SPECIAL GOVERNMENT INTEREST WAIVERS AND (3) PROCURE TWO SUAS THAT ARE BLUE LIST COMPLIANT. SUAS CAPABILITIES INCLUDE LIGHT DETECTION AND RANGING (LIDAR), TELESCOPIC, AND THERMAL IMAGING, AND RADIO FREQUENCY IDENTIFICATION (RFID) SYSTEMS TECHNOLOGY TO IDENTIFY FEBRILE ANIMALS, IDENTIFY DECEASED ANIMALS, REMOTE IDENTIFICATION OF LIVESTOCK AT LARGE, VERIFY EFFECTIVENESS OF MOVEMENT CONTROLS, PROVIDE ACCURATE HEAD COUNTS AND SAFELY ENABLE ANIMAL DISEASE TRACEABILITY WITHOUT NEED FOR EXTENSIVE OR PORTABLE ANIMAL HANDLING FACILITIES. DELIVERABLES INCLUDE A FUNCTIONAL VERSATILE SUAS ASSETS FOR DEPLOYMENT UNDER VARIOUS ENVIRONME NTAL CONDITIONS FOR ALL-HAZARDS AND FOREIGN ANIMAL DISEASE (FAD) INVESTIGATION AND RESPONSE, SUAS PILOT LICENSES; ANIMAL AGRICULTURE SPECIFIC SOPS, SUAS POLICY DEPLOYMENT FOR STATE ANIMAL HEALTH OFFICIALS, AND BEST PRACTICE DOCUMENTS FOR DIGITAL EVIDENCE, DATA COLLECTION AND PRIVACY POLICY DEVELOPMENT, TRAINING AND EXERCISE SCHEDULE (4 PER CALENDAR YEAR). STAKEHOLDER BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE STAT EWIDE ANIMAL AGRICULTURAL INDUSTRIES AND COMMODITY GROUPS, STATE ANIMAL HEALTH OFFICIALS AND COLLABORATORS FOR EXPANDED AND NOVEL SURVEILLANCE TECHNIQUES, ADVANCED SITUATIONAL AWARENESS, IMPROVED RESPONDER SAFETY AND MORE EFFICIENT USE OF RESOURCES.

AWARD PURPOSE THE PURPOSE OF THE NEW COLONELS ISLAND TERMINAL BERTH NO. 4 IS TO SUPPORT INCREASED THROUGHPUT OF RO RO CARGO AT COLONELS ISLAND TERMINAL CIT.  THE NEW BERTH WILL ALSO BE CAPABLE OF SERVICING THE LARGER FUTURE VESSELS AT THE BERTH. THE PROJECT INCLUDES I THE DEMOLITION AND DISPOSAL OF THE EXISTING STRUCTURES AND DREDGING  THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE NEW DOCK STRUCTURE DOCK APPROACH WAY AND CONNECTOR BRIDGE AND MARINE STRUCTURES AND 3 THE CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT EFFORTS NECESSARY TO COMPLETE THE PROJECT.  ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED. THE PROJECT WILL BE COMPLETED IN TWO COMPONENTS UNDER TWO SEPARATE CONTRACTS FINAL ENGINEERING COMPLETE FINAL ENGINEERING AND DESIGN FOR THE PROJECT.  BERTH CONSTRUCTION DREDGING DREDGING FOR THE PROJECT CONSISTS OF HYDRAULIC DREDGING OF THE PROPOSED BERTH TO A DEPTH MATCHING THE SOUTH BRUNSWICK RIVER DEPTH OF 36.0 FEET MEAN LOW WATER MLW WITH AN ALLOWANCE FOR ADVANCE MAINTENANCE AND OVER DREDGE OF 4.0 FEET 40.0 MLW.  THE TOTAL VOLUME OF HYDRAULIC DREDGING IS APPROXIMATELY 385000 CUBIC YARDS CY. HYDRAULIC DREDGE MATERIAL WILL BE PLACED WITHIN THE ANDREWS ISLAND CONFINED DISPOSAL FACILITY.  
PIER CONSTRUCTION AND OTHER WORKS DOCK STRUCTURE CONSTRUCT THE BERTH NO. 4 MAIN DOCK A PILE SUPPORTED CONCRETE SURFACE RO RO VESSEL DOCK MEASURING NOMINALLY 600 FEET LONG AND 140 FEET WIDE.  THE PROPOSED STRUCTURE WILL UTILIZE PRECAST PILE CAPS AND DECK PANELS TO MINIMIZE OVER-THE-WATER CONSTRUCTION ACTIVITY.  THE FACILITY WILL BE CAPABLE OF ACCOMMODATING A RO RO VESSEL UP TO ROUGHLY 970 FEET LONG AND 134 FEET WIDE. BOLLARDS AND CONE TYPE FENDERS WILL BE INSTALLED TO SUPPORT THE DESIGN VESSELS. DOCK APPROACH WAY AND DOCK CONNECTOR BRIDGE CONSTRUCT A PILE SUPPORTED ACCESS BRIDGE CONNECTING BERTH NO. 4 WITH THE MAIN ACCESS ROAD JOE FRANK HARRIS BOULEVARD TO PROVIDE FOR MOVEMENT OF AUTOMOBILES AND OTHER SELF-PROPELLED MACHINERY BETWEEN THE VESSEL BERTH AND STORAGE LOCATIONS AND CUSTOMER FACILITIES LOCATED IN OTHER PARTS OF THE CIT.  CONSTRUCT A DOCK CONNECTOR BETWEEN BERTHS NO. 1 AND NO. 4. THE DOCK CONNECTOR PROVIDES ACCESS FOR HIGH AND HEAVY CARGO TO TRAVEL FROM BERTH NO. 4 ACROSS BERTH NO. 1 AND INTO THE NORTHWEST STORAGE LOTS WITHIN CIT. BERTH NO. 1 STRENGTHENING UPGRADE THE LANDSIDE PORTION OF EXISTING BERTH NO. 1 TO PROVIDE A DIRECT ACCESS ROUTE FOR THE MOVEMENT OF HEAVY EQUIPMENT BETWEEN BERTHS NO. 1 AND NO. 4 AND TERMINAL STORAGE AREAS ON THE MAINLAND. THIS UPGRADE WILL STREAMLINE CARGO MOVEMENTS BETWEEN THE BERTHS AND THE HEAVY EQUIPMENT STORAGE AREA WHICH WILL IMPROVE OPERATIONAL THROUGHPUT AND EFFICIENCY. MARINE STRUCTURES: CONSTRUCT THREE PILE SUPPORTED BREASTING STRUCTURES AND FOUR PILE SUPPORTED MOORING DOLPHINS EXTENDING EAST DOWNRIVER FROM THE END OF THE BERTH TO SECURE THE BOW OF THE SHIP WHEN MOORED AT THE BERTH.  THE MARINE STRUCTURES ARE CONNECTED TO THE BERTH VIA PILE SUPPORTED CATWALKS SPANNING BETWEEN THE STRUCTURES. ADDITIONAL ELEMENTS INCLUDE WETLANDS MITIGATION: ENHANCE THE EXISTING WETLANDS BEHIND BERTH NO. 1 ALL MITIGATION WILL OCCUR ONSITE. ELECTRICAL EXTENSION OF THE ELECTRICAL FEED FROM EXISTING CIT BERTH NO. 1 TO BERTH NO. 4 INSTALLATION OF AREA LIGHTING ALONG THE APPROACH WAY CONNECTOR BRIDGE AND DOCK PLATFORM PROVISION OF SHIP HOTEL POWER AT THE DOCK FACE IN PRECAST ELECTRICAL VAULTS AND WORK LIGHTING AT EACH OF THE BREASTING AND MOORING DOLPHINS.  MECHANICAL INSTALLATION OF POTABLE FIRE WATER PIPING VAULTS AND FIXTURES AT THE PROPOSED DOCK.  WATER SERVICE FOR THE PROPOSED BERTH WILL COME FROM THE EXISTING WATERMAIN LOCATED LANDSIDE OF THE DOWNSTREAM END OF EXISTING BERTH 1.  CIVIL WORK BACKFILLING GRADING AND COMPACTING SELECT MATERIAL FOR SUBGRADE, INSTALLATION OF A GRADED AGGREGATE BASE COURSE, PAVING WITH HOT MIX ASPHALTIC CONCRETE, AND INSTALLATION OF TRAFFIC CONTROL DEVICES SUCH AS SIGNAGE AND STRIPING. THIS WORK IS LIMITED TO THE PORTION OF THE PROJECT WHERE THE PROPOSED DOCK APPROACH WAY INTERSECTS WITH THE EXISTING JOE FRANK HARRIS BOULEVARD.  CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT THE INSPECTION AND CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT FOR THE PROJECT WILL CONSIST OF DAILY AND WEEKLY INSPECTION REPORTS INTERPRETATION OF CONSTRUCTION PLANS AND SPECIFICATIONS, ON-SITE PROBLEM SOLVING, COORDINATION WITH THE RECIPIENT PORT OPERATIONS TO ENSURE MINIMAL IMPACT TO MARITIME OPERATIONS SHOP DRAWING REVIEW ENGINEER OF RECORD SERVICES AND MANAGEMENT OF THE ENTIRE CONSTRUCTION PROJECT. GEOTECHNICAL AND TESTING SERVICES: THE GEOTECHNICAL AND TESTING SERVICES FOR THE PROJECT WILL CONSIST OF ALL GEOTECHNICAL AND PILE DRIVING TESTING AND VERIFICATION SERVICES TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH APPROVED CONSTRUCTION PLANS AND SPECIFICATIONS. PHOTOGRAPHIC DOCUMENTATION AERIAL DRONE EQUIPMENT FOR WEEKLY IN HOUSE PHOTOGRAPHIC DOCUMENTATION OF THE PROJECT. DELIVERABLES EXPECTED OUTCOMES PERFORMANCE MEASURE TABLE FROM ATTACHMENT C PAGE 9 OF THE GRANT AGREEMENT CARGO VOLUMES. TOTAL TONS OF CARGO MOVED PER QUARTER TEUS OR SHORT TONS. CARGO VOLUME MEASURES THE MOVEMENT OF FREIGHT THROUGH THE PROJECT STUDY AREA. TOTAL VESSEL CALLS PER QUARTER.   MEASURES THE NUMBER OF VESSELS CALLING TO THE PROJECT STUDY AREA. MUST BE REPORTED IN TOTAL AND DISAGGREGATED BY ANY OF THE FOLLOWING WHERE APPLICABLE: VESSEL TYPE EG CONTAINER BULK RO RO LNG FREIGHT CAPACITY VESSEL LENGTH. TRUCK MILES REDUCED.  NUMBER OF TRUCK MILES REDUCED PER QUARTER. TOTAL TRUCK MILES REDUCED, CALCULATED INTO REDUCED GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS, CARBON MONOXIDE, AND PARTICULATE MATTER FOR MODE OF TRANSPORTATION DEFINED IN THE PROJECT STUDY AREA.  INTENDED BENEFICIARY GPA.  SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES NONE.

UNIVERSITY OF MISSOURI EXTENSION STRIVES TO BETTER THE LIVES OF MISSOURIANS THROUGH PROGRAMS FOCUSED ON ACHIEVING IMPACTS IN AGRICULTURE AND THE ENVIRONMENT; 4-H YOUTH DEVELOPMENT; FAMILY, NUTRITION AND CONSUMER SCIENCES; BUSINESS AND COMMUNITY; AND HEALTH AND SAFETY. WE DEVELOP AND DELIVER HIGH PRIORITY RESEARCH PROJECTS AND EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS TO ADDRESS NEEDS IDENTIFIED BY OUR STAKEHOLDERS. WE REACH NEARLY A MILLION MISSOURIANS EVERY YEAR THROUGH CAMPUS FACULTY AND COUNTY EXTENSION OFFICES WORKING THROUGHOUT THE STATE'S 114 COUNTIES AND THE CITY OF ST. LOUIS. WE INCORPORATE THE USE OF TECHNOLOGY INTO INNOVATIVE SERVICE AND PRODUCT DELIVERY SYSTEMS, ONLINE RESOURCES FOR OUR STAKEHOLDERS, AND DATA MAPPING, VISUALIZATION, AND REPORTING TOOLS. FUNDING FROM COMPETITIVE GRANTS, GIFTS, AND FEE GENERATION EXCEED THE RESOURCES APPROPRIATED FROM OUR STATE, FEDERAL, AND COUNTY PARTNERS. OUR GOAL IS TO BE RELIABLE, RESPONSIVE AND RELEVANT. WE ACCOMPLISH THAT GOAL BY PROVIDING RESEARCH-BASED KNOWLEDGE TO MISSOURIANS ALIGNED WITH THEIR PRIORITIES OF IMPROVING COMMUNITY ECONOMIES, HEALTH, AND EDUCATION OUTCOMES. WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY WE SEEK TO DEVELOP INNOVATIVE PARTNERSHIPS FOR ECONOMIC AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT THAT WILL GROW OPPORTUNITIES FOR MISSOURIANS, ENGAGE MISSOURIANS IN ATTAINING SKILLS AND CREDENTIALS, EXPAND THE UNIVERSITY'S ECONOMIC IMPACT, AND WILL DOUBLE THE AGRICULTURE ECONOMY BY 2030 WHILE SUSTAINING NATURAL RESOURCES. OUTSIDE OF MISSOURI'S MAJOR URBAN CENTERS IT CAN BE DIFFICULT TO SUSTAIN AN ENTREPRENEURIAL ECOSYSTEM CONDUCIVE TO THE GROWTH OF LOCALLY OWNED SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISES. MU EXTENSION'S CREATING ENTREPRENEURIAL COMMUNITIES CONFERENCE CREATES A STATEWIDE NETWORK OF COMMUNITIES FOCUSED ON ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND THE DEVELOPMENT OF STRONG ENTREPRENEURIAL ECOSYSTEMS. BY BRINGING TOGETHER COMMUNITY STAKEHOLDERS, BUSINESS LEADERS AND POLICYMAKERS TO SHARE EVIDENCE-BASED BEST PRACTICES AND IDEAS, THIS CONFERENCE PROMOTES RESILIENCY, SUSTAINABILITY, JOB GROWTH, AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT IN COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE STATE OF MISSOURI. CONFERENCE SESSIONS ARE EMBEDDED IN SMALL BUSINESSES THROUGHOUT THE HOST COMMUNITY SO PARTICIPANTS CAN SEE, IN REAL TIME, THE IMPACT OF GOOD POLICYMAKING AND IMPLEMENTATION ON A COMMUNITY'S ENTREPRENEURIAL ECOSYSTEM. THE MISSOURI BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER FOR AGRICULTURE, FOOD AND FORESTRY, ESTABLISHED THROUGH FUNDING FROM THE CARES ACT, IS A CO LLABORATIVE PROGRAM THAT BRINGS NEW ENTREPRENEURS INTO THE AGRICULTURE AND FOOD SPACE. THE CENTER BRINGS TOGETHER THE BUSINESS KNOWLEDGE AND RESOURCES OF THE MISSOURI SBDC AND THE RESEARCH-BASED, AGRICULTURE-SPECIFIC KNOWLEDGE OF MU EXTENSION. IN 2021, THE MISSOURI BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CENTER FOR AGRICULTURE, FOOD AND FORESTRY PROVIDED TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND EXPERTISE TO OVER 300 MISSOURI PRODUCERS AND AGRIBUSINESSES. THOSE AGRI-ENTREPRENEURS ATTRACTED MORE THAN $40M IN PRIVATE LOANS AND GRANTS AND CREATED AT LEAST 500 NEW JOBS. MU EXTENSION'S AGRICULTURE AND ENVIRONMENT PROGRAM INTENDS TO DOUBLE THE VALUE OF AGRICULTURE IN MISSOURI BY 2030 WHILE SUSTAINING OUR NATURAL RESOURCES. SUPPLY CHAIN ISSUES ARE A MAJOR THREAT TO THAT GOAL. TWO SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES (SOLUTION-BASED) THAT ADDRESS THESE ISSUES ARE 1) HARNESS DIGITAL TECHNOLOGIES TO REDUCE THE LABOR AND NUMBER OF ENTITIES BETWEEN FOOD PRODUCERS AND CONSUMERS 2) TRAIN FARMERS, FARM WORKERS, AND OTHERS IN THE FOOD SYSTEM ABOUT HOW TO USE THOSE TECHNOLOGIES. WE RETOOLED OUR MEAT TEAM TO FOCUS ON WORKER TRAINING FOR SMALL AND MEDIUM-SIZED PROCESSORS. TWO NEW, CUSTOMIZED MOBILE TRAINING UNITS WILL ARRIVE IN MISSOURI IN MARCH 2022. THOSE STATE-OF-THE-ART UNITS DEMONSTRATE THE NEWEST TECHNOLOGIES. WE HAVE A WAITING LIST OF PROCESSORS WHO WANT US TO TRAIN LOCAL WORKERS. WE EXPANDED OUR WORK IN PRECISION AGRICULTURE TO HELP FARMERS AND PROCESSORS EMPLOY DIGITAL TECHNOLOGIES. THE TECHNOLOGIES REQUIRE LESS MUNDANE LABOR AND GIVE GREATER MANAGERIAL FLEXIBILITY. THINK ROBOTS, DRONES, ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND A CONNECTED FOOD SYSTEM. OUR TRAINING IN DIGITAL AGRICULTURE COVERS INNOVATIONS IN PLANT AND ANIMAL SCIENCES AS WELL AS FOOD PROCESSING. OUR AG BUSINESS AND POLICY TEAM HELPS FARMERS COPE WITH SUPPLY CHAIN ISSUES AND FIND POSSIBLE PATHS TO DIRECT MARKETING. THAT SHORTENS THE SUPPLY CHAIN AND ADDS RESILIENCY TO THE FOOD SYSTEM. MU EXTENSION'S COMMUNITY HEALTH ENGAGEMENT AND OUTREACH AND LABOR AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS ARE CURRENTLY WORKING AT THE INTERSECTION OF SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT. MU EXTENSION IS CREATING CAPACITY AND DEVELOPING THE FRAMEWORK AND GUIDELINES FOR TRAINING AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO BUSINESSES AND LABOR ORGANIZATIONS TO BE DESIGNATED AS RECOVERY-FRIENDLY WORKPLACES. EMPLOYERS AND BUSINESSES - BUSINESSES AND ORGANIZATIONS WILL INCREASE THEIR UNDERSTANDING ABOUT SUBSTANCE MISUSE, BEHAVIORAL HEALTH, AND THEIR CONNECTION TO WORKFORCE CHALLENGES THROUGH TRAININGS, A SERIES OF EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS, AND THE CREATION AND SHARING OF RESOURCES; CREATE A NETWORK OF BUSINESSES AND ORGANIZATIONS INTERESTED IN CREATING HEALTHY AND SAFE WORK ENVIRONMENTS WHERE EMPLOYERS, EMPLOYEES, AND COMMUNITIES CAN COLLABORATE TO CREATE POSITIVE CHANGE, ELIMINATE STIGMA AND SUPPORT RECOVERY; AND, RETAIN A HEALTHIER, MORE PRODUCTIVE, AND MORE MOTIVATED WORKFORCE THROUGH THE DELIVERY OF HEALTH AND SAFETY PROGRAMS AND PRACTICES. HEALTH AND HEALTHY FUTURES WE SEEK TO IMPROVE THE HEALTH OF MISSOURI COMMUNITIES AND INCREASE POSITIVE HEALTH OUTCOMES THROUGH EDUCATION RELATED TO NUTRITION AND OTHER HEALTH BEHAVIORS. OUR FALL RISK REDUCTION PROGRAMS ARE NEEDED FOR MISSOURI'S LARGE AND GROWING OLDER ADULT POPULATION. SUPPORTING AND OFFERING FALL PREVENTION PROGRAMS THROUGH MU EXTENSION AND OUR PARTNER NETWORK ALLOWS US TO ENGAGE MORE OLDER ADULTS IN EVIDENCE-BASED FALL PREVENTION INTERVENTIONS WHICH DECREASE FALLING, THE FEAR OF FALLING, INCREASE ACTIVITY LEVELS, AND IMPROVE OUTLOOK ON LIFE. THESE SHORT-TERM OUTCOMES OFTEN LEAD TO LONG-TERM BENEFITS, SUCH AS INCREASED INDEPENDENCE, LONGEVITY, AND REDUCED ECONOMIC BURDEN. THROUGH THIS PROJECT WE HAVE REDUCED THE PERCENTAGE OF OLDER ADULTS WHO REPORT FALLING IN MISSOURI, REDUCED THE NUMBER OF INJURIES RELATED TO FALLS, DECREASED THE NUMBER OF OLDER ADULTS WHO FEAR FALLING, REDUCED MEDICAL CARE COSTS, AND INCREASED THE NUMBER OF OLDER ADULTS MEETING THE PHYSICAL ACTIVITY GUIDELINES. RURAL MISSOURI IS FACING A MENTAL HEALTH CRISIS, FUELED BY GROWING ECONOMIC AND MENTAL HEALTH CHALLENGES THAT ARE SEVERELY AFFECTING THE AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITY. THESE STRESSORS HAVE CONTRIBUTED TO SUBSTANTIAL AND CRITICAL MENTAL HEALTH ISSUES AMONG RURAL MISSOURIANS, ESPECIALLY FARMERS AND RANCHERS. DESPITE THE CLEAR MENTAL HEALTH NEEDS OF RURAL MISSOURIANS, TOOLS AND SUPPORT ARE OFTEN LIMITED. OUR FARM AND RANCH STRESS PROGRAMS USE FIELD TESTED WORKSHOPS, RURAL PARTICIPANTS WERE EQUIPPED WITH THE TOOLS TO IMPROVE THEIR OWN MENTAL HEALTH, WERE CONNECTED TO USEFUL MENTAL HEALTH RESOURCES AND WERE GIVEN FREE ACCESS TO ONE-ON-ONE TELEPSYCHOLOGY (ONLINE THERAPY) SESSIONS. OUR STAY STRONG, STAY HEALTHY RESISTANCE TRAINING PROGRAM, HAS BEEN SHOWN TO SIGNIFICANTLY IMPROVE MUSCLE STRENGTH, FLEXIBILITY, AND BALANCE IN OLDER ADULTS OF ALL AGES AND FITNESS LEVELS. THIS IS AN EVIDENCE-BASED RESISTANCE TRAINING PROGRAM THAT IS AFFORDABLE, A CCESSIBLE, AND HIGHLY BENEFICIAL TO A LARGE PROPORTION OF OLDER ADULTS. SOCIAL JUSTICE MU EXTENSION WORKS TOWARD SOCIAL JUSTICE BY PROVIDING RESEARCHED-BASED AND EVIDENCED-BASED EDUCATION, SERVICES, AND SUPPORT IN LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT, NUTRITION, HOME <(>&<)> FAMILIES, HEALTH AND SAFETY, YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, AND WORKFORCE READINESS. IN ADDITION, WE ARE EMPOWERING RESIDENTS OF ALL AGES TO ADVOCATE FOR CHANGES TO ADDRESS THE INEQUITIES THAT PLAGUE THEIR LIVES AND COMMUNITIES. ENVIRONMENTAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES MU EXTENSION FORESTRY AND WILDLIFE INITIATIVES SUCH AS MISSOURI MASTER NATURALIST, MISSOURI MASTER POLLINATOR STEWARD, MISSOURI NATIVE GRASS EXTENSION PROJECT, CONTROLLING NUISANCE WILDLIFE PROGRAM, AND MISSOURI FERAL HOG EXTENSION PROJECT, PROVIDE MISSOURI CITIZENS WITH RESEARCH-BASED INFORMATION THAT ENABLES INDIVIDUALS AND COMMUNITIES TO MAKE INFORMED DECISIONS RELATED TO NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT OBJECTIVES ON THEIR PROPERTIES. EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS INCLUDE THOSE THAT ENABLE VOLUNTEERS TO CONDUCT NATURAL RESOURCE EDUCATION AND STEWARDSHIP PROGRAMS WITHIN THEIR COMMUNITIES, EXPANDING THE CAPABILITY OF THE MU EXTENSION TO REACH GREATER NUMBERS OF CLIENTELE AND IMPACT AN INCREASING NUMBER OF ACRES FOR NATURAL RESOURCE BENEFITS. SUSTAINABLE AND RESILIENT AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS NEW PROGRAMS, LIKE OUR AGRICULTURAL INNOVATION CENTER, INTEND TO BRING RESILIENCY TO THE FOOD SYSTEM WITH ENTREPRENEURSHIP. AND OUR LONG-SUCCESSFUL PROGRAMS LIKE INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT, GRAZING SCHOOLS, SHOW-ME-SELECT REPLACEMENT HEIFERS, AGRICULTURAL LENDERS SCHOOL, AND SO MANY OTHERS WILL CONTINUE TO PROVIDE BEST-IN-CLASS EXTENSION EDUCATION. ALL OUR PROGRAMS SEEK SUSTAINABILITY. ALL OF OUR PROGRAMS STRIVE FOR RESILIENCY THROUGH INNOVATION. SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES (SOLUTION-BASED) THAT ADDRESS FOOD SYSTEM RESILIENCY ISSUES ARE DEVELOPING DIGITAL TECHNOLOGIES TO REDUCE THE LABOR AND NUMBER OF ENTITIES BETWEEN FOOD PRODUCERS AND CONSUMERS; AND TRAINING FOR FARMERS, FARM WORKERS, AND OTHERS IN THE FOOD SYSTEM ABOUT HOW TO USE THOSE TECHNOLOGIES. EDUCATION AND COMMUNICATION WITH UNDERSERVED POPULATIONS MISSOURI 4-H SERVES MORE THAN 50,000 YOUTH, OFFERING MORE THAN 100 PROJECTS RELATED TO DIFFERENT FIELDS TO HELP THEM DISCOVER THEIR SPARK AND PASSION IN LIFE. MISSOURI 4-H IS TAKING A TWOFOLD APPROACH TO COLLEGE AND CAREER READINESS. FIRST, COLLEGE AND CAREER READINESS PIECES ARE WOVEN INTO OUR REGULAR PROGRAMMING EVEN AT THE YOUNGEST AGES. THIS CAN BE AS SIMPLE AS DISCUSSING POSSIBLE CAREERS IN ENJOYED PROJECT AREAS AND BY WORKING ON SOFT SKILLS, SUCH AS INTERACTING WITH OTHERS AND LEARNING TO EXPRESS IDEAS. SECONDLY, THE YOUTH FUTURES PROGRAM OFFERS YOUTH WHO FACE OBSTACLES THAT MIGHT MAKE SUCCESS SEEM OUT OF REACH THE OPPORTUNITY TO SEE COLLEGE AS AN ATTAINABLE GOAL. MISSOURI 4-H YOUTH FUTURES IS AN EXTENSIVE COLLEGE ORIENTATION PROGRAM THAT PROMOTES COLLEGE AS AN ATTAINABLE GOAL FOR HIGH SCHOOL YOUTH WHO MAY NOT NECESSARILY CONSIDER COLLEGE AS AN OPTION, SUCH AS FIRST-GENERATION COLLEGE STUDENTS. THE GOAL OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO HELP YOUTH PREPARE FOR, ENROLL IN AND GRADUATE FROM COLLEGE. YOUTH WHO PARTICIPATE IN THE 4-H STATE AGRICULTURE PROGRAMS LEARN ABOUT COLLEGE AND CAREER PATHWAYS RELATING TO AGRICULTURE AND GAIN THE SKILLS AND KNOWLEDGE NEEDED TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THESE AREAS.

awards.Description: CENTER: NATIONAL CENTER FOR AUTONOMOUS TECHNOLOGIES ATE CENTER RENEWAL -IN 2018, THE MIT TECHNOLOGY REVIEW DEFINED EMERGING TECHNOLOGIES AS ?A TECHNOLOGY, OR PERHAPS EVEN A COLLECTION OF TECHNOLOGIES, THAT WILL HAVE A PROFOUND EFFECT ON OUR LIVES?. AUTONOMOUS TECHNOLOGIES (AT) FIT THAT DEFINITION. INNOVATIONS IN AT FOR AIR, LAND, AND SEA ARE HAVING A GLOBAL IMPACT ON: 1) THE ENVIRONMENT: LOWERING EMISSIONS, CARBON FOOTPRINTS, AND HELPING COMBAT CLIMATE CHANGE; 2) SOCIETY: REDUCING HUMAN RISKS IN EMERGENCY OPERATIONS, DELIVERING HEALTHCARE AND MEDICATIONS, IMPROVING RESOURCE ACCESS, AND ENHANCING SUPPLY CHAIN EFFICIENCY; AND 3) THE ECONOMY: CREATING EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES AND GROWING GNP. ACCORDING TO THE WORLD ECONOMIC FORUM AND CANN, AT WILL RESULT IN A NET EMPLOYMENT INCREASE OF 58 MILLION BY 2030, AND AUTONOMOUS VEHICLES ARE PROJECTED TO IMPACT THE US ECONOMY BY $4 TRILLION. THE NATIONAL CENTER FOR AUTONOMOUS TECHNOLOGIES (NCAT) WILL PROVIDE VALUABLE EDUCATIONAL RESOURCES FOR TECHNICAL EDUCATION IN THIS RAPIDLY EVOLVING, HIGHLY DISRUPTIVE FIELD. NCAT'S LEADERSHIP AND COLLABORATIONS WITH AIR AND LAND STANDARDIZATION ORGANIZATIONS WILL SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OF CLEAR COMPETENCY MODELS, EFFECTIVE CREDENTIALS, AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT ROADMAPS FOR ACCESSIBLE PATHWAYS IN AUTONOMOUS VEHICLE CAREERS INCLUDING DRONES, SELF-DRIVING CARS, AND MARITIME EXPLORATION. NCAT WILL BUILD CAPACITY ACROSS EXTENSIVE NETWORKS THROUGH THE FOLLOWING GOALS: 1) LEAD: EVOLVE AND FORTIFY PARTNERSHIPS AND COLLABORATIONS WITH STANDARDS ORGANIZATIONS DEFINING TECHNICIAN EDUCATION, TRAINING PROGRAMS, AND INDUSTRY-RECOGNIZED CERTIFICATIONS; 2) INSPIRE: ADVANCE STUDENT COMPETITION AND EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING FRAMEWORKS TO FACILITATE THE CONNECTIONS BETWEEN STUDENT ENGAGEMENT AND THE IMPACT ON WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT; 3) CONNECT: EXTEND THE NATIONAL COMMUNITY AND SUPPORT NETWORKS THAT FOSTER INNOVATIVE INDUSTRY ENGAGEMENT AND EDUCATIONAL COLLABORATIONS TO ADVANCE AT TECHNICIAN EDUCATION PROGRAMS, INCLUDING WORK-BASED LEARNING (WBL) INITIATIVES; AND 4) SERVE: EXPAND THE EDUCATIONAL RESOURCE HUB WITH A FOCUS ON LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY AND ACCESS TO RESOURCES FOR COMMUNITY COLLABORATORS AND PARTNERS ACROSS ALL AT IN THE AIR, LAND AND SEA DOMAINS OF INDUSTRY. THIS PROJECT IS FUNDED BY THE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGICAL EDUCATION PROGRAM THAT FOCUSES ON THE EDUCATION OF TECHNICIANS FOR THE ADVANCED-TECHNOLOGY FIELDS THAT DRIVE THE NATION'S ECONOMY. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

THE PUERTO RICO AGRICULTURAL EXTENSION SERVICE (PRAES) PROPOSED ACTIVITIES ARE BASED ON SIX CRITICAL ISSUES DEFINED IN THE 2021-2025 APPROVED POW. THESE INCLUDE: 1. FOOD SECURITY, PLANTS AND ANIMAL SYSTEMS; 2. EXTREME WEATHER, NATURAL RESOURCES AND ENVIRONMENT, AND SUSTAINABLE ENERGY; 3. FOOD SAFETY, SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY; 4. COMMUNITY, ECONOMY, AND SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT; 5. FAMILY AND WELL -BEING AND 6. POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT. CRITICAL ISSUE # 1 WILL DIRECT EFFORTS TOWARDS FOOD SECURITY INCLUDE THE USE OF HIGH TUNNELS TECHNOLOGY TO INCREASE LOCAL FOOD PRODUCTION BY FARMERS AND URBAN AREAS. INTEGRATED EFFORTS AMONG EXTENSION AGENTS AND FAMILY EDUCATORS TO IMPROVE AGRI-BUSINESS ECONOMIC AND MARKETING STRATEGIES TO INCREASE FOOD SECURITY IN FARMS, HOMES, SCHOOLS, AND COMMUNITIES WILL CONTINUE. EFFORTS IN ANIMAL PRODUCTION FOCUS ON ANIMAL WELLBEING, ESTABLISHMENT OF IMPROVED FORAGES, PRACTICES THAT PROMOTE EXTREME WEATHER RESILIENCE AND TO INCREASE TO INCREASE ADDED VALUE OF SMALL RUMINANTS. THE USE OF DRONES FOR ESTIMATION OF VEGETATION BIOMASS AND DEVELOPMENT OF WASTE MANAGEMENT PLANS ARE RECENT ADAPTED TOOLS BEING USE TO HELP FARMERS. TROUGH THE PLANT DISEASE CLINIC, EARLY DETECTION AND MANAGEMENT OF PESTS AND DISEASES CONTINUES TO BE CRITICAL FOR PLANT SYSTEMS. NEW DISEASES AND PESTS ARE MAINLY DISSEMINATED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA AND PRAES ONLINE WEB PAGES. CRITICAL ISSUE # 2 WILL BE ADDRESSED BY ENCOURAGING FARMERS TO INCREASE THE USE OF SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE PRACTICES THAT HELP IN PROTECTING OUR NATURAL RESOURCES SUCH AS COVER CROPS, NO-TILL AND ROTATIONAL GRAZING AND AID FARMERS, FAMILIES AND 4H MEMBERS TO PROMOTE THE USE OF RENEWABLE ENERGY. ADDITIONAL EFFORTS WILL IDENTIFY SUCCESSFUL ADAPTATIONS TO EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS BASED ON RISK EXPOSURE ASSESSMENTS, AGRICULTURAL BIODIVERSITY PRACTICES, BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES RELATED TO SOIL QUALITY AND, MITIGATION OF THREATS TO WATER QUALITY. IN TERMS OF CRITICAL ISSUE # 3, PRAES WILL CONTINUE OFFERING: THE FOOD SAFETY CERTIFICATION COURSE TO PEOPLE IN CHARGE OF RETAIL FOOD ESTABLISHMENTS, AND FORMAL EDUCATION TO PROFESSIONALS THAT ENFORCE FOOD COMPLIANCE REGULATIONS. RAES WILL ALSO CONTINUE OFFERING FOOD SAFETY COURSES SUCH AS THE FAMILIES BE FOOD SAFE WHICH IS ADAPTED TO MEET THE CLIENTELE'S NEEDS. ACTIVITIES RELATED TO CRITICAL ISSUE # 4 WILL FOSTER THE REGENERATION OF AGRICULTURE AND EMPLOYMENT. PROMOTING BETTER PAID EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES AND INCREASED LABOR EFFICIENCY ARE VITAL TO IMPROVE THE AVAILABILITY OF LABOR FOR FARM RECOVERY AND IMPROVING COMMUNITY CONDITIONS. ACTIVITIES ARE FOCUSED ON CAPACITY BUILDING IN AREAS OF COMMUNITY FOOD SYSTEMS, EMERGENCY PLANS, VOLUNTEER PROGRAMS, AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP. PRAES WILL CONTINUE COLLABORATING WITH THE CREATION OF ACTION PLANS FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF ECONOMIC PROJECTS THAT GENERATE INCOME AND JOBS; AND WITH EDUCATION ACTIVITIES RELATED TO COMMUNITY SELF-MANAGEMENT, VOLUNTEERS' RESILIENCY, AND DEVELOPMENT OF EMERGENCY AND SECURITY PLANS FOR FAMILIES. IMPROVING THE QUALITY OF LIFE OF OUR VULNERABLE POPULATIONS WILL BE ADDRESSED THROUGH CRITICAL ISSUE # 5. THE HEALTH PROMOTION AND DISEASE PREVENTION PROGRAM TARGETS AREAS OF CHRONIC AND INFECTIOUS DISEASES AND PREVENTION INTERVENTIONS. THE FAMILY RESOURCE MANAGEMENT WILL FOCUS ON HELPING AND EMPOWERING THE INDIVIDUAL, FAMILY, AND COMMUNITY TO FACE CURRENT AND FUTURE FINANCIAL NEEDS. EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS ON SPENDING AND SAVINGS PLANS AND ON MAKING STRATEGIC DECISIONS THAT FAVOR FAMILY WELL-BEING CONTINUE TO BE ONE OF OUR PILLARS. EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS TARGETED THROUGH THE CRITICAL ISSUE # 5 WILL CREATE SAFE LEARNING SPACES, ESTABLISH POSITIVE CONTACTS, AND PROVIDE OPPORTUNITIES FOR CHILDREN AND YOUTH TO DEVELOP SKILLS THAT POSITIVELY CONTRIBUTE TO OUR SOCIETY. WE WILL CONTINUE WORKING WITH YOUTH LEADERS TO PROMOTE EMOTIONAL SUPPORT SYSTEMS FOR YOUTH VOLUNTEER LEADERS, AND EFFICIENT COPING MECHANISMS BEFORE, DURING AND AFTER NATURAL DISASTERS AND EMERGENCIES. OUR POW WILL PROMOTE EDUCATIONAL ACTIVITIES AT COMMUNITIES, SUPPORT YOUTH LEADERS, AND EXPAND 4-H ENRICHMENT ACTIVITIES TO HELP KIDS AND YOUTH MOVE FORWARD AFTER NATURAL DISASTERS AND EMERGENCIES.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Recipient Name: SOUTH CAROLINA DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES
awards.Description:
DESCRIPTION:THIS ACTION APPROVES AN AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,659,234 TO THE SOUTH CAROLINA DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES (SCDES). THIS GRANT WILL PROVIDE THE STATE OF SOUTH CAROLINA WITH THE NECESSARY FUNDING TO ADDRESS THE EXPENSES ASSOCIATED WITH HAZARDOUS AND SOLID WASTE MANAGEMENT DUE TO THE IMPACTS OF HURRICANE HELENE, AS OUTLINED IN THE 2025 AMERICAN RELIEF ACT.
ACTIVITIES:THE ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED INCLUDE ACQUIRING DEBRIS MANAGEMENT EQUIPMENT THROUGH SUBAWARDS TO AFFECTED COUNTIES AND PERMITS OF NEW DISASTER DEBRIS MANAGEMENT SITES. REPORT THE QUANTITIES OF DEBRIS MATERIAL GATHERED. CONDUCT APPROXIMATELY 33 VISITS TO IMPACTED COUNTIES FOR EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH. ACQUIRE A DRONE ALONG WITH THE REQUISITE EQUIPMENT AND LICENSES.  ACQUIRE NEW TECHNOLOGY FOR SCDES' DISASTER COORDINATION CENTER. ACQUIRE SAMPLING MATERIALS REQUIRED FOR HAZARDOUS WASTE TESTING. PARTICIPATE IN HAZARDOUS/SOLID WASTE TRAINING SESSIONS. DEVELOP A DEBRIS MANAGEMENT INITIATIVE.
SUBRECIPIENT:SUBAWARDS TO COUNTIES FOR DEBRIS MGT ACTIVITIES; TO PURCHASE DEBRIS-RELATED EQUIPMENT (AIR CURTAIN INCINERATORS, ETC.); TO REVISE SW MGT. PLANS, ETC. OUTCOMES:THE ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE THE COLLECTION OF SUBSTANTIAL DEBRIS, THE REINSTATEMENT OF ESSENTIAL SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING SERVICES, AND THE ENHANCEMENT OF EMERGENCY RESPONSE, ALL AIMED AT SAFEGUARDING PUBLIC HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT FROM HAZARDOUS AND SOLID WASTE RESULTING FROM HURRICANE HELENE. THE INTENDED BENEFICIARIES ARE RESIDENTS OF SOUTH CAROLINA, PARTICULARLY THOSE IN COUNTIES AFFECTED BY HURRICANE HELENE.

ADDRESSING THE NEEDS OF STAKEHOLDERS IN AGRICULTURE, FOOD AND NUTRITION INDUSTRY IN OHIO AND US THROUGH EVANS ALLEN PROGRAM AT CENTRAL STATE UNIVERSITY CENTRAL STATE UNIVERSITY THROUGH ITS EVANS ALLEN RESEARCH PROGRAM EXPECTS TO FOCUS ON THE FOLLOWING THEMATIC RESEARCH AREAS IN AGRIC ULTURE, FOOD SCIENCES AND NUTRITION: AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY (AT) HAS NOT ALWAYS BEEN ECONOMICALLY VIABLE FOR SMALL OR MEDIUM FARM OPERATIONS. SMALL-SCALE AND SPECIALTY CROP GROWERS MAY NOT BENEFIT FROM THE WAVE OF TECHNOLOGICAL ADVANCEMENTS AS LARGE-SCALE PRODUCTION GROWERS. INADEQUATE EFFORT HAS OCCURRED TO UPLIFT SMALL-SCALE FARMING TO MAKE AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY A PROFITABLE AND DESIRABLE CHOICE FOR BEGINNER FARMERS. THIS ISSUE NEEDS TO BE ADDRESSED FOR THE BETTERMENT OF THE SMALL SCALE, BEGINNING, SOCIALLY DISADVANTAGED, AND SPECIALTY CROP GROWERS. SMALL-SCALE FARMERS WHO FARM LESS THAN 5 ACRES ARE CRITICAL FOR WORLD FOOD SECURITY AS THEY PRODUCE 35% OF THE WORLD'S FOOD. THEREFORE, THE DEVELOPMENT OF EFFECTIVE AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS FOR SMALL-SCALE FARMING IS IMPERATIVE FOR SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE AND WORLD FOOD SECURITY. CROSS DISCIPLINARY APPROACHES ARE NEEDED TO ADDRESS THIS CHALLENGE. APPLICATIONS OF MECHATRONICS THAT INTEGRATE EXPERTISE IN MECHANICAL ENGINEERING, ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING, COMPUTER CONTROL, MACHINE VISION AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY IN A SEAMLESS MANNER IS GAINING TRACTION IN IMPROVING FOOD PRODUCTION. RESEARCH IN MECHANICAL DESIGN OF NEW OR MODIFYING AND ADAPTING EXISTING AGRICULTURAL MACHINERY FOR PRECISION DELIVERY OF NOVEL INTEGRATED AGRICULTURAL NUTRIENT AND PEST MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES, COORDINATED MACHINE CONTROL IN RESPONSE TO SENSORY FEEDBACK, SENSOR TECHNOLOGY AND HIGH-LEVEL PROGRAMMING ARE NEEDED TO IMPROVE EFFICIENCY OF FARMING SYSTEMS. PRECISION AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY IS A CRITICAL SOLUTION TO EXPAND THE ONGOING RESEARCH ON NON-CHEMICAL, HIGH ENER GY-BASED PEST CONTROL CONDUCTED BY RESEARCHERS IN CESTA WHICH REQUIRES ADDITIONAL CAPABILITIES IN MECHATRONICS ENGINEERING TO DEVELOP DEVICES THAT ENHANCE AGRICULTURAL EFFICIENCY, REDUCE COSTS AND IMPROVE SUSTAINABILITY. CSU ALSO INTENDS TO DEVELOP APPROPRIATE SERVICES FOR COMMERCIALIZING THE TECHNOLOGIES TO LOWER FARMING COSTS AND ENHANCE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT THROUGH HIGHER PAYING JOBS IN RELATED ADVANCED TECHNOLOGY COMPANIES. ALTERNATIVE CROPS ARE NEEDED TO PROVIDE GROWERS NEW REVENUE STREAMS. LARGE-SCALE ADOPTION OF ALTERNATIVE CROPS CAN REDUCE OVERPRODUCTION OF MAJOR CROPS, THEREBY INCREASING COMMODITY PRICES. IT IS RELEVANT TO EXPLORE AND DEVELOP KNOWLEDGE TO ESTABLISH VALUABLE NICHE MARKETS FOR ALTERNATIVE AND SPECIALTY CROPS. INTERESTING ALTERNATIVE CROPS FOR OHIO INCLUDE HEMP, HIGH-AMYLOSE CORN, PERENNIAL CROPS, MEDICINAL PLANTS, AND BIOMASS CROPS. HEMP CAN BE UTILIZED AS A FOOD, FIBER, FEED, BIOENERGY, AND MEDICINE. INCREASING OHIO HEMP PRODUCTION WILL DIVERSIFY THE STATE'S AGRICULTURE INDUSTRY, GIVE GROWERS ALTERNATIVE REVENUE STREAMS, REDUCE DEPENDENCE ON TWO CROP SYSTEMS (CORN-SOYBEANS), PROVIDE ECOLOGICAL SUSTAINABLE BENEFITS AND THAT ENHANCE HUMAN HEALTH. CENTRAL STATE IS CONDUCTING AGRONOMY AND PLANT BREEDING RESEARCH IN ALTERNATE, SPECIALTY AND MEDICINAL PLANTS UNDER CONVENTIONAL AND ORGANIC PRODUCTION SYSTEM. NEW HEMP VARIETIES BRED AND ADAPTED TO OHIO WILL PROVIDE GROWERS ACROSS THE REGION SUPERIOR GENETICS TO MAXIMIZE CROP YIELDS AND REDUCE CHANCES OF CROP FAILURE. THESE TRAITS WILL ENHANCE THE SUCCESS OF HEMP CROPS WITHIN THE REGION AND MAXIMIZE FARMER RETURNS. CSU WILL ALSO PURSUE DEVELOPING TECHNIQUES TO MAKE USE OF HEMP FIBER FOR COMPOSITE MATERIALS AND MEDICINAL PRODUCTS. A PROGRAM FOR THE IMPROVEMENT OF SPECIALTY CORN THAT WORKS WITH OHIO PRODUCERS TOWARD IDENTIFYING NEW MARKET OPPORTUNITIES BY THE DEVELOPMENT AND COMMERCIALIZATION OF HYBRID VARIETIES WITH USEFUL NOVEL STARCHES AS DETERMINED BY THE NEEDS OF THE END-USERS APPEARS NECESSARY. IN THIS REGARD HIGH-AMYLOSE CORN, WHICH HAS ELEVATED LEVELS OF AMYLOSE CONTENT IN THE STARCH HAS POTENTIAL BENEFITS. RECENT DEVELOPMENTS INDICATE ITS POTENTIAL UTILIZATION IN BIOPLASTICS AND BY FOOD COMPANIES FOR A STARCH TYPE THAT RESISTS DIGESTION. NATURAL PRODUCTS FROM HORTICULTURAL PRODUCTION THAT HAVE CAPACITY TO PROVIDE ENHANCED ECONOMIC VALUE TO AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS MAY ALSO BRING ECONOMIC BENEFITS TO OHIO PRODUCERS. THE POTENTIAL FOR NATURAL PRODUCTS TO REMEDIATE SOME OF THE MOST CURRENT PUBLIC CONCERNS SUCH AS HUMAN HEALTH AND NUTRITION, PESTS, AND PATHOGENS, AS WELL AS OTHER SOCIETAL PROBLEMS MERITS EXPLORATION. CENTRAL STATE IS ENGAGED IN DEVELOPING NATURAL PRODUCTS FROM PLANTS TO ESTABLISH VALUABLE NICHE MARKETS FOR ALTERNATIVE AND SPECIALTY CROPS. SOIL SECURITY IS A CRITICAL COMPONENT OF PLANT PRODUCTIVITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY IN AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS. INFORMED MANAGEMENT OF SOIL RESOURCES IS IMPERATIVE TO EFFECTIVELY MANAGE NATURAL RESOURCES.,. AN ASSESSMENT OF SOIL INFORMATION AND HEALTH AT LOCAL, REGIONAL, AND GLOBAL SCALES WILL PROVIDE A HOLISTIC SYSTEM-BASED APPROACH TO ADDRESS THE ISSUES AT THE NEXUS OF PLANT, ANIMAL, NATURAL RESOURCES, AND ENVIRONMENTAL SYSTEMS. CENTRAL STATE RESEARCHERS WILL USE ADVANCED TECHNIQUES AND INSTRUMENTATION THAT HARNESSES THE RECENT DEVELOPMENTS IN AGRICULTURAL SCIENCES INCLUDING, PROXIMAL SENSING, SATELLITE SENSING, DRONE-BASED SENSING, SOIL MICROBIOLOGY, ISOTOPE GEOCHEMISTRY, AND PESTICIDE CHEMISTRY TO SOLVE BROAD SOCIETAL NEEDS SUCH AS CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION, CARBON MARKETS, RENEWABLE ENERGY SYSTEMS, NON- POINT SOURCE POLLUTION ABATEMENT, ENHANCED CROP PRODUCTIVITY, DIVERSIFIED CROPPING SYSTEMS AND POLLINATOR BEHAVIOR AND HEALTH. RESEARCHERS WILL ASSESS THE EFFECTIVENESS OF SEVERAL AGRICULTURAL BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES IN REDUCING NUTRIENT LOADS IN THE GREAT AND LITTLE MIAMI WATERSHEDS THAT DRAIN INTO THE OHIO RIVER AND SUBSEQUENTLY INTO THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER AND GULF OF MEXICO. CENTRAL STATE EXPECTS TO ACHIEVE THIS BY UTILIZING ADVANCE GEOSPATIAL AND AI TECHNOLOGIES, MODELING AND OTHER INNOVATIVE LAB AND FIELD TECHNIQUES COUPLED WITH ON FARM RESEARCH. WATER ISSUES IN THE US ARE RELATED TO 1) VARIABILITY IN AVAILABILITY OF THE RESOURCE FOR AGRICULTURE, DOMESTIC INDUSTRIAL AND COMMERCIAL USES, FIREFIGHTING, AND RECREATION; 2) FLOODS AND DROUGHTS; 3) WATER QUALITY IN AGRICULTURAL AND URBAN AREAS; 4) ECONOMICS OF WATER RESOURCES AND 5) WAYS FOR ENERGY GENERATION. THE MAJOR PHENOMENON OF CLIMATE CHANGE IMPACTS ALL ASPECTS OF WATER, WHICH REQUIRES PLANNING STRUCTURAL AND NON-STRUCTURAL MITIGATION MEASURES. A MAJOR WATER ISSUE IN OHIO IS RELATED TO WATER QUALITY. NITROGEN AND PHOSPHORUS IN AGRICULTURAL RUNOFF AND LEACHING CONTINUES TO BE A NON-POINT SOURCE POLLUTION AFFECTING WATER QUALITY IN OHIO WATERSHEDS BY CAUSING HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS. TO DATE, MINIMAL RESEARCH HAS BEEN DONE IN CROPPED ACRES OF SOUTHWESTERN OHIO TO QUANTIFY THE IMPACT OF NUTRIENT POLLUTION ON WATER QUALITY. CENTRAL STATE RESEARCHERS WILL ADDRESS WATER QUALITY ISSUES IN SOUTHWESTERN OHIO BY TARGETED IMPLEMENTATIONOF AGRICULTURAL BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES. IN URBAN LANDSCAPES, A WATER QUALITY ISSUE AFFECTING MUNICIPAL STAKEHOLDERS IS MANAGING THE WATER AND WASTEWATER SYSTEMS UNDER THEIR RESPECTIVE JURISDICTIONS. THE WASTEWATERS FROM THESE SYSTEMS, THOUGH TREATED AND RELEASED STILL CARRY CONSIDERABLE LOADS OF NON-POINT SOURCE POLLUTION. MAJOR WORK ELEMENTS INVOLVE DEVELOPING GUIDELINES FOR WATERSHED LEVEL DECISION MAKING ON WATER QUALITY RESTORATION AND IMPROVEMENTS BY THE MUNICIPALITIES AND COUNTIES; ENHANCE ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY; EXPANSION OF MODELING AS A DECISION-MAKING TOOL TO INCLUDE SOCIOECONOMIC FACTORS; ENHANCING THE SMART MODELS TO INCLUDE OTHER AND EMERGING "CONTAMINANTS OF CONCERN" AND AUGMENTING WASTEWATER SMART SENSORS WITH BIOSENSORS TO DETECT VIRUSES LEADING TO COUNTY-WIDE PUBLIC HEALTH CRISIS. CENTRAL STATE RESEARCHERS WILL CONDUCT RESEARCH ON THESE WORK ELEMENTS. POLLINATOR HEALTH IS CRUCIAL FOR SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE INTO THE FUTURE. HONEYBEES ARE THE MOST IMPORTANT MANAGED POLLINATORS CONTR IBUTING $15 BILLION FOR THE U.S. ECONOMY, BUT THE HONEYBEE COLONIES ARE IN A 40-50% DECLINE IN THE COUNTRY. MANAGED BEE COLONIES ARE IN A 60% ANNUAL DECLINE IN OHIO. PROMOTING MITE RESISTANT BEES IS ONE OF THE MOST EFFECTIVE WAYS TO MITIGATE THE BEE DECLINE. IT IS IMPERATIVE THAT TO DEVELOP NEW METHODS FOR INCREASING GENETIC DIVERSITY AND TO IMPROVE QUEEN BEE QUALITY. THE LIMITING FACTORS AFFECTING LOCAL HONEYBEE RESOURCES INCLUDE FEWER QUEENS AND THE NUCLEI AVAILABLE TO FULFILL THE DEMAND OF BEEKEEPERS IN THE REGION. CENTRAL STATE UNIVERSITY EXPECTS TO GROW THE BEE GENOMIC PROGRAM TO ADDRESS THE NEEDS OF THE OHIO STAKEHOLDERS INVOLVED IN POLLINATOR MANAGEMENT. HUMAN HEALTH ENCOMPASSES MANY FACTORS: BEHAVIORAL, ECONOMIC, ENVIRONMENTAL, CULTURAL, EDUCATIONAL, AND GENETIC. DIET AND NUTRITION A RE IMPORTANT TO OVERALL HEALTH. DISEASES RELATED TO OBESITY, SEDENTARY LIFESTYLES, NUTRIENT DEFICIENCIES AND FOOD INSECURITY, ARE MAJOR DIETARY HEALTH CONCERNS IN THE U.S AND IN OHIO. CENTRAL STATE EXPECT TO DEVELOP METHODS THAT ARE NEEDED TO POSITIVELY IMPACT COMMUNITY HEALTH, BY WORKING WITH INDIVIDUALS AND SYSTEMS TO DETERMINE THE BEST PRACTICES IN HEALTH PROMOTION. UNIVERSITY RESEARCHERS WILL CONDUCT STUDIES ON FACTORS RELATED TO HEART HEALTH OF AFRICAN AMERICAN POPULATION TO FIND PATHWAYS TO IMPROVE WELLNESS OF THE AFFECTED COMMUNITIES. THROUGH RESEARCH, WE ALSO EXPECT TO FOCUS ON IDENTIFYING PREVENTATIVE MECHANISMS FOR REDUCING ANGINA AND DETERMINING THE EFFECTS OF GASOTRANSMITTERS SUCH AS NITRIC OXIDE (NO) AND HYDROGEN SULFIDE (H2S) IN DIABETES AND CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE MODELS INCLUDING TISSUE CULTURE, TO GUIDE POSSIBLE INTERVENTION MECHANISMS USING NUTRITIONAL APPROACHES. STUDIES IN THESE DISEASE MODELS WILL PROVIDE VALUABLE INFORMATION FOR IMPROVING HUMAN HEALTH AND ASSOCIATED SCIENCE. FOOD SAFETY AUGMENTS HUMAN HEALTH. FRESH PRODUCE CONSUMPTION HAS INCREASED IN THE US BECAUSE OF INCREASING HEALTH CONSCIOUSNESS OF C ONSUMERS, IMPROVED YEAR-ROUNDAVAILABILITY, CREATIVE MARKETING, AND IMPROVED VARIETIES. FRESH PRODUCE CONSUMPTION INCREASE HOWEVER, HAS COINCIDED WITH SEVERAL FOODBORNE PATHOGENIC CASES OUTBREAKS AND DISEASES LINKED TO PRODUCE WHICH ARE MAJOR THREATS TO FOOD INDUSTRY. FOR THE PAST FEW YEARS, MOST FOODBORNE CONTAMINATION AND OUTBREAKS ARE ASSOCIATED WITH FRESH PRODUCE (FRUIT AND VEGETABLES). HUMAN PATHOGENIC BACTERIA, SALMONELLA, LISTERIA, AND ESCHERICHIA COLI O157:H7, STAPHYLOCOCCUS AUREUS, AND SHIGELLA, AND NOROVIRUS, HEPATITIS A VIRUS, AND PROTOZOA ARE THE LEADING CONTAMINANTS IN PRODUCE INDUSTRY. CURRENTLY, THERE IS LIMITED LITERATURE IN UNDERSTANDING THE HUMAN PATHOGEN SOURCES, SURVIVAL BEHAVIOR IN PRODUCE AND ENVIRONMENT, AND MODE OF TRANSMISSION AND CONTAMINATION DURING PRODUCTION. EFFECTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE AND CHANGES IN GLOBAL TRADE AND CONSUMPTION PATTERNS ARE EXPECTED TO INFLUENCE THE QUALITY OF FRESH PRODUCE AND IMPACTS FOOD SAFETY. CLIMATE CHANGE EFFECTS INCLUDING EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS AND NATURAL DISASTERS, SUCH AS FLOODS AND DROUGHT HAVE INCREASED THE FREQUENCY OF HEAVY RAINS, FREQUENT AND LONGER DROUGHT PERIODS, ACIDIFICATION OF WATER, CO2 ENRICHED ATMOSPHERE, AND EXTREME PRECIPITATIONS WITH ALTERED PATTERNS AND POTENTIAL RISING IN SEA LEVELS. ALL THESE EFFECTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE ARE EXPECTED TO IMPACT THE AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION METHODS AND POTENTIALLY SUPPORT THE GROWTH, SURVIVAL, ANTIBIOTIC RESISTANCE, AND EMERGENT OF NEW STRAINS OF PATHOGENIC MICROORGANISMS ASSOCIATE WITH FOOD AND WATER SOURCES. ALSO, THE INSECT PESTS, MICROBIAL VECTORS, POPULATION IS EXPECTED TO GROW DUE TO ALTERED WEATHER PATTERNS. INCREMENT IN TEMPERATURES IS EXPECTED TO INCREASE THE RISE OF SEA LEVEL THAT MAY INFLUENCE HUMAN PATHOGENS SURVIVAL AND PROLIFERATION IN AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION SYSTEMS. CENTRAL STATE UNIVERSITY INTENDS TO DEVELOP OF A FOOD SAFETY RESEARCH PROGRAM TO ADDRESS THE FOOD SECURITY ISSUES IN OHIO. WORKFORCE DIVERSITY IN AGRICULTURE, FOOD, AND NUTRITION INDUSTRY IS A CRITICAL NEED AT THE STATE AND NATIONAL LEVEL. CENTRAL STATE AS ONE OF THE 19 HBCU LAND GRANT INSTITUTIONS IN THE COUNTRY IS DEDICATED TO SERVING THE UNDERREPRESENTED STUDENTS IN AGRICULTURE, FOOD, AND NUTRITION DISCIPLINES. THE UNIVERSITY WILL ENGAGE UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS IN EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES IN THE ABOVE DISCUSSED THEMATIC RESEARCH AREAS AS UNDERGRADUATE RESEARCH ASSISTANTS IN THE LAB AND IN THE FIELD. THE STUDENTS WILL BE PROVIDED OPPORTUNITIES FOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT IN USDA DISCIPLINES TO FOSTER A DIVERSIFIED WORKFORCE.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: THROUGH THE INSTITUTE OF AGRICULTURAL AND ENVIRONMENTAL RESEARCH, THE GOAL OF AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH AT TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY IS TO CREATE AND COMMUNICATE NEW KNOWLEDGE IN THE AGRICULTURAL AND ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCES. THROUGH OUR INNOVATIVE RESEARCH, WE ADDRESS THE NEEDS OF HUMANKIND, FOCUSING ON FINDING SOLUTIONS TO CHALLENGES FACED BY SOCIALLY AND ECONOMICALLY DISADVANTAGED GROUPS, AND CONTRIBUTING TO THE PROSPERITY OF THE CITIZENS OF TENNESSEE, THE NATION, AND THE WORLD. RESEARCH IN TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY AGRICULTURE IS ORGANIZED TO ADDRESS THE CRITICAL ISSUES DESCRIBED BELOW. ENHANCING BIODIVERSITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY SAFEGUARDING AND ENHANCING THE NATURAL RESOURCE ENVIRONMENT HAS BECOME INCREASINGLY COMPLEX WITHIN ENVIRONMENTAL, SOCIAL, RESOURCE AND PERSONAL CONTEXTS. THROUGH COLLABORATION AMONG RESEARCHERS, INDUSTRY, PRODUCERS, ENVIRONMENTAL GROUPS, AND GOVERNMENT, WE ARE HELPING PRESERVE A DIVERSE AND RESILIENT ENVIRONMENT FOR FUTURE GENERATIONS. ENRICHING OUR ECONOMY HELPING FARMS AND AGRICULTURE BUSINESSES BECOME MORE PROFITABLE AND INDIVIDUALS BECOME FINANCIALLY SECURE BOOSTS THE LOCAL, STATE, AND NATIONAL ECONOMIES. OUR PROGRAMS STRIVE TO STRENGTHEN THE ECONOMIC VIABILITY OF ALL FACETS OF AGRIBUSINESS, AND IMPROVE THE FINANCIAL LITERACY OF TENNESSEANS AND BEYOND SO THEY MAY BUILD AND PROTECT WEALTH FOR THEMSELVES AND FUTURE GENERATIONS. STRENGTHENING OUR HEALTH MAKING HEALTHY CHOICES IS IMPORTANT FOR HUMANS, ANIMALS, AND EVEN OUR PLANET. AS A LEADER IN NUTRITION, ANIMAL WELFARE, AND ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION, WE ARE WORKING TO UNDERSTAND HOW FOOD, PHYSICAL ACTIVITY, AND SOCIAL CONNECTIONS AFFECT OVERALL HEALTH. FROM FOOD SAFETY TO DISEASE PREVENTION AND MAINTENANCE TO THE STATE OF OUR ENVIRONMENT, THESE EFFORTS WILL HELP AMERICANS AND THE REST OF THE WORLD LEAD HEALTHIER LIVES FOR GENERATIONS TO COME. SUPPORTING FOOD, FIBER, AND ENERGY SYSTEMS SAFE, SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS THAT ARE SOCIALLY, ECONOMICALLY, AND ENVIRONMENTALLY RESPONSIBLE ARE KEY TO ENHANCING THE LIVES OF TENNESSEANS AND SUPPORTING A GROWING GLOBAL POPULATION. WE ARE EXPLORING WAYS TO DELIVER DISCOVERIES USING A SYSTEMS APPROACH TO AGRICULTURE PRODUCTIVITY THAT WILL PROVIDE CUSTOMIZABLE SOLUTIONS FOR PRODUCERS. UTILIZING FACULTY IN THE IN THE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURAL AND ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCES AND THE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SCIENCES, OUR RESE ARCH ADDRESSES THESE CRITICAL ISSUES THROUGH FACULTY GROUPS IN THE PLANT, ANIMAL, FOOD, AND HUMAN SCIENCES. THESE FACULTY-LED GROUPS FACILITATE THE IDENTIFICATION OF RESEARCH PRIORITIES WITHIN OUR CRITICAL ISSUES THAT ARE OF IMPORTANCE TO STAKEHOLDER GROUPS. WE CONDUCT RESEARCH IN AGRICULTURAL LEADERSHIP, EDUCATION AND COMMUNICATION THAT BRIDGES THE GAP BETWEEN THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND SCIE NTISTS WORKING TO SOLVE CURRENT AND EMERGING CHALLENGES RELATED TO SUSTAINABLY FEEDING AN EVER-GROWING POPULATION UNDER VARIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL AND SOCIETAL COMPLICATIONS. THIS RESEARCH ALSO BUILDS HUMAN AND INSTITUTIONAL CAPACITYNEEDED BY A VARIETY OF PUBLIC, PRIVATE, AND NONPROFIT EMPLOYERS IN THE AGRICULTURE SECTOR. ALSO STUDIED ARE WAYS TO DEVELOP HUMAN CAPACITY AND PROVIDE LEADERSHIP WITHIN COMMUNITIES. MUCH RESEARCH IN THIS AREA ALSO IDENTIFIES THE IMPACT OF TECHNOLOGY IN AGRICULTURAL EDUCATION, TECHNOLOGIES SUCH AS LECTURE-CAPTURE, DRONES, ROBOTS, EXTENDED REALITY, AND 360-DEGREE VIDEO. IN THE AREA OF AGRIBUSINESS, WE HAVE STRONG APPLIED-RESEARCH COMPONENT. WE PREPARE STUDENTS, FARMERS, AND STAKEHOLDERS IN THE USE OF ECONOMIC ANALYSIS AND MANAGEMENT COMPETENCIES TO TAKE ON THE CHALLENGES AND OPPORTUNITIES OF A DYNAMIC AGRI-FOOD SYSTEM. THE RESEARCH AREA EMPHASIZES PROMOTING, DEVELOPING, AND IMPROVING THE UTILIZATION OF RESEARCH, OUTREACH CAPABILITIES AND OPPORTUNITIES INVOLVING AGRIBUSINESSES, NATURAL RESOURCES AND COMMUNITIES. MAIN ACTIVITIES INCLUDE INVESTIGATIONS ON THE SOLE OR COMBINATION OF STRATEGIES FOR PROFITABILITY, AND THE SUSTAINABILITY, SURVIVAL, EFFICIENCY AND EFFECTIVENESS OF AGRICULTURALOPERATIONS, PARTICULARLY SMALL FARM OPERATIONS. ANTICIPATED RESULTS INCLUDE FARMERS' ADOPTION OF NEW CROPS; INVESTMENT FOR ALTERNATE INCOME DIVERSIFICATION STRATEGIES SUCH AS AGRITOURISM; ENHANCED KNOWLEDGE ON BENEFITS AND COSTS OF SUSTAINABLE PRACTICES; IMPROVED FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT AND MARKETING STRATEGIES AND DEVELOPMENT OF ENTREPRENEURSHIP SKILLS. RESEARCH IN ANIMAL SCIENCE IS DIRECTED AT IMPROVING LIVESTOCK PRODUCTION SYSTEMS BY GENERATING NOVEL FINDINGS THAT IMPACT ANIMAL WELL-BEING AND PERFORMANCE. SUB-DISCIPLINES SUCH AS GENETICS, HEALTH, NUTRITION, AND REPRODUCTIVE PHYSIOLOGY ARE INTEGRATED IN THE POULTRY AND RUMINANT LIVESTOCK RESEARCH ENDEAVORS. RESEARCH SPANS FROM BASIC, MOLECULAR MODELS TO APPLIED, WHOLE-ANIMAL SYSTEMS. PRIMARY END-USERS OF THE RESEARCH FINDINGS ARE LIVESTOCK PRODUCERS THAT ARE OFTEN SOCIALLY-DISADVANTAGED, RESOURCE-LIMITED OWNER-OPERATORS OF SMALL-SCALE ENTERPRISES. WE HAVE RESEARCH EFFORTS DIRECTED AT ELUCIDATING, MOLECULARLY AND FUNCTIONALLY, ANIMAL IMMUNE AND METABOLIC GENES. IN PARTICULAR, THOSE GENES THAT HAVE ANTIMICROBIAL ACTIVITIES AND THERAPEUTIC POTENTIAL. WE ARE USING COMBINED APPROACHES OF BIOINFORMATICS, GENETICS AND MOLECULAR BIOLOGY TO ELUCIDATE GENES OR GENE FAMILIES THAT ARE POTENTIALLY IMPORTANT IN ANIMAL HEALTH. SOME OF OUR ANIMAL PRODUCTION RESEARCH EMPHASIZES GOAT PRODUCTION. PROJECTS HAVE FOCUSED ON EVALUATING BREEDS TO GENERATE RECOMMENDATIONS ON HOW TO BEST USE THE DIFFERENT BREEDS FOR ENHANCED COMMERCIAL MEAT GOAT PRODUCTION. PRODUCERS HAVE MODIFIED THEIR GENETIC MANAGEMENT DECISION-MAKING LEADING TO IMPROVED HERD HEALTH, PERFORMANCE, AND ECONOMIC RETURN. IN POULTRY RESEARCH, THE CURRENT DEMAND FOR POULTRY AND POULTRY PRODUCTS REQUIRES A GAIN IN GENETIC POTENTIAL COMPLEMENTED WITH NUTRITIONAL REGIMENS THAT MAXIMIZE GROWTH AND IMPROVE EFFICIENCY OF FEED UTILIZATION. EXAMINATION OF OPTIMAL FEED COMPOSITION AND ALLOCATION IS OPTIMIZING SPECIFIC AMINO ACID CONCENTRATIONS. FEEDING THESE NEW LEVELS OF AMINO ACIDS WILLREDUCE FEEDING COST IN GUINEA FOWL PRODUCTION. OBESITY AND CHRONIC DISEASE PREVENTION IS ADDRESSED IN A PROGRAM THAT FOCUSES ON DEVELOPING A SMARTPHONE APPLICATION FOR WIC FAMILIES THAT WILL PROVIDE TOOLS TO MAKE USING THEIR BENEFITS EASIER WHILE PROVIDING HEALTHY RECIPES, NUTRITION EDUCATION, AND A GOAL TRACKING TOOL. HEALTHY EATING EXTENDS BEYOND LIMITING INTAKE OF FATS AND CALORIES. OUR NUTRITION RESEARCH IS AIMED AT UNDERSTANDING THE EFFECTS AND MECHANISMS OF FOOD-DERIVED BIOACTIVE COMPOUNDS AND THEIR ROLE IN COMBATING HUMAN CHRONIC DISEASES SUCH CANCER, OBESITY, AND DIABETES. RESEARCH WILL PROVIDE DIRECT EVIDENCE THAT HUMAN CHRONIC DISEASE CAN BE PREVENTED OR TREATED BY SELECTING FOODS HAVING HIGH LEVELS OF BIOACTIVE COMPOUNDS. FOOD IS A CORNERSTONE OF HUMAN EXISTENCE. ONGOING FOOD SCIENCE RESEARCH FOCUSES ON IMPROVING THE SAFETY, QUALITY, SHELF-LIFE, NUTRIENT PROFILE AND SENSORY ATTRIBUTES OF NOVEL FOOD INGREDIENTS, DEVELOPING AND VALIDATING ADVANCED AND INNOVATIVE TECHNOLOGIES FOR FOOD PROCESSING, MANUFACTURING, PACKAGING, AND SANITATION TO EFFECTIVELY REDUCE THE PRESENCE OF FOODBORNE PATHOGENS. ALSO, WE ARE DEVELOPING AND VALIDATING NOVEL STRATEGIES FOR THE EFFECTIVE CONTROL OF PERSISTENT RESERVOIRS OF FOODBORNE PATHOGENS AND MITIGATING ANTIMICROBIAL RESISTANCE ACROSS THE FOOD CHAIN. FOR EXAMPLE, WE ARE LOOKING AT NEW WAYS TO REMOVE PATHOGENS FROM FOODS BY PRECISELY DEFINING THE LIMITS OF USING UV LIGHT AS A SUBSTITUTE FOR PASTEURIZATION TO ACHIEVE A SPECIFIED LETHALITY FOR DEADLY AGENTS IN FOODS. THIS PROCESS IS USING ADVANCED MATHEMATICAL AND COMPUTATIONAL MODELS. TECHNIQUES DEVELOPED IN THIS PROGRAM ARE ALSO BEING INVESTIGATED FOR POSSIBLE APPLICATION TO CONTROL THE SPREAD OF SARS-COV-2 AND OTHER VIRUSES. OTHER FOOD SCIENCE RESEARCH IS ADDRESSING THE PROBLEM OF CAMPYLOBACTER. THIS ORGANISM IS ONE OF THE MOST COMMON CAUSES OF DIARRHEAL ILLNESS IN THE UNITED STATES. DUE TO THE PREVALENCE OF CAMPYLOBACTER IN RAW CHICKEN PRODUCTS, ROUTINE AND RELIABLE MONITORING IS NECESSARY TO REDUCE ITS IMPACT UPON HUMAN HEALTH. THE METHOD BEING DEVELOPED IS INTENDED TO BE USED BY FOOD INDUSTRY AND REGULATORY TESTING LABORATORIES TO FACILITATE IMPROVED IDENTIFICATION OF CAMPYLOBACTER IN PROCESSING FACILITIES AND FINAL PRODUCTS. TO MITIGATE THE SPREAD OF ANTIMICROBIAL RESISTANT CONTAMINANTS IN FOODS, WE HAVE RESEARCH THAT HAS GENERATED A SERIES OF HOTSPOT MAPS THAT WILL BE USED IN WORKSHOPS/WEBINARS TO ILLUSTRATE ANTIMICROBIAL RESISTANT BACTERIAL COMMUNITIES ON PRODUCER'S FARMS. BY USING PERSONALIZED HOTSPOT MAPS THAT DISPLAY THE FREQUENCY OF ANTIBIOTIC RESISTANT BACTERIA IN FARMS, PRODUCERS WILL MAKE INFORMED DECISIONS ON THE USAGE OF ANTIBIOTICS IN THEIR LIVESTOCK, THUS REDUCING THE THREAT OF CONTAMINATION OF FOOD CROPS FERTILIZED WITH THE ANTIMICROBIAL-RESISTANT MANURE. RESEARCH IN NATURAL RESOURCES USES MULTIDISCIPLINE APPROACHES TO UNDERSTAND AND ATTENUATE PROBLEMS CAUSED BY CLIMATE CHANGE, LAND DE GRADATION, LOSS OF BIODIVERSITY, EMERGING CONTAMINANTS, AND ANTHROPOGENIC STRESSORS. TRADITIONAL AND CUTTING-EDGE CHEMISTRY METHODS ARE USED TO STUDY FATE OF CHEMICALS IN THE ENVIRONMENT, THE INTERACTION OF HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES WITH SOILS AND WATER AND BIOGEOCHEMICAL CYCLES. BIOTECHNOLOGY IS USED TO STUDY AND RECTIFY HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS, WILDLIFE DISEASES AND PARASITES, MONITOR RARE AND ENDANGERED SPECIES, AND SOIL HEALTH. GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION SYSTEMS, REMOTE SENSING AND ADVANCED STATISTICS ARE USED TO MODEL CLIMATE CHANGE ON A LARGE SCALE, WILDLIFE ECOSYSTEMS, DETERMINE THE VALUE OF ECOSYSTEM SERVICES AND DOCUMENT WATER RESOURCES. OUR RESEARCH TAKES PLACE IN CHEMISTRY LABS, COMPUTER LABS, RURAL AND URBAN SETTINGS AS WE SEEK TO SUSTAIN AND IMPROVE THE NATION'S NATURAL RESOURCES. IN ADDITION TO RESEARCH, WE BUILD INSTITUTIONAL CAPACITY AND STRENGTHEN OUR ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAM THROUGH BRIDGE PROGRAMS, STUDENT RECRUITMENT AND MENTORING, STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIPS, AGGRESSIVE PROPOSAL WRITING AND CURRICULUM DEVELOPMENT. THE GOAL IS TO DEVELOP A STRONG DIVERSE WORKFORCE IN THE NATURAL RESOURCES THAT REFLECTS OUR NATION'S DEMOGRAPHICS. PLANT SCIENCE RESEARCH INCLUDES DIVERSE FIELDS IN AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION SYSTEMS WITH A FOCUS ON DEVELOPING AND APPLYING SCIENTIFIC AND BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRINCIPLES TO IMPROVE CROP PRODUCTIVITY, REDUCE LOSSES FROM DISEASE, INSECT PESTS, AND TOXIC METAL STRESS; IN ADDITION TO IMPROVING THE UNDERSTANDING OF PLANT STRESS TOLERANCE MECHANISMS FOR HANDLING HEAT, DROUGHT AND OTHER STRESS FACTORS. RESEARCH INCLUDES THE DEVELOPMENT OF NEW TECHNOLOGIES FOR IMPROVING THE PRODUCTION OF RENEWABLE ENERGY SOURCES AND DEVELOPMENT OF ALTERNATIVE PRODUCTS FOR REDUCING PESTICIDE INPUTS IN CROP PRODUCTION SYSTEMS SUCH AS MICROBIAL-BASED PRODUCTS, PHEROMONES, AND PLANT OILS, PLANT GENETIC RESISTANCE AND BY BROADENING SOYBEAN GENETIC DIVERSITY FOR FUTURE GAINS IN CROP PRODUCTIVITY AND PREPAREDNESS FOR EMERGING NEW DISEASES AND INSECT PESTS. CROPS ADDRESSED ARE DIVERSE, INCLUDING VEGETABLES, CORN, SOYBEANS, ORNAMENTAL NURSERY CROPS, BIOENERGY CROPS, HYDROPONICS, AND URBAN AGRICULTURE. TECHNOLOGICAL CONTRIBUTIONS FROM THIS RESEARCH INCLUDE INCREASING CROP DIVERSITY BY INTRODUCING NEW EXOTIC VEGETABLES AND AMARANTH, DEVELOPING NEW CROP ALTERNATIVES LIKE HEMP, AND BIOFUEL ALTERNATIVES SUCH AS PERENNIAL SWITCHGRASS AND WINTER CANOLA THAT HAVE IMPROVED DIGESTIBILITY AND PROCESSING, AND DEVELOPING NEW PLANTS TOLERANT TO HEAT STRESS. OTHER RESEARCH ACTIVITIES ON BEST CROP MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, NITROGEN APPLICATION RATES TO ENHANCE YIELD AND FEEDSTOCK CHARACTERISTICS, THE BEST WINTER CANOLA VARIETIES AND NITROGEN RATES FOR TENNESSEE DELIVER SERVICES OF VALUE TO SOCIETY. AN OVERALL LACK OF CROP DIVERSITY IN THE AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS OF THE SOUTHEASTERN UNITED STATES INCREASES THE THREATS OF DISEASE EPIDEMICS, INSECT PEST OUTBREAKS OR RISKS OF CROP FAILURE DUE TO ABIOTIC STRESSES. IT IS IMPORTANT TO DEVELOP ALTERNATIVE CROPS FOR PRODUCTIVE SYSTEMS. RESEARCH TO ADDRESS THIS ISSUE HAS PRODUCED THE RELEASE OF TWO GRAIN AMARANTH VARIETIES AND THE RELEASE OF TWO GRAIN LEGUME VARIETIES. THESE ALTERNATIVE CROPS GIVE PRODUCERS OPTIONS IN SELECTING PRODUCTION MARKETS. ANOTHER PROGRAM LEADING TO CROP DIVERSITY IS EXAMINING THE ROLE OF SWEET SORGHUM AS A POTENTIAL DEDICATED US BIOFUEL CROP. THIS PROGRAM IS ESTABLISHING INNOVATIVE PROTOCOLS FOR SWEET SORGHUM GENETIC ENHANCEMENT. AS MORE KNOWLEDGE IS GAINED ABOUT THE INTERCONNECTEDNESS OF HUMAN IMPACTS ON THE EARTH, THE ROLE OF ANTIBIOTIC CONTAMINATION ON SOIL HEALTH IS BEING EXAMINED. MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY IS A MAJOR CONCERN IN THE PRODUCTION OF SOME CROPS, SOME OF OUR RESEARCH WILL SIGNIFICANTLY IMPROVE THE UNDERSTANDING THE BIOGEOCHEMICAL CYCLING OF MICRONUTRIENTS IN THE PRESENCE OF ANTIBIOTICS. THREATS FROM DISEASE AND INSECT PESTS ARE BEING ADDRESSED IN A NUMBER OF RESEARCH PROGRAMS. PLANT DISEASE IS A MAJOR THREAT TO GLOBAL FOOD PRODUCTION COSTING THE US ECONOMY AN ESTIMATED $33 BILLION ANNUALLY IN YIELD LOSS. WE HAVE RESEARCH THAT IS FOCUSED ON UNEARTHING THE MOLECULAR SECRETS OF PLANT-BACTERIAL INTERACTIONS THAT RESULT IN TWO IMPORTANT DISEASES OF VEGETABLES - BACTERIAL SOFT ROT OF ALL VEGETABLES AND BACTERIAL WILT OF CUCURBITS CROPS. RESEARCH HAS IDENTIFIED ACCESSIONS RESISTANT TO WILT DISEASE AND WHICH CAN BE EMPLOYED IN A BREEDING PROGRAM FOR RESISTANCE CULTIVARS. OTHER DISEASE RESEARCH IS EXAMINING THE USE OF BIOCONTROL AGENTS FOR DISEASE MANAGEMENT. THESE ALTERNATIVES TO CHEMICAL FUNGICIDES WILL PROVIDE TOOLS FOR DISEASE MANAGEMENT IN VEGETABLE PRODUCTION ESPECIALLY IN ORGANIC FARMING AND REDUCE USAGE OF TOXIC CHEMICALS IN CONVENTIONAL FARMING. INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM) OF INSECT PESTS IS A HOLISTIC APPROACH TO SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE THAT FOCUSES ON MANAGING PESTS THROUGH A COMBINATION OF CULTURAL, PHYSICAL, BIOLOGICAL AND CHEMICAL METHODS. IPM IS A CROP PROTECTION PRACTICE THAT IS COST EFFECTIVE, ENVIRONMENTALLY SOUND AND SOCIALLY ACCEPTABLE. TO FULFILL THIS NEED, WE CONDUCT RESEARCH FOCUSED ON CONDUCTING APPLIED RESEARCH EXPERIMENTS AND EDUCATING STAKEHOLDERS ON IPM THROUGH TRAINING AND PUBLICATIONS. IN-SERVICE TRAINING PROGRAMS FOR EXTENSION AGENTS AND IPM WORKSHOPS FOR OTHER STAKEHOLDERS ARE CONDUCTED ON STATE-WIDE BASIS IN EAST, MIDDLE AND WEST TENNESSEE REGIONS. IN OTHER INSECT RESEARCH, JAPANESE MAPLE SCALE RESEARCH, TIMED APPLICATIONS OF GROWTH REGULATORS AND ANNUAL MANAGEMENT PLANS DEVELOPED AT THE TSU NURSERY RESEARCH CENTER HAVE BEEN ADOPTED BY TENNESSEE GROWERS, REDUCING THE NUMBER OF REJECTED SHIPMENTS AND CULLED TREES. INVASIVE INSECTS ARE A MAJOR CONCERN IN NURSERY PRODUCTION. OUR RESEARCH IS PRODUCING DATA FOR NEW PESTICIDE REGULATIONS FOR FIRE ANT, JAPANESE BEETLE AND OTHER IMPORTANT INSECTS THAT ARE REDUCING WORKER EXPOSURE RISK, SAVING PRODUCERS TIME, AND OVERALL REDUCING THE COST OF PRODUCTION. MORE PRODUCER SAVINGS ARE EXPECTED WITH NEW PROTOCOLS UNDER DEVELOPMENT. SUSTAINABILITY RESEARCH WILL LEAD TO NEW PRACTICES THAT RESULTS IN REDUCTION OF INPUTS (PESTICIDES, FERTILIZER, WATER, LABOR, ETC.) IN THE NURSERY PRODUCTION SYSTEM, THUS ENHANCING THE SUSTAINABILITY OF THE INDUSTRY, IMPROVING OUR ENVIRONMENT, REDUCING PRODUCTION COSTS FOR GROWERS, IMPROVING CROP QUALITY, AND BROADENING THE MARKET FOR TENNESSEE GREEN INDUSTRY PRODUCTS. TO ADDRESS THE WELL-BEING AND HEALTH OF INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES AND COMMUNITIES, RESEARCH IN FAMILY AND CONSUMER SCIENCES IS FOCUSED ON IMPROVING THE LIVES OF OUR STAKEHOLDERS THROUGH FCS EDUCATION, LEADERSHIP AND COMMUNICATION TO IMPROVE NOT ONLY THEIR HEALTH AND W ELL-BEING, BUT ALSO THEIR RELATIONSHIPS AND RESOURCES. THIS GROUP IS CONTINUING TO FOCUS ON EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS, LIVING WITH CHRONIC DISEASES, FOOD SECURITY VIA FOOD RESOURCE MANAGEMENT, AND OF COURSE, MITIGATING THE EFFECTS OF COVID-19 AS WE LIVE THROUGH THE P ANDEMIC. TSU AGRICULTURE IS PROUD OF THE RESEARCH CONDUCTED BY OUR FACULTY AND STAFF AS WE CONTINUE TO ADDRESS ISSUES THAT ARE CRITICAL TO THE WELFARE OF OUR STAKEHOLDERS, AND CONTRIBUTING TO THE PROSPERITY OF THE CITIZENS OF TENNESSEE, THE NATION, AND THE WORLD. WE LOOK FORWARD TO CONTINUING TO FULFILL OUR UNIQUE ROLE AS AN HBCU LAND-GRANT INSTITUTION IN TENNESSEE AND TO CONTINUED GROWTH AND SUCCESS.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO PROVIDE IMPLEMENTATION, MONITORING, AND STRATEGY INTEGRATION CAPACITY TO SUPPORT THE SALMON AND CHALLIS FIELD OFFICES, THE IDAHO BLM STATE OFFICE, AND OTHER REGIONAL AND STATE PARTNERS TO IMPROVE CLIMATE RESILIENCE AND HABITAT QUALITY BY CONSERVING WATER AT NATURAL WATER SOURCES THROUGH WATER DEVELOPMENT IMPROVEMENTS, AND BY PROTECTING  WATER SOURCES, STREAMS, AND WETLAND AND RIPARIAN AREAS  VIA VIRTUAL FENCING AND TRADITIONAL RIPARIAN EXCLOSURE FENCING. ADDITIONALLY, OUR WORK WILL ACCELERATE RIPARIAN RESTORATION AND ASSOCIATED UPLAND RESTORATION VIA SEED COLLECTION, BOLSTERING POLLINATOR RESOURCES, AND THROUGH GRAZING MANAGEMENT PROJECTS IN HIGH PRIORITY IDAHO SAGEBRUSH LANDSCAPES. THE PROPOSED WORK WILL HELP SUPPORT AND MAINTAIN FUNCTIONING WILDLIFE HABITATS AND WILL HELP TO PROVIDE FOR THE LONG-TERM CONSERVATION OF WILDLIFE AND BIODIVERSITY ON PUBLIC LANDS. WE WILL DELIVER AND SUPPORT BLM AND PARTNERS ON ADDITIONAL RIPARIAN RESTORATION PROJECTS AND INTEGRATE THEM WITH ESSENTIAL SUPPORT SERVICES FOR SCIENCE-BASED PRIORITIZATION, IMPLEMENTATION, MONITORING, AND ADAPTIVE MANAGEMENT. THE ADDITIONAL IMPLEMENTATION OF GRAZING AND MANAGEMENT PRACTICES TOOLS THAT INCLUDE VIRTUAL FENCING AND STOCK WATER DISTRIBUTION SYSTEMS WILL ALIGN WITH BLM PRIORITIES AND PLANS TO IMPROVE WILDLIFE HABITAT QUALITY AND CONNECTIVITY AND IMPROVE RANGE CONDITIONS. WE WILL LOOK FOR OPPORTUNITIES TO CONNECT WORK IN THE UPPER SALMON WITH OTHER PRIORITY LANDSCAPES IN IDAHO. THE SCOPE OF WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING IS SCALABLE, AND WE WILL LOOK TO BLM TO HELP DETERMINE THE DESIRED SCOPE AND DELIVERABLES. THE PROPOSED EFFORTS WILL: 1) BENEFIT THE BLMS MISSION TO CONSERVE THE NATURAL RESOURCES OF THE REGION, 2) DIRECTLY BENEFIT THE PEOPLE AND COMMUNITIES OF LEMHI AND CUSTER COUNTIES AND OTHER PROJECT LOCATIONS THROUGH WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND CONTRACTING OPPORTUNITIES, 3) IMPROVE IMPORTANT HABITAT CONDITIONS FOR WILDLIFE INCLUDING SAGE-GROUSE AND AQUATIC SPECIES, AND 4) IMPROVE RIPARIAN-UPLAND INTEGRATION, THAT BETTER BALANCES GRAZING USE WITH WILDLIFE NEEDS IN SENSITIVE HABITAT LOCATIONS LIKE RIPARIAN RESOURCES. ALL TOLD, THIS APPROACH LEVERAGES SCIENCE, MONITORING, AND COMPLEMENTARY MANAGEMENT TOOLS TO ACHIEVE LASTING BENEFITS FOR WATERSHED FUNCTION WHILE BOOSTING SOCIAL SUPPORT FOR LTPBR AND DEVELOPING EVIDENCE-BASED INFORMATION ABOUT CONSERVATION OUTCOMES AND CLIMATE RESILIENCE. OUR OVERALL GOAL IS TO HELP THE BLM MEET ECOSYSTEM RESTORATION AND RESILIENCE TARGETS AND ADVANCE THE OBJECTIVES OF THE RESTORATION LANDSCAPE FUNDING. THROUGH A SCIENCE-BASED PRIORITIZATION APPROACH, WE WILL CONTRIBUTE TO COLLABORATIVE EFFORTS TO IMPLEMENT THE DEFEND THE CORE, GROW THE CORE FRAMEWORK, AND CREATE GREATER WATERSHED RESILIENCE TO DROUGHT, WILDFIRE, AND CLIMATE CHANGE TO ENSURE WILDLIFE POPULATIONS HAVE ABUNDANT AND CONNECTED HABITAT AND COMMUNITIES THRIVE.THIS PROPOSAL INCLUDES 1.4 FTE TO SUPPORT PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INCLUDING PLANNING, IMPLEMENTATION, STRATEGIC COORDINATION, AND CONTRACT MANAGEMENT. SUBAWARDS TO BOISE STATE UNIVERSITY (BSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF IDAHO (UI) WILL SUPPORT COLLABORATIVE WORK WITH TNC AND BLM TO DEVELOP SCIENCE-BASED AND EXPORTABLE FRAMEWORKS FOR SUPPORTING WATERSHED AND BASIN SCALE RESTORATION PRIORITIES AND FOR INTEGRATING MONITORING AT MULTIPLE SCALES (SITE-, DRONE-, AND SATELLITE-BASED) TO PROVIDE LONG-TERM QUANTITATIVE DATA FOR STREAM AND RIPARIAN RESTORATION OUTCOMES AND MESIC PERSISTENCE AND RESILIENCE.
USE DATA COLLECTED BY DRONES, EQIP INCENTIVES, AND CAREFUL PROGRAM EVALUATION TO ENHANCE ECONOMIC, ENVIRONMENTAL, AND SOCIAL CONSERVATION PLANNING OUTCOMES FOR HU PRODUCERS.

PROJECT TITLE: IL 2 RECONSTRUCTION FROM IL 72 IN BYRON TO BELTLINE ROAD SOUTH OF ROCKFORD. :::: PROJECT DESCRIPTION: PRELIMINARY ENGINEERING SERVICES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:  FIELD VISITS TO ACCESS THE SECTION OF IL 2 BETWEEN OREGON & BYRON TO IDENTIFY CONCERNS, EVALUATE EXISTING SURFACE MODEL, DRONE SURVEY TO PROVIDE A DIGITAL AERIAL  IMAGE, REVIEW ENVIRONMENTAL SURVEY REQUEST FORMS, REVIEW PREVIOUS TRAFFIC DATA & COMPLETED TRAFFIC STUDIES, ROADWAY AND ALTERNATE DESIGN STUDIES FOR ROADWAY & PEDESTRIAN INFRASTRUCTURE ELEMENTS, DRAINAGE DESIGN CRITERIA NECESSARY FOR DRAINAGE EFFORTS, PATH DESIGN FOR PATH BETWEEN IL 72 & LUTHER DRIVE, UTILITY COORDINATION, ENVIRONMENTAL CONCERNS WITH CULTURAL RESOURCES FOR GUARD HOUSES, PREPARATION OF PROJECT REPORT, REVIEW EXISTING STRUCTURE REPORTS & ROUTINE INSPECTION REPORTS FOR BRIDGES & CULVERTS, SUBSURFACE UTILITY ENGINEERING SURVEY, BORING & TEST DATA FOR ALL STRUCTURE LOCATIONS, TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT ANALYSIS, VALUE ENGINEERING, PREPARE 3D MODELS FOR CULVERT & STORM SEWER WORK, PREPARE PROJECT STATUS REPORTS AND PLAN EXECUTION.

THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2023. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, NUTRITION, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES, AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY AND THE UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDE CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, PLANT PRODUCTION, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES, SUCH AS BIOFUELS AND DRONE TECHNOLOGY. USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE MODEL INCLUDES A METHOD FOR ASSESSING NEEDS AND IDENTIFYING PROBLEMS. IT ALSO INCLUDES PROGRAM EVALUATION TO DETERMINE IMPACT OF EXTENSION EDUCATIONAL TRAININGS. THE MODEL WORKS IN UNISON WITH MULTIPLE ADVISORY SYSTEMS AT BOTH COUNTY AND STATE LEVELS. GOALS OF THESE PROGRAMS ARE TO DEMONSTRATE SHORT AND LONG-TERM IMPACTS. STATE FUNDING SUCH AS STATE MATCH, COUNTY FUNDING, GRANTS AND GIFTS LEVERAGED AS A DIRECT RESULT OF THE WORK IN THIS PLAN AIDS IN CREATING THE GREATEST FINAL IMPACTS. THE RESEARCH PORTION OF THE PLAN OF WORK UNDERGOES SCIENTIFIC PEER REVIEW PRIOR TO EACH PROJECT BEING SUBMITTED. ALL SCIENTISTS ARE REQUIRED TO HAVE ACTIVE PROJECTS FOR EXPENDITURES TO BE MADE. EACH PROJECT IS PEER-REVIEWED BY BOTH INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL REVIEWERS AND MUST BE APPROVED BY THE APPROPRIATE DEAN AND DIRECTOR PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURE. MOST APPLIED RESEARCH PROGRAMS ARE DEVELOPED BASED ON FEEDBACK FROM THE STAKEHOLDERS THROUGH THE EXTENSION CONTACTS AND BASIC RESEARCH ARE CONDUCTED TO FIND SPECIFIC SCIENTIFIC ANSWERS OR TO MAKE DISCOVERY. CURRENT ANIMAL RELATED PROJECTS INVOLVE EFFICIENT PRODUCTION SYSTEMS INCLUDING ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND NUTRITION, ANIMAL HEALTH AND WELLBEING WITH INTERNAL PARASITE CONTROL, AND ECONOMIC PROSPERITY A ND MARKETING USING SHEEP, GOATS, AND CATTLE AS MODELS. CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURE WAS INTRODUCED THIS YEAR ALONG WITH OTHER 1890 INSTITUTIONS UNDER THE 1890 MULTISTATE CLIMATE CHANGE PROJECT WITHIN THE EVANS-ALLEN PROGRAM. THE FOOD SAFETY PROGRAM EMPHASIZES NON-THERMAL METHODS TO CONTROL FOOD BORN PATHOGENS IN MEAT AND NUTS. PLANT PRODUCTION PROGRAM ENCOMPASS A BROAD ARRAY OF TARGETED FIELDS OF STUDY THAT INCLUDE ENTOMOLOGY, PLANT PATHOLOGY, NEMATOLOGY, PLANT BREEDING AND GENETICS, SOIL SCIENCE, POSTHARVEST PHYSIOLOGY. SOME OF THESE PROJECTS ARE DONE IN COLLABORATION WITH ARS SCIENTISTS ON COMMODITY OF GREAT IMPORTANCE TO THE STATE OF GEORGIA. FUNDING FROM THE FEDERAL ALLOCATION AND THE CORRESPONDING MATCHING FUND FROM THE STATE ARE USED TO COVER EXPENSES RELATED TO THESE ACTIVITIES. ADDITIONAL FUNDS COME FROM EXTERNAL GRANTS SECURED BY THE RESEARCH SCIENTISTS. APPLIED RESEARCH PROJECTS USING THESE FUNDS TARGET MAINLY LIMITED-RESOURCE PRODUCERS IN THE STATE BUT SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES IMPACT THE CITIZEN OF STATE OF GEORGIA, THE COUNTRY, AND THE WORLD.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture
