awards.Description: KENTUCKY STATE UNIVERSITY'S 1890 COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM SERVES YOUTH, FARMERS, FAMILIES, AND COMMUNITIES THROUGHOUT THE STATE. TO ENSURE THAT OUR WORK IS IMPACTFUL, RESEARCHBASED, AND DEMAND-DRIVEN, KENTUCKY STATE UNIVERSITY CONDUCTS REGULAR NEEDS ASSESSMENTS OF OUR FELLOW KENTUCKIANS, COLLABORATES WITH OUR 1890 LAND-GRANT RESEARCHERS, AND STRATEGICALLY DELIVERS EDUCATIONAL AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS. OUR EXTENSION PROGRAMMING COVERS AGRICULTURE EDUCATION THROUGH PRODUCTION DEMONSTRATIONS, MONTHLY THIRD THURSDAY THING WORKSHOPS, AND ONE-ON-ONE TECHNICAL SUPPORT TO FARMERS FROM OUR EDUCATORS IN TOPICS SUCH AS SOIL MANAGEMENT, POND MANAGEMENT, CLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE, ORGANIC AGRICULTURE, HORTICULTURE, TREE FRUIT CULTURE, APICULTURE, AQUACULTURE, AQUAPONICS, HYDROPONICS, SMALL RUMINANTS, BEEF CATTLE, ETC. OUR EXTENSION DRONE TEAM, IN COLLABORATION WITH OUR REMOTE SENSING RESEARCHERS, IS A POWERFUL RESOURCE FOR FARMERS AND NEXT-GENERATION ORGANIZATIONS THAT SEEK TO TRAIN YOUTH AND UTILIZE MODERN TECHNOLOGY-BASED AGRICULTURE TO MANAGE AGRONOMIC CROPS, CROP PEST AND DISEASE, AND LIVESTOCK HERDS. OUR EXTENSION EDUCATORS PROVIDE KNOWLEDGE AND SUPPORT IN TOPICS RELATED TO HUMAN NUTRITION AND HUMAN HEALTH TO COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE STATE THROUGH VARIOUS AVENUES INCLUDING OUR MOBILE HEALTH UNITS. WORKING IN COLLABORATION WITH HUMAN HEALTH AND NUTRITION RESEARCHERS, THESE EDUCATORS ARE TRAINING STAKEHOLDERS OF ALL AGES HOW TO INCORPORATE NUTRITIOUS AND TASTY FOODS INTO HEALTHY, BUDGET-FRIENDLY DIETS, WHILE ALSO MITIGATING CHRONIC ILLNESSES SUCH AS DIABETES AND HEART DISEASE THROUGH INCREASED PHYSICAL ACTIVITY. OUR 4-H AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT STAFF ARE TRAINING YOUTH IN STEM, AGRICULTURE, NATURAL RESOURCES, THE ENVIRONMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FUTURE CAREERS, AND MENTAL HEALTHCARE. THESE EDUCATORS ARE REACHING SCHOOLS, RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATIONS, 4-H CLUBS, AND OTHER PLACES WHERE THEY CAN ENGAGE YOUTH AND PROMOTE POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT APPROACHES. THEY ARE ALSO FORMING STRONG PARTNERSHIPS WITH PARENTS, GUARDIANS, SCHOOLTEACHERS, COACHES, RELIGIOUS LEADERS, ETC., TO ENGAGE YOUTH THROUGH IN-SCHOOL ENRICHMENT, AFTER-SCHOOL PROGRAMS, AND A MULTIPLICITY OF SUMMER CAMPS. OUR COMMUNITY RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT STAFF COLLABORATE WITH LOCAL LEADERS AND ORGANIZATIONS TO STRENGTHEN COMMUNITIES THROUGH LEADERSHIP TRAINING, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, WORKFORCE READINESS, LAND RETENTION, AND DISASTER EDUCATION INITIATIVES THAT PROMOTE SUSTAINABLE GROWTH AND RESILIENCE. THEY ALSO BUILD PARTNERSHIPS THAT ENHANCE CIVIC ENGAGEMENT, INCREASE ACCESS TO RESOURCES, AND SUPPORT EQUITABLE ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITIES ACROSS KENTUCKY COMMUNITIES. ALL OF OUR EXTENSION PROGRAMS ARE FUNDAMENTALLY DRIVEN BY THE NEEDS OF OUR LAND-GRANT CLIENTS AND ARE DESIGNED IN RES PONSE TO THE FOLLOWING CRITICAL ISSUES IDENTIFIED IN OUR USDA PLAN OF WORK: 1) AGRICULTURE, ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES; 2) ECONOMIC AND FINANCIAL WELL-BEING; 3) FOOD SAFETY AND SECURITY; 4) LEADERSHIP AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT; 5) LIFE SKILL DEVELOPMENT; 6) NUTRITION AND HEALTHY LIFESTYLES; 7) SMALL FARM DEVELOPMENT; AND, 8) SUBSTANCE USE RECOVERY AND MENTAL WELLBEING.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: KENTUCKY STATE UNIVERSITY'S 1890 LAND-GRANT EXTENSION PROGRAM IS TO SERVE THE NEEDS OF LIMITED RESOURCE AND SMALL FARMERS, AND LOW-INCOME AND SOCIALLY DISADVANTAGED RESIDENTS OF THE STATE. THIS SERVICE ENTAILS NEEDS ASSESSMENTS OF OUR FELLOW KENTUCKIANS, COORDINATION WITH 1890 LAND- GRANT RESEARCHERS, AND MEANINGFUL AND TIMELY DELIVERY OF EDUCATIONAL AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS. EXTENSION PROGRAMS COVER AGRICULTURE EDUCATION THROUGH PRODUCTION DEMONSTRATIONS, MONTHLY WORKSHOPS, AND ONE-ON-ONE SUPPORT TO FARMERS FROM OUR EDUCATORS IN TOPICS INCLUDING SOIL MANAGEMENT, POND MANAGEMENT, CLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE, ORGANIC AGRICULTURE, HORTICULTURE, TREE FRUIT CULTURE, APICULTURE, AQUACULTURE, AQUAPONICS, HYDROPONICS, SMALL RUMINANTS, AND BEEF CATTLE. OUR EXTENSION DRONE TEAM, IN COLLABORATION WITH OUR REMOTE SENSING RESEARCHERS, IS A POWERFUL RESOURCE TO ASSIST FARMERS TO OVERSEE AGRONOMIC CROPS, SPRAY CROPS, MONITOR LIVESTOCK HERDS, AND TRAIN YOUTH ABOUT MODERN TECHNOLOGY-BASED AGRICULTURE. EXTENSION EDUCATORS PROVIDE KNOWLEDGE AND SUPPORT IN TOPICS RELATED TO HUMAN NUTRITION AND HEALTH; THAT INCLUDE RECENTLY ACQUIRED MOBILE HEALTH UNITS TO EDUCATE THE UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE STATE. THESE EDUCATORS ARE TRAINING LOW-INCOME STAKEHOLDERS ABOUT NUTRITIOUS AND TASTY FOODS THAT ARE BUDGET FRIENDLY, WHILE MITIGATING CHRONIC ILLNESSES SUCH AS DIABETES AND HEART DISEASE. OUR 4-H AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT STAFF ARE ENGAGING YOUTH FROM SOCIALLY DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES AND TRAINING THEM IN STEM, AGRICULTURE, NATURAL RESOURCES, THE ENVIRONMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FUTURE CAREERS, AND MENTAL HEALTHCARE. THESE EDUCATORS ARE REACHING SCHOOLS, RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATION, 4-H CLUBS, AND OTHER PLACES WHERE THEY CAN ACCESS YOUTH TO THE KNOWLEDGE OF ALL THAT 1890 LAND-GRANT PROGRAMS CAN OFFER THEM. THEY ARE FORMING STRONG PARTNERSHIPS WITH PARENTS, GUARDIANS, SCHOOLTEACHERS, COACHES, AND RELIGIOUS LEADERS, TO ENGAGE YOUTH DURING IN SCHOOL AND AFTER-SCHOOL PROGRAMS AND A MULTIPLICITY OF SUMMER CAMPS. OUR EXTENSION PROGRAMS ARE FUNDAMENTALLY DRIVEN BY THE NEEDS OF OUR LAND-GRANT CLIENTS THAT ARE EXEMPLIFIED BELOW IN THROUGH THE CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND IN OUR USDA PLAN OF WORK WHICH INCLUDES: 1) LIFE SKILLS DEVELOPMENT. PROGRAMING WILL EXPAND EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH IN STEM/STEAM AREAS TO INCREASE ENROLLMENT OF LIMITED-RESOURCE FAMILIES AND COMMUNITIES WHO ARE AT OR BELOW THE POVERTY LEVEL AND WHO QUALIFY FOR SUPPORT SERVICES. 2) NUTRITION AND HEALTHY LIFESTYLES. PROGRAMS WILL FOCUS ON ENSURING SAFE, NUTRITIOUS, AND HIGH QUALITY SUPPLY OF FOOD FOR ALL. IN ADDITION, THERE IS A COMMON GOAL FOR LAND GRANT INSTITUTIONS TO PROMOTE HEALTH AND SAFETY WITHIN KENTUCKY HOMES AND COMMUNITY ENVIRONMENTS. MOREOVER, PROGRAMS TO COMBAT SOCIAL ILLS, SUCH AS CHILDHOOD OBESITY AND THE OPIOID EPIDEMIC. 3) FOOD SAFETY AND SECURITY. EXTENSION AND RESEARCH STRIVE TO REDUCE THE EXTENT OF FOODBORNE ILLNESSES DUE TO CONTAMINATION OF FOOD. PROGRAMS ARE DESIGNED TO TACKLE THE PROBLEM AT THE LEVELS OF PRODUCTION, PROCESSING, AND CONSUMPTION. RESEARCH INCLUDES THE REDUCTION OF RISKS FROM NATURAL AND MANUFACTURED TOXINS IN FOOD. A SIGNIFICANT AMOUNT OF EFFORT IS PLACED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF SECURING NUTRITIOUS LOCAL FOODS THROUGH ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS. 4) AGRICULTURAL, ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES. RESEARCH-BASED PRACTICES AND TOOLS DELIVERED THROUGH EXTENSION WILL ENSURE CONTINUED PRODUCTIVITY OF OUR PRODUCERS WHILE ENCOURAGING THE IMPLEMENTATION OF SOUND ENVIRONMENTAL PRACTICES AND EFFECTIVE STEWARDSHIP OF NATURAL RESOURCES. PROGRAMS MUST ADDRESS WATER QUALITY, SOIL HEALTH AND CONSERVATION IN ROW- CROP PRODUCTION, INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT AND POLLINATOR HEALTH, AQUACULTURE, HORTICULTURE, ORGANIC PRODUCTION, MASTER GARDENERS, AND SUSTAINABLE ANIMAL PRODUCTION AND MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS. PROGRAMS WILL ALSO CONTINUE FOCUSING ON SUSTAINABLE ENERGY AND AGROECOSYSTEM ADAPTATION TO CLIMATE CHANGE.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: THE EXTENSION AND OUTREACH COMPONENT OF TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY'S LAND-GRANT MISSION IS CARRIED OUT THROUGH THE COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM IN THE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE. THE PROGRAM'S MISSION AND PURPOSE ARE TO PROVIDE RESEARCH-BASED, PRACTICAL INFORMATION TO TENNESSEE RESIDENTS THROUGH INFORMAL EDUCATION ACTIVITIES AIMED AT ENHANCING THE STATE'S SOCIAL, ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL STRENGTHS. TSU EXTENSION PLACES A SPECIFIC EMPHASIS ON REACHING OUT TO ASSIST FARMERS AND PRODUCERS, WORKING COLLABORATIVELY WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF TENNESSEE, THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE, AND COUNTY GOVERNMENTS. TSU EXTENSION HAS A BROAD REACH, WITH COUNTY EXTENSION AGENTS/EDUCATORS, PROGRAM ASSISTANTS, AND SUPPORT STAFF IN 62 COUNTIES ACROSS THE STATE. THESE COUNTY PERSONNEL RECEIVE TRAINING AND EDUCATIONAL/TEACHING MATERIAL FROM TSU'S 23 CAMPUS-BASED EXTENSION FACULTY/SPECIALISTS. OUR PROGRAM IS ORGANIZED AROUND ADDRESSING CRITICAL ISSUES IN FOUR EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING AREAS: 1) AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES (ANR), WHICH ENHANCES URBAN AND RURAL PROSPERITY BY ASSISTING FARMERS AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES IN PRODUCING FIELD CROPS, RAISING LIVESTOCK, MANAGING FORESTLANDS, GROWING FRUIT CROPS, AND TENDING HOME GARDENS, ACTIVITIES THAT PRODUCE FOOD AND ENHANCE THE ENVIRONMENTAL INTEGRITY OF THE AGROECOSYSTEM. THE ANR PROGRAM PROVIDES FARMERS WITH A WIDE VARIETY OF RESEARCH-BASED TRAINING PROGRAMS TO ENHANCE THEIR PRODUCTIVITY, FARM PROFITABILITY, AND QUALITY OF LIFE, FOCUSING ON ENTOMOLOGY, FORESTRY, SOIL HEALTH, BIOENERGY, PLANT NUTRITION, HORTICULTURE, DRONE TECHNOLOGY,  ECONOMICS, AGRICULTURAL COMMUNICATIONS, AND ORGANIC AGRICULTURE; 2) FAMILY AND CONSUMER SCIENCES, WHICH IMPROVES THE HEALTH AND WELL-BEING OF TENNESSEANS BY PROVIDING PROGRAMS ON COMMUNITY NUTRITION EDUCATION, NUTRITION SECURITY, FAMILY AND CHILD DEVELOPMENT, AND HEALTH EDUCATION; 3) EXTENSION 4-H YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, WHICH FOCUSES ON WORKFORCE PREPARATION, STEM EDUCATION, LIFE SKILLS DEVELOPMENT, YOUTH LEADERSHIP, CITIZENSHIP DEVELOPMENT, AND COMMUNICATION SKILLS; AND 4) COMMUNITY RESOURCES AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT, WHICH PROVIDES INFORMATION AND EDUCATION ON COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT, AND LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT AND INCLUDES AGRIBUSINESS/ENTREPRENEURSHIP TRAINING FOR FARMERS (MANAGEMENT, BASIC ACCOUNTING, ECONOMIC PRINCIPLES, BUDGETS, WHOLE-FARM PLANNING, LAND ACQUISITION, TAX MANAGEMENT) AS WELL AS TRAINING FOR LOCAL BUSINESSES (BUSINESS PLANNING, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, MODERN MARKETING AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT).
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: THE EXTENSION AND OUTREACH COMPONENT OF THE LAND-GRANT MISSION OF THE TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY IS CARRIED OUT THROUGH THE COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM, IN THE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE. THE MISSION AND PURPOSE OF THE COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM IS TO PROVIDE RES EARCH-BASED, USEFUL, AND PRACTICAL INFORMATION TO THE RESIDENTS OF TENNESSEE, THROUGH INFORMAL EDUCATION TO IMPROVE SOCIAL, ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS. TSU'S COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM PUTS EXTRA EFFORT TO REACH OUT TO THE LIMITED-RESOURCE FARMERS, PRODUCERS, AND OTHERS IN THE STATE OF TENNESSEE. AND THE PROGRAM WORKS COLLABORATIVELY WITH UNIVERSITY OF TENNESSEE, THE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE, AND THE COUNTY GOVERNMENTS. TSU EXTENSION HAS COUNTY EXTENSION AGENTS/EDUCATORS, PROGRAM ASSISTANTS, AND SUPPORT STAFF IN 60 COUNTIES ACROSS THE STATE. OUR COUNTY PERSONNEL RECEIVE THEIR TRAINING AND EDUCATIONAL/TEACHING MATERIAL FROM THE 20 CAMPUS-BASED EXTENSION FACULTY/SPECIALISTS. COOPERATIVE EXTENSION AT TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY IS ORGANIZED TO ADDRESS CRITICAL ISSUES IN FOUR EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING CATEGORIES. I. AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES (ANR) THE AGRICULTURE, NATURAL RESOURCES (ANR) PROGRAMS FOCUSES ON ASSISTING FARMERS AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES IN GROWING FIELD CROPS OR HOME GARDENS TO PRODUCE FOOD AND ENHANCE ENVIRONMENTAL INTEGRITY OF THE AGROECOSYSTEM. THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES A WIDE VARIETY OF RESEARCHBASED TRAINING PROGRAMS TO FARMERS TO ENHANCE THEIR PRODUCTIVITY, FARM PROFITABILITY, AND QUALITY OF LIFE. THE KEY COMPONENTS OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE ENTOMOLOGY, PLANT PATHOLOGY, SOIL HEALTH, BIOENERGY, PLANT NUTRITION, HORTICULTURE, DRONE TECHNOLOGY, ECONOMICS, AGRICULTURAL COMMUNICATIONS AND ORGANIC AGRICULTURE. THE OVERALL GOAL OF THE ANR PROGRAM IS TO ENHANCE THE RURAL PROSPERITY. II. FAMILY AND CONSUMER SCIENCES (FCS) FAMILY CONSUMER SCIENCE PROGRAM FOCUSES ON COMMUNITY NUTRITION EDUCATION, NUTRITION SECURITY, FAMILY AND CHILD DEVELOPMENT AND HEALTH EDUCATION THROUGH THE EXTENSION AGENTS AND CAMPUS BASED FACULTY WITH THE OBJECTIVE OF IMPROVING HEALTH AND WELLBEING OF THE POPULATION. III. EXTENSION 4-H YOUTH DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM THE 4-H AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM FOCUSES ON WORKFORCE PREPARATION, STEM EDUCATION, LIFE SKILLS DEVELOPMENT, YOUTH LEADERSHIP AND CITIZENSHIP DEVELOPMENT AND COMMUNICATION SKILLS. IV. COMMUNITY RESOURCES AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT: THE FOCUS OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION AND EDUCATION ON COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT. TO ACHIEVE THIS OBJECTIVE, TSU EXTENSION PROGRAM PROVIDES AGRIBUSINESS AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP TRAINING TO FARMERS. THE TRAINING INCLUDES TOPICS ON MANAGEMENT, BASIC ACCOUNTING, ECONOMIC PRINCIPLES, BUDGETS, WHOLE-FARM PLANNING, LAND ACQUISITION, TAX MANAGEMENT, AND WHOLE FARM PLANNING. THE PROGRAM ALSO PROVIDES TRAINING TO LOCAL BUSINESSES ON BUSINESS PLANNING, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, MODERN MARKETING AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: COLLEGE OF DUPAGE WILL CREATE EMPLOYER-ALIGNED SHORT-TERM AVIATION AND UAS CERTIFICATES THAT USE NEW FACULTY, SIMULATION, AND DRONE TECHNOLOGY TO QUICKLY PREPARE STUDENTS FOR HIGH-SKILL AVIATION JOBS

RII TRACK-2 FEC: NATURAL RESOURCE SUPPLY CHAIN OPTIMIZATION USING AERIAL IMAGERY INTERPRETED WITH MACHINE LEARNING METHODS -THE UNIVERSITY OF MONTANA AND THE UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA, ANCHORAGE WILL CONDUCT SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH THAT IS RESPONSIVE TO THE NEEDS OF THEIR NATURAL RESOURCE BASED ECONOMIES. SPECIFICALLY, QUESTIONS FROM THE AREAS OF SNOW WATER RESOURCES, WILDLAND FIRE MANAGEMENT, AND ABANDONED OIL WELL MONITORING WILL DRIVE THE RESEARCH AGENDA. THE SCIENTIFIC WORK IS UNITED BY A COMMON SET OF TECHNIQUES FOR ANSWERING QUESTIONS. MOREOVER, THE COMMON APPROACH IS TO: 1) USE UNMANNED FLYING VEHICLES CALLED DRONES TO COLLECT PICTURES AND OTHER MEASUREMENTS. WHILE THE INFORMATION ACQUIRED BY DRONES IS HIGH QUALITY, IT IS ALSO A LARGE AMOUNT OF COMPLEX DATA. 2) TO AID IN THE DATA?S INTERPRETATION AND ADDRESS THE SCIENCE QUESTIONS, WE USE MACHINE LEARNING METHODS THAT TRAIN COMPUTERS TO IDENTIFY PATTERNS IN DATA. COLLECTING DATA USING DRONES AND THE USE OF MACHINE LEARNING ARE CRITICAL SKILLS FOR AMERICA?S FUTURE WORKFORCE. OUR ACTIVITIES ARE ALIGNED WITH CAREER TRAINING THROUGH PARTNERSHIPS WITH LOCAL COMPANIES AND INTERNSHIPS FOR PARTICIPATING STUDENTS. WITH THE EMPHASIS ON INTERNSHIPS, THE FOCUS WILL BE RETENTION, RATHER THAN RECRUITMENT OF STUDENTS. DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION EFFORTS WILL WORK IN TANDEM WITH WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT TO ENSURE THAT INDIGENOUS, LOW INCOME, AND RURAL MEMBERS OF OUR JURISDICTIONS ARE INTEGRAL TO THE EFFORTS. IT IS PROPOSED TO USE MACHINE LEARNING (ML) TO PROCESS IMAGERY AND OTHER DATA ACQUIRED BY AUTONOMOUS AERIAL SYSTEMS (UAS). PROCESSED DATA WILL SUPPORT SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH IN NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT BY PROVIDING A CLEAR MEANS OF TESTING HYPOTHESES. THE THREE AREAS OF NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT TO APPLY THIS APPROACH ARE: 1) SNOW WATER RESOURCES, BECAUSE ENERGY PRODUCTION, AGRICULTURAL OUTPUT, AND ECONOMIC GROWTH REQUIRE IMPROVED ASSESSMENT OF THE NATURAL CAPITAL BANKED IN THE MOUNTAIN SNOWPACK. 2) FIRE MANAGEMENT AND SCIENCE, BECAUSE AN ADVANCED UNDERSTANDING OF THE PHYSICAL AND ECOLOGICAL PROCESSES DRIVING WILDFIRE IS REQUIRED FOR MANAGEMENT PRACTICES THAT BETTER PROTECT FORESTS AND THE CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE WITHIN THEM. 3) ABANDONED OIL WELL MONITORING, BECAUSE DETECTING AND MAPPING UNCAPPED OR IMPROPERLY SEALED OIL AND GAS WELLS WILL PROVIDE CRITICAL DATA FOR IMPROVED MITIGATION, SITE RECLAMATION, AND HAZARD REMOVAL. THE TEAM BRING TOGETHER THE UNIVERSITY OF MONTANA AND THE UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA ANCHORAGE TO CONDUCT LOCALLY RELEVANT RESEARCH. THE LOCAL FOCUS STRENGTHENS THE RELATIONS WITH NEARBY COMMERCIAL INTERESTS TO IDENTIFY QUESTIONS COMMON TO ACADEMIC AND COMMERCIAL ENDEAVORS IN MODERN NATURAL RESOURCE BASED ECONOMIES. ATTENTION IS CALLED TO UAS AND ML AS SKILLS VITAL TO BOTH COMMERCIAL AND ACADEMIC WORK. TO ADVANCE THE NATURAL RESOURCE BASED ADVANCED MANUFACTURING INDUSTRIES OF THE JURISDICTIONS, SPECIAL CONSIDERATION IS GIVEN TO DEVELOPING A WORKFORCE THAT SPANS FROM TWO-YEAR COLLEGE GRADUATES TO JUNIOR FACULTY MEMBERS. THIS AWARD'S PLAN CENTERS ON PAID, CREDIT-BEARING INTERNSHIPS WITH KEY PARTNERS, DRAWING COMMERCIAL INTERESTS INTO THE PROPOSED WORK, AND BRINGING JUNIOR FACULTY TO THE RESEARCH. DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION EFFORTS WILL WORK IN TANDEM WITH WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT TO ENSURE THAT INDIGENOUS, LOW INCOME, AND RURAL MEMBERS OF THE JURISDICTIONS ARE AN INTEGRAL PART OF EFFORTS. WITH THE EMPHASIS ON INTERNSHIPS, THE FOCUS WILL BE RETENTION, RATHER THAN RECRUITMENT OF STUDENTS. THE PROJECT ENGAGES WITH DISRUPTIVE TECHNOLOGIES THAT HAVE LONG REACHING CONSEQUENCES FOR WORKERS. TO ADDRESS THE CONSEQUENCES RELATED TO THESE TECHNOLOGIES, THE TEAM ENLISTED THE AID OF A SOCIAL SCIENTIST TO CONDUCT STUDIES EVALUATING THE SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC IMPACTS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.

THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2025. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS MANY OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN THE STATE'S 1890 AND 1862 LAND-GRANT INSTITUTIONS, FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY (FVSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA (UGA), THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDES CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS PLANT AND ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES (I.E. AI-POWERED DRONES AND AUTONOMOUS TRACTORS IN AGRICULTURE). USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE MODEL INCLUDES A METHOD FOR ASSESSING NEEDS AND IDENTIFYING PROBLEMS. IT ALSO INCLUDES PROGRAM EVALUATION TO DETERMINE IMPACTS OF EXTENSION EDUCATIONAL TRAININGS. THE MODEL WORKS IN UNISON WITH MULTIPLE ADVISORY SYSTEMS AT BOTH COUNTY AND STATE LEVELS. GOALS OF THESE PROGRAMS ARE TO DEMONSTRATE SHORT AND LONG-TERM IMPACTS. STATE FUNDING SUCH AS STATE-MATCH, COUNTY FUNDING, GRANTS AND GIFTS LEVERAGED AS A DIRECT RESULT OF THE WORK IN THIS PLAN AIDS IN CREATING THE GREATEST FINAL IMPACTS. CRITICAL ISSUES ARE RELATED TO ANIMAL PRODUCTION; COMMUNITY, HOME AND LIFE SKILLS; FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY; PLANT PRODUCTION; SUSTAINABILITY, CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT; URBAN AGRICULTURE; AND YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT. USDA FACILITY FUNDING AFFORDED FVSU EXTENSION THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLETE ITS COLD VEGETABLE PROCESSING FACILITY. THIS FACILITY IS A COMPLIMENT TO FVSU RESEARCH MEAT PROCESSING FACILITY (ENGAGED IN EXPANSION) AS THEY ARE USED TO ADDRESS CONSUMER DEMAND FOR FOOD AND VALUE-ADDED MARKETING OPPORTUNITIES FOR FARMERS. ALSO, DURING THIS FISCAL YEAR, EFFORTS TO MAXIMIZE RETURN ON INVESTMENT VIA IMPACTS, THE 1890 EXTENSION REGION INSTITUTED A PROJECT TO GATHER PROGRAM DATA ACROSS THE 1890 REGION (ALL 1890 LAND-GRANT UNIVERSITIES) CALLED COMMON MEASURES. THE GEORGIA POW IS DESIGN TO MEET THE EMERGING ISSUES OF OUR COMMUNITIES, SUPPORT THE S USTAINABILITY AND PROFITABILITY OF THE AGRICULTURE INDUSTRY WHILE PROVIDING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS FOR FAMILIES AND YOUTH.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2023. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, NUTRITION, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES, AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY AND THE UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDE CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, PLANT PRODUCTION, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES, SUCH AS BIOFUELS AND DRONE TECHNOLOGY. USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE MODEL INCLUDES A METHOD FOR ASSESSING NEEDS AND IDENTIFYING PROBLEMS. IT ALSO INCLUDES PROGRAM EVALUATION TO DETERMINE IMPACT OF EXTENSION EDUCATIONAL TRAININGS. THE MODEL WORKS IN UNISON WITH MULTIPLE ADVISORY SYSTEMS AT BOTH COUNTY AND STATE LEVELS. GOALS OF THESE PROGRAMS ARE TO DEMONSTRATE SHORT AND LONG-TERM IMPACTS. STATE FUNDING SUCH AS STATE MATCH, COUNTY FUNDING, GRANTS AND GIFTS LEVERAGED AS A DIRECT RESULT OF THE WORK IN THIS PLAN AIDS IN CREATING THE GREATEST FINAL IMPACTS. THE RESEARCH PORTION OF THE PLAN OF WORK UNDERGOES SCIENTIFIC PEER REVIEW PRIOR TO EACH PROJECT BEING SUBMITTED. ALL SCIENTISTS ARE REQUIRED TO HAVE ACTIVE PROJECTS FOR EXPENDITURES TO BE MADE. EACH PROJECT IS PEER-REVIEWED BY BOTH INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL REVIEWERS AND MUST BE APPROVED BY THE APPROPRIATE DEAN AND DIRECTOR PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURE. MOST APPLIED RESEARCH PROGRAMS ARE DEVELOPED BASED ON FEEDBACK FROM THE STAKEHOLDERS THROUGH THE EXTENSION CONTACTS AND BASIC RESEARCH ARE CONDUCTED TO FIND SPECIFIC SCIENTIFIC ANSWERS OR TO MAKE DISCOVERY. CURRENT ANIMAL RELATED PROJECTS INVOLVE EFFICIENT PRODUCTION SYSTEMS INCLUDING ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND NUTRITION, ANIMAL HEALTH AND WELLBEING WITH INTERNAL PARASITE CONTROL, AND ECONOMIC PROSPERITY A ND MARKETING USING SHEEP, GOATS, AND CATTLE AS MODELS. CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURE WAS INTRODUCED THIS YEAR ALONG WITH OTHER 1890 INSTITUTIONS UNDER THE 1890 MULTISTATE CLIMATE CHANGE PROJECT WITHIN THE EVANS-ALLEN PROGRAM. THE FOOD SAFETY PROGRAM EMPHASIZES NON-THERMAL METHODS TO CONTROL FOOD BORN PATHOGENS IN MEAT AND NUTS. PLANT PRODUCTION PROGRAM ENCOMPASS A BROAD ARRAY OF TARGETED FIELDS OF STUDY THAT INCLUDE ENTOMOLOGY, PLANT PATHOLOGY, NEMATOLOGY, PLANT BREEDING AND GENETICS, SOIL SCIENCE, POSTHARVEST PHYSIOLOGY. SOME OF THESE PROJECTS ARE DONE IN COLLABORATION WITH ARS SCIENTISTS ON COMMODITY OF GREAT IMPORTANCE TO THE STATE OF GEORGIA. FUNDING FROM THE FEDERAL ALLOCATION AND THE CORRESPONDING MATCHING FUND FROM THE STATE ARE USED TO COVER EXPENSES RELATED TO THESE ACTIVITIES. ADDITIONAL FUNDS COME FROM EXTERNAL GRANTS SECURED BY THE RESEARCH SCIENTISTS. APPLIED RESEARCH PROJECTS USING THESE FUNDS TARGET MAINLY LIMITED-RESOURCE PRODUCERS IN THE STATE BUT SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES IMPACT THE CITIZEN OF STATE OF GEORGIA, THE COUNTRY, AND THE WORLD.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: SOUTH CAROLINA STATE UNIVERSITY (SC STATE) PROVIDES NON-BIASED, SCIENCE-BASED INFORMATION TO CITIZENS ACROSS THE STATE ON CRITICAL I SSUES SUCH AS AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT; AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY; ENVIRONMENTAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES CONSERVATION; FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, AND FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION. NINE COMPETITIVE FUNDED GRANTS WERE APPROVED AND IMPLEMENTED. DURING THE FISCAL YEAR, SEVEN RESEARCH PROJECTS WERE CONDUCTED. RELEASE TIME DOLLARS INCLUDING FRINGE BENEFITS TOTAL $548 ,812.14, WHICH WAS A SAVINGS FOR THE UNIVERSITY. THE GRANTS DEALT WITH FOOD INSECURITY, FOOD SAFETY, RURAL BUSINESS, SMALL FARMS, DRONES AND AI, HIGH TUNNEL DEMONSTRATIONS AND AGDISCOVERY SUMMER ENRICHMENT PROGRAMS. THE EXTENSION PROGRAM SERVED 221,779 DIRECT CONTACTS THROUGH 571 WORKSHOPS AND EVENTS. THERE WERE 320 IN PERSON SESSIONS CONDUCTED ACROSS THE PROGRAM AREAS, WHILE 5 HYBRID SESSIONS WERE TAUGHT, AND 7 VIRTUAL SESSIONS WERE IMPLEMENTED. SC STATE PLANNED AND HOSTED ITS FIRST AG AND LAND-GRANT CELEBRATION. THE EVENT EMPHASIZED THE IMPACT OF SC STATE'S AG PROGRAMS AND BROUGHT AWARENESS TO THE INSTITUTION'S LAND-GRANT MISSION OF IMPROVING THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES AND COMMUNITIES THROUGH RESEARCH, TEACHING, AND OUTREACH. PRESS RELEASES INCREASED AND SOCIAL MEDIA FOLLOWERS AND INTERACTIONS INCREASED 50% ON ALL PLATFORMS.

awards.Description: COLLABORATIVE RESEARCH: FRAMEWORKS: GROWING OPEN ONDEMAND: LEVERAGING UNIFIED COMMUNITY KNOWLEDGE (GOODLUCK) -THE ABILITY TO PROCESS LARGE SETS OF DATA ON RESEARCH COMPUTING (RC) PLATFORMS HAS LED TO REMARKABLE ADVANCES IN SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING AND HAS BECOME AN INDISPENSABLE TOOL FOR RESEARCHERS, INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS, AND STUDENTS. THE IMPORTANCE OF RC PLATFORMS AND TOOLS (AND IN PARTICULAR THOSE INVOLVING AI TECHNOLOGY) HAS BEEN RECOGNIZED BY THE HIGHEST LEVELS OF THE UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT, AS EVIDENT BY THE NATIONAL AI INITIATIVE ACT OF 2020, WHICH CALLS FOR THE CREATION OF A NATIONAL ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE RESEARCH RESOURCE (NAIRR). UNFORTUNATELY, FOR MANY INDIVIDUALS, RC USE AND ADOPTION IS HINDERED BY THE COMPLEX WAY IN WHICH THESE RESOURCES ARE ACCESSED. WHILE WEB BROWSERS AND SMART DEVICES HAVE BECOME THE DOMINANT ACCESS MECHANISM FOR REMOTE CONSUMER AND ENTERPRISE COMPUTING SERVICES, THE ADOPTION OF SUCH MECHANISMS HAS LAGGED FOR MANY RC SERVICE PROVIDERS, CREATING AN ACCESSIBILITY GAP THAT IMPEDES FURTHER ADOPTION. OPEN ONDEMAND IS A MATURE, NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FUNDED, OPEN-SOURCE PLATFORM THAT ENABLES REMOTE WEB ACCESS TO RC SERVICES, THEREBY SIMPLIFYING USE OF THOSE RESOURCES AND FACILITATING COLLABORATION. RC CLIENTS CAN MANAGE FILES AND JOBS, CREATE AND SHARE APPS, RUN GUI APPLICATIONS, AND UTILIZE A TRADITIONAL TERMINAL ALL THROUGH A WEB BROWSER FROM ANYWHERE ON ANY DEVICE BECAUSE IT REQUIRES NO SPECIALIZED CLIENT SOFTWARE AND HAS A SIMPLE INTERFACE THAT IS EASY TO LEARN AND USE. THE OPEN ONDEMAND COMMUNITY HAS BEGUN TO EXPOSE PLATFORM LIMITATIONS AND REQUEST ADDITIONAL FEATURES AND CAPABILITIES, MANY OF WHICH WILL REQUIRE SIGNIFICANT COLLABORATIVE DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS TO IMPLEMENT. FURTHER ADOPTION OF THIS POWERFUL PLATFORM, AND THE RESULTANT SCIENTIFIC ADVANCES, CAN BE ACCELERATED VIA A VARIETY OF INNOVATIONS: (1) CATALOG, PROVIDING CAPABILITIES TO CONCEPTUALIZE, CREATE, MODIFY, SHARE, AND PUBLICIZE APPS; (2) CLASSROOM, FOCUSED ON OPEN ONDEMAND IN EDUCATIONAL ENVIRONMENTS; (3) CUSTOMIZATION, INVOLVING INNOVATION IN CLIENT INTERFACES AND NEW TECHNOLOGIES; AND (4) COMMUNITY, FACILITATING COMMUNICATIONS AMONG THE COMMUNITY. A CROSS-CUTTING ASPECT OF THE GOODLUCK PROJECT WILL PILOT AND DELIVER INTEGRATED SETS OF TOOLS, DOCUMENTATION, AND TRAINING MATERIALS, WHICH RESEARCHERS CAN SELECT FROM TO CREATE AN ENVIRONMENT TAILORED TO THEIR RESEARCH. INITIAL BENEFICIARIES OF THESE EFFORTS INCLUDE: (1) BIOCHEMISTS UTILIZING CRYOGENIC ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (CRYO-EM) TO IDENTIFY THE UNDERLYING MECHANISMS OF VIRAL ACTION; (2) MATERIALS SCIENTISTS DESIGNING NEW ALLOYS FROM FIRST PRINCIPLES; (3) ART DESIGN STUDENTS CREATING NOVEL WORKS VIA THE USE OF GENERATIVE ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE; AND (4) AEROSPACE ENGINEERS IMPROVING THE PERFORMANCE OF DRONES VIA COMPUTATIONAL FLUID DYNAMICS. IN SUMMARY, THE GOODLUCK EFFORT WILL PROVIDE TOOLS AND PROCESSES THAT ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO MORE EASILY AND EXTENSIVELY UTILIZE OPEN ONDEMAND, AS WELL AS TO ANALYZE AND SHARE THEIR COMPUTATIONAL RESULTS, REGARDLESS OF THEIR ACCESS TO RC RESOURCES OR POSITION IN THE RESEARCH LIFECYCLE. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.
BIOMATERIAL DRONES FOR IMAGE-GUIDED DRUG DELIVERY DURING RADIOTHERAPY
awards.Awarding Agency: Department of Health and Human Services

awards.Description: PURPOSE: THE PURPOSE OF THE GRANT PROGRAM IS TO DEVELOP A COMPREHENSIVE PHOTONIC SENSOR SYSTEM TRAINING AND TESTING LABORATORY AT MONTANA STATE UNIVERSITY TO ENHANCE EDUCATION AND INDUSTRY SKILLS IN PHOTONIC REMOTE SENSORS. THIS INCLUDES ESTABLISHING TRAINING FACILITIES, HIRING EXPERTS, DEVELOPING CORE PHOTONIC SENSORS, AND PROVIDING TRAINING TO BOTH STUDENTS AND INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS. THE PROGRAM AIMS TO SUPPORT LONG-TERM PHOTONIC SENSOR CHARACTERIZATION AND FACILITATE THE COMMERCIALIZATION OF PHOTONICS TECHNOLOGY THROUGH HANDS-ON TRAINING AND COLLABORATIVE INDUSTRY PROJECTS. ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED:  PROCURE EQUIPMENT AND SUPPLIES AND CREATE FOUR TRAINING TEST STATIONS. COLLABORATE WITH THE HEADWATERS TECH HUB-ADVANCED SENSOR SYSTEM INTEGRATION AND SUPPORT TECHNOLOGY (HTH-ASSIST) PROJECT TO DEVELOP A SYSTEM FOR PHOTONIC SENSOR USERS AND MANUFACTURERS TO PROVIDE ONGOING INPUT REGARDING TRAINING NEEDS. COORDINATE WITH GLOBAL PHOTONICS ORGANIZATIONS TO DEFINE THE POSITION AND IDENTIFY RECRUITING METHODS. RECRUIT NATIONALLY FOR INTEGRATED PHOTONICS FACULTY, PREFERABLY WITH EXPERIENCE COLLABORATING WITH OR WORKING IN INDUSTRY. RUGGEDIZE LIDAR FOR AIRBORNE FIELD TESTING AT HEADWATERS TECH HUB (HTH) TESTBEDS. CONDUCT AIRBORNE LIDAR TEST FLIGHTS AT HTH-ASSIST TESTBEDS. INTEGRATE A SMART HYPERSPECTRAL IMAGER ONTO AN AERIAL DRONE FOR TEST FLIGHTS IN COLLABORATION WITH INDUSTRY. INCORPORATE NEW HTH CORE SENSOR SYSTEMS INTO EXISTING ?REMOTE SENSING SYSTEMS,? ?OPTICAL DESIGN,? AND ?ELECTRO-OPTICAL SYSTEMS? COURSES AT MONTANA STATE UNIVERSITY. COORDINATE WITH THE MONTANA PHOTONICS & QUANTUM ALLIANCE (MPQA) AND HTH TO CONDUCT INDUSTRY TRAINING SESSIONS. COLLABORATE WITH TRIBAL AND COMMUNITY COLLEGES AND OTHER MONTANA UNIVERSITIES TO CONDUCT PHOTONIC REMOTE SENSOR TRAINING FOR STUDENTS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES: ESTABLISHMENT FOUR OPERATIONAL TRAINING TEST STATIONS AND HIRE OF AN INTEGRATED PHOTONICS EXPERT. TRAIN AT LEAST 100 MONTANA STATE UNIVERSITY STUDENTS AND 200 NON-MSU STUDENTS AND INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS. CONDUCT TESTBED DEPLOYMENTS AT THREE DIFFERENT LOCATIONS, AND PRODUCING FUNCTIONAL AIRBORNE LIDAR AND HYPERSPECTRAL IMAGING SYSTEMS. GATHER INDUSTRY FEEDBACK THROUGH SURVEYS AND MAINTAIN AN ONLINE PORTAL FOR ONGOING TRAINING INPUT. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES:  BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE MONTANA STATE UNIVERSITY STUDENTS WHO WILL GAIN HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE AND TRAINING IN PHOTONIC SENSOR SYSTEMS, AS WELL AS INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS WHO WILL RECEIVE SPECIALIZED TRAINING AND SUPPORT IN PHOTONICS TECHNOLOGY. INDUSTRY PARTNERS AND ORGANIZATIONS INVOLVED IN PHOTONICS WILL BENEFIT THROUGH ENHANCED COLLABORATION, FEEDBACK MECHANISMS, AND THE DEVELOPMENT OF ADVANCED SENSOR TECHNOLOGIES. COMMUNITY COLLEGES AND OTHER MONTANA UNIVERSITIES WILL ALSO BENEFIT FROM COLLABORATIVE TRAINING OPPORTUNITIES. LOCAL WORKFORCE WILL BENEFIT FROM INCREASED EMPLOYMENT THROUGH THE HIRING OF EXPERT FACULTY AND EXPERTS IN RELATED TESTING SYSTEMS.

AWARD DESCRIPTION CTL23-1004-E00 SMART-X INTERNET OF THINGS (IOT) LIVING LAB PURPOSE:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS THE DEVELOPMENT OF A SMART TECHNOLOGY LAB AND EXPERIMENTAL TESTING ENVIRONMENT ENABLING ADVANCES IN INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGIES, INCLUDING FOR USE IN SMART CITIES AND COMMUNITIES, UNCREWED AERIAL SYSTEMS (UAS), AUTONOMOUS VEHICLES, ROBOTIC MANUFACTURING SYSTEMS, AND OTHER APPLICATIONS.ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED:THE RESEARCHERS WILL DEVELOP A STRATEGIC PLAN FOR, AND IMPLEMENT, A FLEXIBLE, SECURE, STANDARDS-BASED RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURE, INCLUDING ADVANCED COMMUNICATIONS AND COMPUTATIONAL CAPABILITIES. THIS RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURE WILL ENABLE THE FUTURE USE, EXPERIMENTATION, AND TESTING OF CYBER-PHYSICAL SYSTEMS (CPS) AND INTERNET OF THINGS (IOT) CAPABILITIES IN SELECTED SMART-X APPLICATIONS, WHERE X CAN BE A HOME, A CITY OR MILITARY BASE, A COMPLEX INDUSTRIAL CONTROL SYSTEM, AUTONOMOUS VEHICLE (DRONE), OR OTHER TECHNOLOGY APPLICATIONS OF COMMUNITY INTEREST. EXPECTED OUTCOMES:THE RESULTS OF RESEARCH IN THE PLANNED SMART TECHNOLOGY LAB AND CPS/IOT TESTING ENVIRONMENT ARE EXPECTED TO ADVANCE THE NATION'S GOALS FOR EFFICIENT, SUSTAINABLE, AND RESILIENT CPS/IOT-BASED SYSTEMS INCLUDING AUTONOMOUS TRANSPORTATION, ADVANCED MANUFACTURING, AND CLEAN ENERGY. THE PLANNED DEVELOPMENT OF A METHODOLOGY FOR IDENTIFYING AND EVALUATING PROJECT OUTCOMES, ALIGNED WITH THE NIST SMART CITIES FRAMEWORK AND NIST HOLISTIC KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS FRAMEWORK, WILL DEMONSTRATE FEASIBILITY FOR WIDER CIVIC ADOPTION AND REPLICATION IN OTHER SMART CITIES AND COMMUNITIES, AND SUPPORT IMPROVED SERVICES, ECONOMIC GROWTH, AND ENHANCED QUALITY OF LIFE FOR THEIR CITIZENS. THE RESEARCH AND ENHANCED TESTING CAPABILITIES WILL ALSO SUPPORT A GLOBALLY COMPETITIVE U.S. MANUFACTURING SECTOR THROUGH ADVANCEMENTS IN INTELLIGENT CONTROL SYSTEMS AND ROBOTICS.INTENDED BENEFICIARIES:CITY OF ROME, NY AND ONEIDA COUNTY HIGH-TECH ECOSYSTEM DEVELOPMENT; U.S. TRANSPORTATION, MANUFACTURING, AND ENERGY SECTORS; AND RESEARCHERS AND STUDENTS IN THE NORTHEAST ACADEMIC AND INDUSTRIAL COMMUNITIES.SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES:THE RECIPIENT PLANS TO SUBAWARD FUNDS, FOR STEM CAMP INDIVIDUAL INSTRUCTORS, BRANDING AND MARKETING OUTREACH, USE INSPIRED CHALLENGE GRANTS (4), CYBER DEVICE CHALLENGE HACKING EVENTS (4), AND A CONTRACTED WRITER.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Standards and Technology

awards.Description: THIS MCRA AAM EDUCATION PROGRAM WILL INSPIRE  ENGAGE  EDUCATE  AND EMPLOY THE  NEXT GENERATION OF EXPLORERS  IN WEST VIRGINIA AND APPALACHIA FOR THE FUTURE JOBS IN THE AAM MARKETPLACE. TODAY  MANY INDUSTRIES ARE NOW DEVELOPING AND LEVERAGING DRONE TECHNOLOGIES TO ADVANCE THE DELIVERY OF GOODS AND SERVICES FROM HEALTHCARE USE CASES TO INFRASTRUCTURE INSPECTION USE CASES  TO FIRST RESPONDER AND NATURAL DISASTER DEPLOYMENT USE CASES  AND WITH MORE APPLICATIONS OF THESE TECHNOLOGIES YET TO BE DISCOVERED. ALL THESE UNCREWED AERIAL SYSTEMS (UAS) ACTIVITIES AND OPERATIONS WILL CREATE SIGNIFICANT JOB OPPORTUNITIES FOR THOSE WHO ARE PREPARED AS THIS MARKET REALIZES SIGNIFICANT GROWTH OVER THE NEXT DECADE. MINGO COUNTY  WHERE COAL MINING ONCE PROVIDED SUFFICIENT ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY FOR FAMILIES  IS NOW AN UNDERREPRESENTED GROUP WITHOUT EQUAL OPPORTUNITY WITHIN OUR SCHOOL SYSTEMS AND WITH SIGNIFICANT CHALLENGES IN ACHIEVING ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT FOR THE JOBS OF THE FUTURE.

IN 2024, SC STATE UNIVERSITY ADVANCED ITS MISSION OF TEACHING, RESEARCH, AND OUTREACH BY DELIVERING IMPACTFUL PROGRAMS ACROSS 32 SOUTH CAROLINA COUNTIES. THE UNIVERSITY FOCUSED ON FIVE CRITICAL ISSUES: AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, ENVIRONMENTAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES CONSERVATION, FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, AND FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION. THESE INITIATIVES AIMED TO IMPROVE THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR CITIZENS THROUGH EDUCATION, INNOVATION, AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT. UNDER AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, SC STATE ADDRESSED CHALLENGES SUCH AS UNEMPLOYMENT, POVERTY, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS. STAFF FACILITATED 20 WORKSHOPS, 7 IN-PERSON, 7 HYBRID, AND 6 VIRTUAL, REACHING 9,269 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,948 HOURS AND 37,727 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,402 HOURS. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS REPORTED KNOWLEDGE GAINS, AND SIX JOBS WERE CREATED THROUGH A RURAL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE GRANT. IN AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, THE SMALL FARMS TEAM CONDUCTED 58 WORKSHOPS AND WORKED WITH OVER 577 SMALL FARMERS. THEY LOGGED 3,969 DIRECT CONTACT HOURS WITH 16,269 INDIVIDUALS AND 1,516 INDIRECT HOURS WITH 9,678 CONTACTS. PROGRAMMING COVERED ANIMAL PRODUCTION, SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE, AND RISK MANAGEMENT. TOPICS INCLUDED BEEF CATTLE HERD HEA LTH, COLLABORATIVE MARKETING, AGRICULTURAL TAXES, AND NRCS SERVICES. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS HAD ACRES IMPACTED BY INTEGRATED CROP MANAGEMENT, REFLECTING A 56% INCREASE FROM THE PREVIOUS YEAR. A TOTAL OF 117 FARMERS REPORTED PROFIT GAINS, AND 650 PRODUCERS ADOPTED RECOMMENDED PRACTICES. FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS INCLUDED 400 WORKSHOPS FOCUSED ON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FINANCIAL LITERACY, S.T.R.E.A.M. (SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, READING, ENGINEERING, AGRICULTURE/ARTS, AND MATHEMATICS), AND THE AGRICULTURE INNOVATION SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM. STAFF MADE 43,896 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 4 ,360 HOURS AND REACHED 1,484,408 INDIVIDUALS INDIRECTLY OVER 2,004 HOURS. YOUTH PARTICIPATED IN HANDS-ON LEARNING SUCH AS CODING, DRONE EXPLORATION, AND ROBOTICS, FOSTERING LIFELONG SKILLS AND CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY. FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION INITIATIVES WERE DELIVERED THROUGH 1,181 WORKSHOPS PROMOTING HEALTHY BEHAVIORS AND DISEASE PREVENTION. STAFF COLLABORATED WITH ADVISORY COUNCILS AND STAKEHOLDERS TO DESIGN EVIDENCE-BASED NUTRITION EDUCATION PROGRAMS. THEY MADE 22,720 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 2,969 HOURS AND 332,595 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,289 HOURS. SIXTY-EIGHT PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS GAINED NUTRITION KNOWLEDGE. EIGHTEEN MEMORANDUMS OF UNDERSTANDING WERE SIGNED WITH EXTERNAL AGENCIES. STAFF EARNED SERVSAFE AND UTAH STATE NUTRITION CERTIFICATIONS. THE EFNEP EXPLORERS CAMP GRADUATED 636 YOUTH, ALL OF WHOM REPORTED INTENTIONS TO DRINK MORE WATER AND EAT MORE FRUITS. SC STATE'S RESEARCH PORTFOLIO INCLUDED SIX PROJECTS: AI AND DATA ANALYSIS, PHYTOCHEMICALS FROM SWEET POTATOES, FOOD HUB NETWORKS, AIENABLED SMART FARMING IOT SYSTEMS, POLYCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBONS IN SOIL, AND ATMO SPHERIC WATER GENERATION FROM RECYCLED PLASTICS. THREE PROJECTS CONCLUDED IN JULY 2025 AND PREPARED BULLETINS FOR PUBLICATION; THREE REMAIN ACTIVE. THESE RESEARCH EFFORTS HAVE BROAD IMPLICATIONS FOR SUSTAINABILITY, INNOVATION, AND AGRICULTURAL ADVANCEMENT. COLLECTIVELY, SC STATE UNIVERSITY'S PROGRAMS AND RESEARCH INITIATIVES DEMONSTRATE A STRONG COMMITMENT TO COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT, SCIENTIFIC PROGRESS, AND INCLUSIVE EDUCATION, SERVING AS A CATALYST FOR POSITIVE CHANGE ACROSS SOUTH CAROLINA.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: SINCE 1882, THE UNIVERSITY OF TENNESSEE AGRESEARCH (FORMERLY THE TENNESSEE AGRICULTURAL EXPERIMENT STATION) HAS SERVED AS THE BACKBONE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRI-FORESTRY ECONOMY THROUGH RESEARCH, INNOVATION, AND APPLICATION. AS THE STATE'S TOP INDUSTRY, AGRI-FORESTRY CONTRIBUTES $103 BILLION ANNUALLY TO TENNESSEE'S ECONOMY. WORKING IN TANDEM WITH UT EXTENSION, MORE THAN 850 AGRESEARCH FACULTY, STAFF, AND GRADUATE STUDENTS ADDRESS CHALLENGES FACING AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES THROUGH SOLUTION-ORIENTED RESEARCH AND DISSEMINATION OF KNOWLEDGE TO FARMERS, ALLIED INDUSTRIES, AND CONSUMERS. UT AGRESEARCH OPERATES TEN RESEARCH AND EDUCATION CENTERS (RECS) ACROSS TENNESSEE, COVERING DIVERSE GEOGRAPHIES, PRODUCTION SYSTEMS, CROPS, AND LIVESTOCK. THESE 40,000 ACRES OF FIELD LABORATORIES ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO CONDUCT TRIALS UNDER REAL WORLD CONDITIONS AND SERVE AS PLATFORMS FOR OUTREACH THROUGH ROUGHLY 20 FIELD DAYS EACH YEAR, ATTRACTING TENS OF THOUSANDS OF PARTICIPANTS FROM ACROSS THE STATE, REGION, AND BEYOND. THE RECS ALSO PROVIDE HANDS-ON LEARNING FOR COLLEGE STUDENTS AND YOUTH. AGRESEARCH ENCOMPASSES SEVEN ACADEMIC DEPARTMENTS AND ONE SCHOOL OF NATURAL RESOURCES, WITH ABOUT 150 FACULTY HOLDING RESEARCH APPOINTMENTS. GUIDED BY THE UT INSTITUTE OF AGRICULTURE'S A DECADE OF EXCELLENCE (2018-2028) AND GROWING GREATNESS (2025-2030) STRATEGIC PLANS, UT AGRESEARCH HAS ESTABLISHED THE AGRESEARCH STRATEGIC ACTION PLAN (ASAP) AS A ROADMAP TO MEET STATE AND NATIONAL NEEDS. ASAP FOCUSES ON BALANCING A PORTFOLIO OF INTEGRATED, INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAMS; CULTIVATING COLLABORATIVE TEAMS; ADVANCING INNOVATIVE CONCEPTS WITH STRUCTURED SUPPORT; AND SUSTAINING RESEARCH CAPACITY THROUGH TARGETED INVESTMENTS. FOUR THEMATIC INTEGRATED PROGRAMS ADDRESS COMPLEX, EMERGING CHALLENGES: UT ONE HEALTH INITIATIVE - A UT SYSTEM-WIDE PROGRAM HOUSED WITHIN AGRESEARCH THAT ADVANCES HUMAN, ANIMAL, PLANT, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH TO PROMOTE ECONOMIC GROWTH AND ECOSYSTEM RESILIENCE AT LOCAL, NATIONAL, AND GLOBAL LEVELS. PRECISION AND DIGITAL AGRICULTURE - A TRANSDISCIPLINARY EFFORT INCORPORATING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE, SENSING TECHNOLOGIES (E.G., DRONES AND ROBOTICS), AND DATA ANALYTICS TO ENHANCE PRODUCTION EFFICIENCY, CONSERVE FINITE NATURAL RESOURCES, REDUCE LABOR DEMANDS, AND IMPROVE PROFITABILITY. PROJECTS SPAN PLANT AND ANIMAL SYSTEMS, ENABLING PRODUCERS TO MAKE INFORMED, DATA DRIVEN DECISIONS. AGRICULTURAL GENOMICS AND SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY - A TRANSFORMATIONAL INITIATIVE ADVANCING PRODUCTIVITY AND RESILIENCE IN PLANTS AND ANIMALS THROUGH GENETIC INNOVATION. RESEARCH FOCUSES ON DISEASE AND DROUGHT RESISTANCE, WATER-USE EFFICIENCY, YIELD IMPROVEMENT, AND MITIGATION OF WILDLIFE DAMAGE, POSITIONING TENNESSEE AT THE FOREFRONT OF NEXT-GENERATION CROP AND LIVESTOCK DEVELOPMENT. BIOECONOMY ADVANCEMENT - AN INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAM CREATING SUSTAINABLE FUELS, CHEMICALS, AND MATERIALS FROM RENEWA BLE OR STRANDED BIOMASS RESOURCES. THE CIRCULAR BIOECONOMY SYSTEM (CBS), A CONVERGENT RESEARCH INITIATIVE BETWEEN UT/UTIA AND OAK RIDGE NATIONAL LABORATORY, DEVELOPS TECHNOLOGIES THAT CONVERT BIOMASS (E.G., MISCANTHUS GRASS) GROWN ON MARGINAL LANDS INTO MATERIALS FOR AUTOMOTIVE, PACKAGING, AND CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRIES-STIMULATING RURAL ECONOMIC GROWTH ACROSS TENNESSEE AND THE REGION. THROUGH THESE INTEGRATED INITIATIVES, UT AGRESEARCH ENHANCES THE SUSTAINABILITY, PROFITABILITY, AND RESILIENCE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRICULTURAL AND NATURAL RESOURCE SECTORS, ENSURING THAT RESEARCH-BASED INNOVATION CONTINUES TO DRIVE THE STATE'S ECONOMIC VITALITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP INTO THE FUTURE
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description: FIRE-WUI: COMMUNITY RESILIENCE ASSESSMENT FRAMEWORK FOR WILDFIRES USING MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS -WILDFIRES POSE A GROWING THREAT TO COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE US, PARTICULARLY IN AREAS WHERE HOMES AND NATURAL LANDSCAPES MEET, KNOWN AS THE WILDLAND-URBAN INTERFACE (WUI). EACH YEAR, MORE THAN 60,000 WILDFIRES OCCUR IN THE US, OFTEN RESULTING IN TRAGIC LOSS OF LIFE, DESTRUCTION OF HOMES, AND SEVERE ECONOMIC DISRUPTION. EFFECTIVE WILDFIRE RESPONSE AND PREVENTION EFFORTS ARE HINDERED BY A LACK OF DETAILED, UP-TO-DATE INFORMATION ABOUT VEGETATION AND BUILDINGS IN THESE HIGH-RISK AREAS. TO ADDRESS THESE CHALLENGES, THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A FRAMEWORK THAT CREATES DETAILED, 3D DIGITAL REPRESENTATIONS, REFERRED TO HERE AS DIGITAL TWINS, OF BOTH NATURAL AND BUILT ENVIRONMENTS. THESE DIGITAL TWINS WILL PROVIDE ACCURATE, LOCATION-SPECIFIC INFORMATION NEEDED TO SIMULATE HOW FIRES SPREAD AND ENABLE THE DEVELOPMENT OF MORE EFFECTIVE MITIGATION STRATEGIES. THE INTELLECTUAL MERIT OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDES THE CREATION OF A GROUNDBREAKING FRAMEWORK FOR GENERATING SCALABLE, MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS THAT COMBINE DATA FROM SATELLITES, DRONES, AND GROUND SENSORS. BY INTEGRATING AI AND ADVANCED MODELING TECHNIQUES, THE SYSTEM WILL ENHANCE THE UNDERSTANDING OF FIRE BEHAVIOR IN WUI ENVIRONMENTS AND SUPPORT MORE ACCURATE FIRE-SPREAD PREDICTIONS. THIS RESEARCH REPRESENTS A CONVERGENT AND INTERDISCIPLINARY APPROACH THAT COMBINES GEOSPATIAL SCIENCE, WILDFIRE MODELING, AND DATA ANALYTICS IN NOVEL WAYS STILL UNREALIZED AT THIS SCALE. THE BROADER IMPACTS OF THIS PROJECT ARE SIGNIFICANT. THE DIGITAL TWIN FRAMEWORK WILL HELP IMPROVE COMMUNITY RESILIENCE AND PREPAREDNESS AGAINST WILDFIRES BY PROVIDING FIRST RESPONDERS, PLANNERS, AND POLICYMAKERS WITH THE TOOLS TO MAKE FASTER, DATA-DRIVEN DECISIONS. THE SYSTEM IS DESIGNED TO SCALE NATIONWIDE AND CAN BE ADAPTED TO OTHER TYPES OF NATURAL DISASTERS. THE PROJECT WILL ALSO PREPARE THE NEXT GENERATION OF EXPERTS THROUGH INTERDISCIPLINARY EDUCATION AND TRAINING PROGRAMS, INCLUDING NEW COURSES, ONLINE LEARNING MODULES, MENTORSHIP, AND PROFESSIONAL TRAINING DEVELOPED IN COLLABORATION WITH INDUSTRY ORGANIZATIONS. THESE EFFORTS WILL ENSURE A MORE KNOWLEDGEABLE WORKFORCE READY TO CONFRONT THE COMPLEX CHALLENGES OF WILDFIRE MANAGEMENT. THE ?COMMUNITY RESILIENCE ASSESSMENT FRAMEWORK AGAINST WUI-FIRES USING MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS ? (CRAFT)? PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A SCALABLE, DYNAMIC DIGITAL TWIN FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT WILDFIRE MODELING AND MITIGATION IN THE WUI. CRAFT WILL INTEGRATE HETEROGENEOUS GEOSPATIAL DATA FROM SATELLITE, AERIAL, AND GROUND-BASED PLATFORMS USING AI-BASED SUPER-RESOLUTION, GENERATIVE MODELING, AND QA/QC PROTOCOLS TO CREATE MULTI-RESOLUTION, CONTINUOUSLY UPDATED 3D MODELS. THESE DIGITAL TWINS WILL FEED INTO ENHANCED FIRE-SPREAD MODELS TO IMPROVE PREDICTION ACCURACY AND INVESTIGATE THE TRADE-OFF AMONG RESOLUTION, PERFORMANCE, AND COMPUTATIONAL COMPLEXITY. THE IMPROVED MODELS ARE THEN USED TO EVALUATE MITIGATION STRATEGIES. VALIDATION WILL OCCUR THROUGH TESTBEDS WITH DISTINCT CHARACTERISTICS IN THE U.S. EASTERN AND WESTERN WUI REGIONS. EDUCATIONAL COMPONENTS WILL SUPPORT WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT IN GEOSPATIAL AND WILDFIRE SCIENCES. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2025. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS MANY OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN THE STATE'S 1890 AND 1862 LAND-GRANT INSTITUTIONS, FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY (FVSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA (UGA), THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDES CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS PLANT AND ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES (I.E. AI-POWERED DRONES AND AUTONOMOUS TRACTORS IN AGRICULTURE). USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA AND AMERICAN CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE RESEARCH PORTION OF THE PLAN OF WORK UNDERGOES SCIENTIFIC PEER REVIEW PRIOR TO EACH PROJECT BEING SUBMITTED. ALL SCIENTISTS ARE REQUIRED TO HAVE ACTIVE PROJECTS FOR EXPENDITURES TO BE MADE. EACH PROJECT IS PEER-REVIEWED BY BOTH INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL REVIEWERS AND MUST BE APPROVED BY THE APPROP RIATE DEAN AND DIRECTOR PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURE. CURRENT ANIMAL RELATED PROJECTS INVOLVE EFFICIENT ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND NUTRITION, ANIMAL HEALTH AND WELLBEING WITH INTERNAL PARASITE CONTROL USING SHEEP, GOATS, AND CATTLE AS MODELS. A NEW MULTISTATE EVANS-ALLEN PROJECT WAS INTRODUCED THIS YEAR ALONG WITH OTHER 1890 INSTITUTIONS UNDER THE TITLE ECOLOGICAL RESILIENCE AND EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS: IMPACTS ON FARMERS, LANDOWNERS AND COMMUNITIES. THE FOOD SAFETY PROGRAM EMPHASIZES NON-THERMAL METHODS TO CONTROL FOOD BORNE PATHOGENS IN MEAT AND NUTS. PLANT PRODUCTION PROGRAMS ENCOMPASS A BROAD ARRAY OF TARGETED FIELDS OF STUDY INCLUDING ENTOMOLOGY, PLANT PATHOLOGY, NEMATOLOGY, PLANT BREEDING AND GENETICS, SOIL SCIENCE, AND POSTHARVEST PHYSIOLOGY. SOME OF THESE PROJECTS ARE DONE IN COLLABORATION WITH ARS SCIENTISTS ON COMMODITY OF GREAT IMPORTANCE TO THE STATE OF GEORGIA. FUNDING FROM THE FEDERAL ALLOCATION AND THE CORRESPONDING MATCHING FUND FROM THE STATE ARE USED TO COVER EXPENSES RELATED TO THESE ACTIVITIES. ADDITIONAL FUNDS COME FROM EXTERNAL GRANTS SECURED BY THE RESEARCH SCIENTISTS. APPLIED RESEARCH PROJECTS USING THESE FUNDS TARGET MAINLY LIMITED-RESOURCE PRODUCERS IN THE STATE BUT SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES IMPACT THE CITIZEN OF STATE OF GEORGIA, THE COUNTRY, AND THE WORLD.
awards.Awarding Sub Agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

awards.Description:
NEW COMPETITIVE COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WITH NORTHEASTERN UNIVERSITY UNDER FOA NUMBER DE-FOA-00002459 (OPEN 2021) AND CONTROL NUMBER 2459-4936.
PROJECT TITLE: HIGH-PERFORMANCE AND MINIATURIZED GREENHOUSE GAS SENSOR FOR DRONE-BASED REMOTE SENSING.
IN THIS PROJECT, WE PROPOSE TO SIMULTANEOUSLY ADDRESS THE CHALLENGES ASSOCIATED WITH SWAP AND SPEED BY DEVELOPING A NEW CLASS OF HIGH-PERFORMANCE AND MINIATURE N2O SENSORS (TABLE 2) BASED ON LASER ABSORPTION SPECTROMETRY (LAS) AND ADVANCED MEMS RESONANT IR DETECTORS WITH ULTRA-HIGH RESOLUTION AND FAST DETECTION SPEED.

awards.Recipient Name: REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF MINNESOTA
awards.Description: COLLABORATIVE RESEARCH: FRAMEWORKS: BUILDING THE TWENTY-FIRST CENTURY CITIZEN SCIENCE FRAMEWORK TO ENABLE SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERY ACROSS DISCIPLINES -TWENTY-FIRST CENTURY RESEARCH IS ENABLED BY THE AVAILABILITY OF VAST AMOUNTS OF DATA, COLLECTED ACROSS A WIDE RANGE OF TEMPORAL AND SPATIAL SCALES, OFTEN IN REAL TIME. ENTIRE FLEETS OF SATELLITES, DRONES AND OTHER DEVICES MONITOR THE EARTH AT EVER-INCREASING RESOLUTION, ADDING TO THE ENORMOUS CORPUS OF MAPS AND METADATA THAT ENABLE SCIENCES FROM GEOLOGY TO BIODIVERSITY. WHILE CITIZEN SCIENCE - OR CROWDSOURCING SCIENCE - HAS BEEN SUCCESSFULLY LEVERAGED OVER THE PAST SEVERAL DECADES TO CLOSE THE ANALYSIS GAP ARISING FROM LARGE AMOUNTS OF DATA, THE SHEER SCALE AND COMPLEXITY OF THESE NEW DATA SETS PRESENTS NEW CHALLENGES. THIS PROJECT ADDRESSES THESE CHALLENGES THROUGH NOVEL CITIZEN SCIENCE CYBERINFRASTRUCTURE (CSCI) WHERE NEW TOOLS AND TECHNIQUES, INCLUDING TEAMING HUMANS WITH ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE (AI), ARE BEING DEVELOPED TO ENABLE RESEARCHERS TO EFFICIENTLY EXTRACT THE BEST RESULTS FROM LARGE AND COMPLEX DATA SETS. THIS EFFORT INCORPORATES MAPPING, MACHINE LEARNING, AND DATA SHARING INNOVATIONS IN BIODIVERSITY, GEOSCIENCE, AND ASTRONOMY RESEARCH AND EXPANDS THE CAPACITY OF RESEARCH COMMUNITIES ACROSS A WIDE RANGE OF DISCIPLINES TO USE CITIZEN SCIENCE AS A SUITABLE, OPEN AND SUSTAINABLE RESEARCH METHODOLOGY. THIS PROJECT LEVERAGES NSF-SUPPORTED CYBERINFRASTRUCTURE, INCLUDING THE ZOONIVERSE CITIZEN SCIENCE PLATFORM WITH ITS NEARLY THREE MILLION VOLUNTEERS, TO PROVIDE A NOVEL CYBERINFRASTRUCTURE BY: (1) INTEGRATING MAPPING INFRASTRUCTURE INTO ZOONIVERSE TO ACCELERATE ACCURATE PROCESSING AND CURATION OF OFTEN COMPLEX DATA SETS FOR GEOSCIENCE AND BIODIVERSITY PROJECTS; (2) PROVIDING AN INCUBATOR HUB FOR RESEARCHERS AND DEVELOPERS TO DESIGN AND DEPLOY INNOVATIVE CITIZEN SCIENCE PROJECTS WITH A FAST PRODUCTION TURN-AROUND PARTICULARLY FOR AI TRAINING; AND (3) PROVIDING CYBER-PATHWAYS TO EXISTING CYBERINFRASTRUCTURE, CREATING DOCUMENTATION FOR ZOONIVERSE PROJECTS TO RESPONSIBLY DEPOSIT THEIR DATA INTO APPROPRIATE REPOSITORIES, AND FACILITATING WORKSHOPS FOR ZOONIVERSE PROJECT TEAMS TO DEVELOP BEST PRACTICES FOR DATA AND MODEL SHARING. TO ENSURE WIDE DISSEMINATION OF THE NEW CSCI, A STRONG COMMUNITY OF PRACTICE IS ENGAGED AND SUPPORTED THROUGHOUT THE PROJECT EFFORT. THE PROJECT IS LED BY THE UNIVERSITY OF MINNESOTA IN COLLABORATION WITH THE ADLER PLANETARIUM AND THE UNIVERSITY OF OXFORD, AS CORE ZOONIVERSE INSTITUTIONS AND ASTROPHYSICS EXPERTISE, AND JOINED BY THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA AND THE FLORIDA MUSEUM OF NATURAL HISTORY WITH EXPERTISE IN BIODIVERSITY, MUSEUM SPECIMEN COLLECTIONS, AND DATA REPOSITORIES, AS WELL AS NORTHERN ARIZONA UNIVERSITY WITH EXPERTISE IN EARTH SCIENCES, MAPPING CYBERINFRASTRUCTURE, AND THE STUDY OF THE ANTARCTICA?S DRY VALLEYS. AS THE LARGEST CITIZEN SCIENCE PLATFORM, AND WITH KEY TOOLS FOR HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP DATA ANALYSIS TASKS, THE ZOONIVERSE IS UNIQUELY POSITIONED TO DEVELOP SKILLS AND BEST PRACTICES TO LOWER THE BARRIER FOR RESEARCH TEAMS TO RESPONSIBLY SHARE DATA AND MODELS. THIS IS CARRIED OUT THROUGH FACT-FINDING AND DEMONSTRATION WITH SEVERAL KEY GROUPS WHO ARE EXEMPLARS IN THE DATA AND MODEL SHARING ECOSYSTEM, INCLUDING GITHUB, THE NATIONAL SCIENCE DATA FABRIC (NSDF), THE GLOBAL BIODIVERSITY INFORMATION FACILITY (GBIF), AND OPEN SCIENCE CHAIN. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

DESCRIPTION:EPA'S CERCLA SECTION 128(A) GRANT PROGRAM FUNDS ACTIVITIES THAT ESTABLISH OR ENHANCE THE CAPACITY OF STATE AND TRIBAL RESPONSE PROGRAMS. THE GOALS OF THIS FUNDING ARE TO PROVIDE FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR THE ELEMENTS OF AN EFFECTIVE STATE OR TRIBAL RESPONSE PROGRAM, AS SPECIFIED IN CERCLA SECTION 128, AND TO ENSURE THAT STATES AND TRIBES MAINTAIN A PUBLIC RECORD OF SITES INCLUDED IN THEIR PROGRAMS. THE INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTMENT AND JOBS ACT ('IIJA') PROVIDED ADDITIONAL FUNDING TO CARRY OUT THE SECTION 128(A) GRANT PROGRAM. THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO ENHANCE THE CAPACITY OF YUROK TRIBE'S (YUROK) BROWNFIELDS RESPONSE PROGRAM TO MEET THE SECTION 128(A) ELEMENTS. YUROK WILL OVERSEE AND PERFORM PLANNING, ASSESSMENT, AND CLEANUP OF BROWNFIELDS SITES THROUGHOUT THEIR TRIBAL LANDS.  THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FULL FEDERAL FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $574,504.  PREAWARD COSTS HAVE BEEN APPROVED BACK TO 10/1/22.ACTIVITIES:YUROK WILL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES:  TIMELY SURVEY AND INVENTORY OF BROWNFIELD SITES, WHICH ARE PROPERTIES WHOSE EXPANSION, REDEVELOPMENT, OR REUSE MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OF HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES. IT ALSO INCLUDES OVERSIGHT AND ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITIES TO ENSURE THAT RESPONSE ACTIONS PROTECT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT; RESOURCES TO PROVIDE MEANINGFUL PUBLIC INVOLVEMENT; MECHANISMS FOR APPROVAL OF CLEANUP PLANS, AND VERIFICATION OF COMPLETE RESPONSES. THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO ASSIST IN REMEDIATING AND REUSING BROWNFIELDS SITES THROUGHOUT THE STATE. ADDITIONALLY, YUROK WILL IDENTIFY AND COMPLETE AT LEAST ONE TECHNICAL TRAINING ON ENVIRONMENTAL CRIMES, INVESTIGATION AND ENFORCEMENT. SPECIFIC FOCUS WILL BE ON ILLEGAL DUMP SITES AND ILLEGAL MARIJUANA CULTIVATION SITES IDENTIFIED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT AS POTENTIALLY CONTAMINATED AS A RESULT OF ILLEGAL ACTIVITIES, TRESPASSING, PESTICIDE AND RODENTICIDE USE, HAZARDOUS AND SOLID WASTE.
SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:YUROK WILL EXPAND PUBLIC EDUCATION AND OUTREACH OF THE YUROK VOLUNTARY CLEANUP PROGRAM TO UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES, ASSESS MULTIPLE SITES THROUGH THE STATE TARGETED BROWNFIELD ASSESSMENT PROGRAM, OVERSEE THE CLEANUP OF MULTIPLE SITES, INCLUDING UP TO FOUR PHASE I'S AND TWO PHASE II'S. ADDITIONALLY, YUROK WILL HIRE AN ENVIRONMENTAL SPECIALIST TO DEVELOP A DATABASE OF ILLEGAL CANNABIS CULTIVATION SITES AND PRIORITIZATION LIST AND COORDINATE WITH THE YUROK FISHERIES DEPARTMENT FOR LIGHT DETECTION AND RANGING (LIDAR) DRONE SUPPORT.

DEMONSTRATION OF RAPID DEPLOYMENT OF OVERHEAD MONITORING SENSORS IN COMBINATION WITH WEATHER-BASED DYNAMIC LINE RATINGS SYSTEM

THE DEMONSTRATION OF RAPID DEPLOYMENT OF OVERHEAD MONITORING SENSORS IN COMBINATION WITH WEATHER-BASED DYNAMIC LINE RATINGS SYSTEM PROJECT WILL IMPLEMENT, TEST, AND OPTIMIZE A WEATHER-BASED DYNAMIC LINE RATING (DLR) SYSTEM ON A SECTION OF IDAHO POWER TRANSMISSION NETWORK. THE PROJECT WILL UTILIZE DRONE-INSTALLED WEATHER SENSORS AND WEATHER-BASED AMPACITY PREDICTION SOFTWARE.

awards.Recipient Name: SOUTHEAST COASTAL OCEAN OBSERVING REGIONAL ASSOCIATION
awards.Description: PRIORITIES FOR SECOORA FOR THE NEXT THREE YEARS ARE TO SUSTAIN, REPAIR AND EXPAND CRITICAL OBSERVING INFRASTRUCTURE, AND ENHANCE DATA MANAGEMENT, DATA SHARING, AND DELIVERY OF DATA PRODUCTS TO STAKEHOLDERS THROUGHOUT THE SOUTHEAST. PROJECTS UNDER TOPIC 1 INCLUDE: INSTALL AND MAINTAIN WATER LEVEL SENSORS TO HELP FILL GAPS IN WATER LEVEL DATA NEEDED FOR DECISION MAKING; MAINTAINING OCEAN ACIDIFICATION SENSOR IN FLORIDA KEYS; MAINTAIN WAVE BUOY INSTALLATION; COLLECT SUBSURFACE OCEAN TEMPERATURE DATA TO MONITOR SEA SURFACE TEMPERATURE CHANGE; AND RECAPITALIZE GLIDERS, HFRS, AND SOUNDSCAPE MONITORING. PROJECTS UNDER TOPIC 2 INCLUDE: CREATE A REGIONAL OCEAN DATA SHARING WEBSITE; UTILIZE GLIDERS FOR PASSIVE ACOUSTIC MONITORING OF NORTH ATLANTIC RIGHT WHALES; CREATE A SOUTHEAST MARINE MAPPING TOOL AND CONDUCT TRAINING; SUPPORT SURFACE ELEVATION TABLE MONITORING DATA COLLECTION AND DATA SHARING; AND SUPPORT RESEARCHERS TO ATTEND DRONE COURSE IN BROADER SUPPORT OF DRONES IN THE COASTAL ZONE COMMUNITY OF PRACTICE.

SCC-IRG TRACK 1: COMMUNITY-DRIVEN DESIGN OF FAIR, URBAN AIR MOBILITY TRANSPORTATION MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS -URBAN AIR MOBILITY (UAM) ENVISIONS INTEGRATING THE SKYSCAPE INTO THE TRANSPORTATION NETWORK AND ENCOMPASSES SERVICES SUCH AS DELIVERY DRONES, ON-DEMAND SHARED MOBILITY BY VERTICAL-TAKE OFF AND LANDING (VTOL) AIRCRAFT FOR INTRA-CITY PASSENGER TRIPS, AND, IN THE LONGER RUN, ELECTRIC AND AUTONOMOUS VTOLS. THIS POSSIBLE MODAL ALTERNATIVE PROVIDES A SAFE, RELIABLE, AND ENVIRONMENTALLY SOUND OPTION TO REDUCE SURFACE-LEVEL CONGESTION. NEVERTHELESS, THE HISTORY OF TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT SHOWS THAT IT IS IMPERATIVE TO DESIGN TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURES WITH THE COMMUNITY TO FIND THE BEST BALANCE BETWEEN THESE SOCIOTECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS. MUCH RESEARCH SHOWS THAT THE DESIGN OF TRANSPORTATION SYSTEMS HAS A LONG-LASTING, OFTEN DISCRIMINATORY EFFECT THAT REINFORCES EXISTING SOCIO-ECONOMIC INEQUALITY. AS UAM IS BEING DEVELOPED AS A NEW TRANSPORTATION MODE, WE ARE AT AN OPPORTUNE MOMENT TO DESIGN ITS INFRASTRUCTURE TO PROVIDE EFFECTIVE AND EQUITABLE AIR MOBILITY FOR ALL, AVOIDING OUR PAST MISTAKES. THIS PROJECT WILL FOCUS ON UNDERSTANDING THE PREFERENCES, ATTITUDES, AND CONCERNS OF ALL STAKEHOLDERS OF UAM, INCLUDING THE POTENTIAL USERS OF UAM, THE GENERAL PUBLIC IN DIFFERENT COMMUNITIES WHO MAY BE POSITIVELY AND/OR ADVERSELY AFFECTED BY UAM, POLICYMAKERS, AND CITY PLANNERS. THE KNOWLEDGE ELICITED FROM THE STAKEHOLDERS WILL GUIDE THE DESIGN OF AN OPEN-SOURCE COMPUTER AIDED PLANNING TOOL THAT POLICY-MAKERS AND URBAN PLANNERS CAN USE TO DESIGN UAM INFRASTRUCTURE THAT ACCOMMODATES COMMUNITIES? PRIORITIES AND ENABLES TRANSPORTATION EQUITY. WHILE THE TIMELINE FOR UAM MAY BE IN THE FUTURE, ITS DEPLOYMENT MAY ENTAIL SIGNIFICANT FUTURE INVESTMENT IN INFRASTRUCTURE WHICH MAKES INCLUSION OF EQUITY CONSIDERATIONS AND EARLY COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT CRITICAL. WE PROPOSE A ''COMMUNITY-IN-THE-LOOP INTEGRATIVE FRAMEWORK FOR FAIR AND EQUITABLE URBAN AIR MOBILITY (UAM) INFRASTRUCTURE DESIGN''. OUR INTEGRATIVE FRAMEWORK WILL DEVELOP METHODS TO ENGAGE WITH KEY STAKEHOLDERS TO ADDRESS SIGNIFICANT SOCIO-TECHNICAL CHALLENGES, INCLUDING (A) UNDERSTANDING THE COMMUNITY PREFERENCES AND DESIDERATA IN TERMS OF NECESSARY CONSIDERATIONS FOR EQUITABLE MOBILITY, (B) DEVELOPING NOVEL MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUES TO GENERATE DESIGN OPTIONS THAT OPTIMIZE FOR COMMUNITY DESIDERATA EFFICIENTLY AND (C) DEVISING COMMUNITY-DRIVEN EVALUATIVE MEASURES AND TRADE-OFF DECISION MECHANISMS. WE ADDRESS THESE CHALLENGES BY DRAWING FROM URBAN AND TRANSPORTATION ENGINEERING, AEROSPACE, AND COMPUTER AND INFORMATION SCIENCES. THE FINAL PRODUCT OF OUR FRAMEWORK IS AN OPEN-SOURCE COMPUTER AIDED PLANNING TOOL CALLED VERTICAP. VERTICAP WILL BE EQUIPPED WITH NOVEL MACHINE LEARNING-BASED ALGORITHMS TO NAVIGATE COMPLEX DESIGN SPACE OPTIONS, INCLUDING LONG-TERM DECISIONS (I.E., ALLOCATION OF UAM AIRPORTS, ALSO KNOWN AS VERTIPORTS), MEDIUM-TERM DECISIONS (I.E., DESIGN OF AIR SPACE), AND SHORT-TERM DECISIONS (I.E., AIR-TRAFFIC CONTROL). WE WILL ESTABLISH A ''COMMUNITY COUNCIL'' REPRESENTING DIFFERENT STAKEHOLDERS. THROUGH CONTINUOUS INTERACTIONS WITH THE COMMUNITY COUNCIL, WE WILL EVALUATE AND DEMONSTRATE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE DEVELOPED VERTICAP TOOL IN THE CITY OF AUSTIN, TX AND SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.

ICE-TI: THOKATAKIYA (TOWARDS THE FUTURE) -A GOAL OF THE TRIBAL COLLEGES AND UNIVERSITIES PROGRAM (TCUP) IS TO INCREASE THE SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, ENGINEERING, AND MATHEMATICS (STEM) INSTRUCTIONAL AND RESEARCH CAPACITIES OF SPECIFIC INSTITUTIONS OF HIGHER EDUCATION THAT SERVE THE NATION'S INDIGENOUS STUDENTS. EXPANDING THE STEM CURRICULAR OFFERINGS AT THESE INSTITUTIONS EXPANDS THE OPPORTUNITIES FOR THEIR STUDENTS TO PURSUE CHALLENGING, REWARDING CAREERS IN STEM FIELDS, PROVIDES FOR RESEARCH STUDIES IN AREAS THAT MAY BE CULTURALLY SIGNIFICANT, AND ENCOURAGES A COMMUNITY AND GENERATIONAL APPRECIATION FOR SCIENCE AND MATHEMATICS EDUCATION. THIS PROJECT ALIGNS DIRECTLY WITH THAT GOAL, AND MOREOVER SEEKS TO INCREASE THE REGIONAL CAPACITY FOR DATA SCIENCE THROUGH THE DEVELOPMENT OF INNOVATIVE CURRICULUM AND SERVICE-BASED EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING, PROVIDING CONTEXT FOR SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING EDUCATION THAT IS FOCUSED ON ACTIVE AND COLLABORATIVE LEARNING. THE PROJECT AIMS TO BUILD AN ENVIRONMENTAL DATA SCIENCE STEM EDUCATION MODEL THROUGH THE INCORPORATION OF TRADITIONAL ECOLOGICAL KNOWLEDGE TO ADDRESS COMPLEX ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING QUESTIONS. THE T?OK?TAKIYA (?TOWARDS THE FUTURE?) PROJECT AIMS TO STRENGTHEN ENVIRONMENTAL DATA SCIENCE EDUCATION AND UNDERGRADUATE RESEARCH AT OGLALA LAKOTA COLLEGE. A RESEARCH FOCUS ON ADVANCING UNDERSTANDING OF GROUNDWATER AND SURFACE WATER FLOW IN THE REGION PROVIDES A BASIS FOR FACULTY AND STUDENT RESEARCH EXPERIENCES AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT. USING ADVANCED SENSING TOOLS, SUCH AS AIRBORNE AND DRONE-ACQUIRED LIDAR AND MULTISPECTRAL IMAGERY, THE PROJECT AIMS TO CHARACTERIZE AND UNDERSTAND THE GEOMETRY, SCALE, AND EFFECTS OF STRUCTURAL FEATURES IN COMPLEX HYDROLOGIC SYSTEMS. CURRICULUM DESIGNED AROUND THESE THEMES AND FOUNDED ON LAKOTA WAYS OF KNOWING AIMS TO TRAIN FUTURE LEADERS IN THE FIELD OF DATA SCIENCE. CORE COMPETENCIES WILL BE BUILT IN COURSES IN ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE AND PRE-ENGINEERING DEGREE PROGRAMS. COMMUNITY PROGRAMMING EMPHASIZES FOOD SOVEREIGNTY, AQUIFER HEALTH, AND DEVELOPING A SELF-PACED GIS CERTIFICATE PROGRAM FOR THE REGIONAL STEM WORKFORCE. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.

ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE FOR ARID LAND AGRICULTURE (AIALA) -THIS AWARD IS FUNDED IN WHOLE OR IN PART UNDER THE AMERICAN RESCUE PLAN ACT OF 2021 (PUBLIC LAW 117-2). GLOBAL FOOD SUPPLY AND FOOD SECURITY ARE AT RISK DUE TO AN INCREASING WORLD POPULATION, CLIMATE CHANGE, DIMINISHING NATURAL RESOURCES, AND LIMITED AVAILABLE LAND. IN AGRICULTURE, THE PRIMARY CHALLENGE HAS BEEN HOW TO BE MORE PRODUCTIVE WITH LESS - LESS ARABLE LAND, LESS WATER, LESS LABOR, LESS CERTAINTY. IN ARID LANDS, THESE CHALLENGES ARE AMPLIFIED. AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS STRUGGLE TO COPE WITH RAPID CHANGES IN WATER AVAILABILITY AND LAND-USE PATTERNS, SCARCITY OF LABOR DUE TO DECLINING POPULATION, VARIABILITY AND UNCERTAINTY RELATED TO CHANGING WEATHER AND CLIMATE, AND AGING RURAL INFRASTRUCTURES. ARID LANDS AND DRYLANDS, WHICH COVER MUCH OF THE WESTERN US, ARE EXPECTED TO EXPAND AS THE CLIMATE CHANGES. ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE (AI) CAN BRING A PARADIGM SHIFT IN HOW THE TWIN ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL CHALLENGES OF FARMING AND RANCHING IN ARID LANDS CAN BE ADDRESSED. AI CAN SUPPORT FARMERS TO OPERATE WITH GREATER EFFICIENCY AND PRECISION THROUGH THE ASSISTANCE OF AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS (E.G., DRONES, GROUND VEHICLES, AND INTELLIGENT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS) AND THE SUPPORT OF INTELLIGENT SOFTWARE SYSTEMS TO AID IN DECISION MAKING (E.G., DETECTING AND RESOLVING CROP DISEASES). AI-DRIVEN SOLUTIONS WILL NOT ONLY ENABLE FARMERS TO DO MORE WITH LESS; THEY WILL ALSO IMPROVE QUALITY AND ENSURE A FASTER PATH-TO-MARKET FOR CROPS AND LIVESTOCK. THIS NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION RESEARCH TRAINEESHIP (NRT) AWARD TO NEW MEXICO STATE UNIVERSITY (NMSU) WILL ENABLE THE CREATION OF A COORDINATED GRADUATE TRAINING PROGRAM, CALLED ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE FOR ARID LAND AGRICULTURE (AIALA), TO PREPARE THE NEXT GENERATION OF SCHOLARS AND PRACTITIONERS BY TEACHING GRADUATE STUDENTS HOW TO BRIDGE THE DIVIDES BETWEEN AI AND AGRICULTURE FOR ARID LANDS. THE PROJECT ANTICIPATES TRAINING 33 MS AND PH.D. STUDENTS, INCLUDING 18 FUNDED TRAINEES, FROM COMPUTING-RELATED DISCIPLINES AND AGRICULTURE-RELATED DISCIPLINES . THE AIALA SCHOLAR EXPERIENCE WILL INTEGRATE WITH AND COMPLEMENT THE TRADITIONAL GRADUATE DISCIPLINARY TRAINING, THUS CONTEXTUALIZING THE IN-DEPTH DISCIPLINARY RESEARCH FOR RESEARCHERS IN EITHER AI OR AGRICULTURE-RELATED AREAS. MOREOVER, THE EXPERIENCE WILL ALLOW SCHOLARS TO EFFECTIVELY SERVE AS CATALYSTS IN RESEARCH TEAMS USING AI TO SOLVE ARID LAND CHALLENGES. THE RESEARCH CONDUCTED BY THE AIALA SCHOLARS AND THEIR RESEARCH MENTORS WILL ADVANCE THE STATE OF THE ART IN BOTH AI AND ARID LAND AGRICULTURE. THE RESEARCH WILL PROMOTE THE CREATION OF NOVEL MULTI-AGENT SYSTEMS FRAMEWORKS, ADVANCING THE STATE OF THE ART IN MACHINE LEARNING AND DISTRIBUTED DATA ANALYTICS. IN ADDITION, IT WILL PROVIDE METHODOLOGIES AND TECHNOLOGIES TO ENHANCE THE ADAPTABILITY OF CROPS, RANGELAND PLANTS AND LIVESTOCK, IMPROVE THE RESILIENCY OF LIVESTOCK IN EXPANSIVE RUGGED RANGELANDS, AND ULTIMATELY LEAD TO RESILIENT AND SUSTAINABLE ARID LAND AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS. THE AIALA TRAINING MODEL BENEFITS FROM A NUMBER OF INNOVATIONS. FIRST, IT ESTABLISHES A TRANSDISCIPLINARY TRAINING PIPELINE, EMBEDDING AI RESEARCH CHALLENGES IN ARID LAND AGRICULTURAL CHALLENGES, ENABLING CONTEXTUALIZED AND SITUATED LEARNING. SECOND, IT INTEGRATES GRADUATE STUDENTS AND FACULTY MENTORS IN MUTUALLY SUPPORTIVE TEAMS OF LEARNERS, SUPPORTED, IN TURN, BY AN EXTENSIVE MENTORING INFRASTRUCTURE. THIRD, IT INFUSES DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION IN ALL OPERATIONS AND LEARNING ACTIVITIES, PROMOTING ENGAGEMENT OF A DIVERSE AUDIENCE OF SCHOLARS AND PREPARING THE SCHOLARS TO SERVE AS AGENTS OF CHANGE FOR INCLUSION. FINALLY, IT EMPHASIZES THE DEVELOPMENT OF PROFESSIONAL SKILLS AS PART OF HOLISTIC DISCIPLINARY TRAINING. THE NSF RESEARCH TRAINEESHIP (NRT) PROGRAM IS DESIGNED TO ENCOURAGE THE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF BOLD, NEW POTENTIALLY TRANSFORMATIVE MODELS FOR STEM GRADUATE EDUCATION TRAINING. THE PROGRAM IS DEDICATED TO EFFECTIVE TRAINING OF STEM GRADUATE STUDENTS IN HIGH PRIORITY INTERDISCIPLINARY OR CONVERGENT RESEARCH AREAS THROUGH COMPREHENSIVE TRAINEESHIP MODELS THAT ARE INNOVATIVE, EVIDENCE-BASED, AND ALIGNED WITH CHANGING WORKFORCE AND RESEARCH NEEDS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.

NRT: COMMUNITY RESILIENCE TO CASCADING IMPACTS OF EXTREME EVENTS AND GEOHAZARDS ACROSS SCALES THROUGH ENGAGED SCHOLARSHIP AND LEARNING -MANY CLIMATE-RELATED NATURAL HAZARDS SUCH AS FLOODS, DROUGHTS, AND WILDFIRES ARE INCREASING IN FREQUENCY AROUND THE GLOBE, DISRUPTING CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE AND DIMINISHING HUMAN WELL-BEING. THESE PROBLEMS ARE EXACERBATED BY SOCIAL AND TECHNOLOGICAL FACTORS INCLUDING POOR LAND USE PLANNING, AGING INFRASTRUCTURE, AND LOOSE SOCIAL COHESION. COMBINING THESE HAZARDS WITH EACH OTHER OR WITH GEOLOGIC RISKS SUCH AS EARTHQUAKES AND VOLCANIC ERUPTIONS CAN MULTIPLY THEIR DEVASTATING IMPACTS ON SOCIETY. RECENT EXAMPLES IN THE WESTERN UNITED STATES INCLUDE PROLONGED DROUGHTS FOLLOWED BY MEGA WILDFIRES AND EXTREME FLOODING. UNDERSTANDING CAUSES, CONSEQUENCES, AND HUMAN RESPONSES TO THESE COMPOUND HAZARDS REQUIRES VERSATILE SKILLS RANGING FROM COMMUNICATION, COMPUTATIONAL, AND SYSTEMS THINKING TO TRANSLATE KNOWLEDGE TO ACTION. THIS NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION RESEARCH TRAINEESHIP (NRT) AWARD TO PORTLAND STATE UNIVERSITY (PSU) WILL DEVELOP A TRANSFORMATIVE GRADUATE TRAINING PROGRAM FOR ADDRESSING AND IDENTIFYING SOLUTIONS FOR CASCADING HAZARDS. THE PROJECT WILL BUILD ON COMMUNITY-ENGAGED TRANSDISCIPLINARY COLLABORATION AMONG DIVERSE FACULTY FROM DEPARTMENTS ACROSS THREE COLLEGES: ATMOSPHERIC SCIENCE, COMPUTER SCIENCE, GEOTECHNICAL ENGINEERING, GEOSCIENCES, URBAN STUDIES AND PLANNING, AND ENVIRONMENTAL SOCIAL SCIENCES. THE PROJECT ANTICIPATES TRAINING 22 NRT-FUNDED TRAINEES AND 100 ADDITIONAL STUDENTS PARTICIPATING IN AT LEAST ONE ELEMENT OF THE PROGRAM. THIS NRT PROGRAM WILL PRODUCE A HIGHLY COMPETENT AND RESPONSIVE WORKFORCE WITH BROAD TECHNOLOGICAL, SCIENTIFIC, AND SOCIAL SKILLS NECESSARY TO ADDRESS CHALLENGES. THIS PROJECT PROPOSES TO DEVELOP A TRANSDISCIPLINARY TRAINING PROGRAM THAT USES A SOCIAL-ECOLOGICAL-TECHNOLOGICAL SYSTEMS (SETS) FRAMEWORK WHILE ADDRESSING ETHICS AND RISK COMMUNICATION ISSUES AS PART OF LEARNING. THE PRIMARY GOAL IS TO DEVELOP RESPONSIVE CURRICULA THAT ALLOW FLEXIBLE PATHWAYS FOR THE NEXT GENERATION OF SCIENTISTS, MANAGERS, AND PRACTITIONERS IN THE FIELD OF DISASTER MANAGEMENT AND COMMUNITY RESILIENCE BY ACTIVELY ENGAGING WITH DIVERSE STAKEHOLDERS FROM CITY PRACTITIONERS, FEDERAL AGENCIES, CIVIC ORGANIZATIONS, AND PRIVATE INDUSTRIES USING A COLLABORATIVE GOVERNANCE MODEL. USING PSU AS AN INITIAL TESTBED FOR BUILDING COMMUNITY RESILIENCE, NRT STUDENTS WILL EXTEND THE GAINED UNDERSTANDINGS AND BEST PRACTICES TO HELP URBAN AND RURAL COMMUNITIES BY ENGAGING WITH DIVERSE COMMUNITY PARTNERS AND BEING CO-MENTORED BY A GROUP OF PARTICIPATING FACULTY WITH VARIOUS EXPERTISE. POTENTIAL RESEARCH TOPICS INCLUDE 1) IDENTIFYING CHALLENGES, OPPORTUNITIES, AND SOLUTIONS FOR IMPLEMENTING COMMUNITY RESILIENCE PLANNING AT PSU CAMPUS AND EXTEND ACROSS SECTORS AND SCALES, 2) UNDERSTANDING AND MODELING CASCADING EFFECTS OF CLIMATIC AND OTHER GEOHAZARDS AND IDENTIFYING POTENTIAL SETS SOLUTIONS IN URBAN COMMUNITIES, AND 3) USING DRONE TECHNOLOGY, SCIENCE COMMUNICATION, AND CLIMATE INFORMATION TO IMPROVE UNDERSTANDING OF AND SOLVING HEAT AND WILDFIRE-RELATED RISKS IN RURAL COMMUNITIES, WITH AN EYE TO AND SCALING THOSE UP TO ENGAGE OTHER COMMUNITIES ELSEWHERE. THE NSF RESEARCH TRAINEESHIP (NRT) PROGRAM IS DESIGNED TO ENCOURAGE THE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF BOLD, NEW POTENTIALLY TRANSFORMATIVE MODELS FOR STEM GRADUATE EDUCATION TRAINING. THE PROGRAM IS DEDICATED TO EFFECTIVE TRAINING OF STEM GRADUATE STUDENTS IN HIGH PRIORITY INTERDISCIPLINARY OR CONVERGENT RESEARCH AREAS THROUGH COMPREHENSIVE TRAINEESHIP MODELS THAT ARE INNOVATIVE, EVIDENCE-BASED, AND ALIGNED WITH CHANGING WORKFORCE AND RESEARCH NEEDS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

THIS ACTION APPROVES AN AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,979,615 TO SUPPORT THE MASSACHUSETTS DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION SMART GRANT PROGRAM. THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO PROVIDE USE OF DRONE TECHNOLOGY FOR ENVIRONMENTAL SENSING IN CAPE COD, MA. THE ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED ARE: IMPROVING RAIL SAFETY, EFFICIENCY, AND CLIMATE RESILIENCE BY USING DRONES AND SENSORS TO MONITOR AND ANALYZE RAILROAD INFRASTRUCTURE THREATENED BY GROUND WATER VARIABILITY. THE INTENDED BENEFICIARIES WILL BE THE STATE OF MASSACHUSETTS. THERE ARE NO SUBAWARD ACTIVITIES IN THIS PROJECT.

PROJECT PURPOSE: THE RED LAKE NATION COLLEGE (RLNC) CONNECTING MINORITY COMMUNITIES PROJECT AIMS TO UPGRADE BROADBAND ACCESS, CAPACITY, AND SKILLS FOR RLNC STUDENTS, STAFF, AND COMMUNITY MEMBERS. THE PROJECT HAS THREE MAIN GOALS: (1) SUPPORT AND EXPAND RLNC BROADBAND CAPACITY AND DIGITAL HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE TO BETTER FACILITATE EDUCATIONAL INSTRUCTION AND LEARNING; (2) PROVIDE BROADBAND ACCESS AND DIGITAL DEVICES TO RLNC STUDENTS TO BETTER FACILITATE LEARNING; AND (3) ENHANCE THE DIGITAL AND CYBERSECURITY SKILLS AND CAPACITY OF RLNC IT STAFF, FACULTY, STUDENTS, AND COMMUNITY MEMBERS.ACTIVITIES:THE RED LAKE NATION COLLEGE (RLNC) PROJECT WILL PROVIDE AND UPGRADE ON-SITE INTERNET SERVICE AND SECURITY, UPGRADE LAPTOP AND DESKTOP COMPUTERS, AND INCREASE LEARNING SOFTWARE OPTIONS AT RLNC. THE PROJECT WILL PROVIDE DIGITAL DEVICES AND BROADBAND SERVICE PLANS FOR RLNC STUDENTS TO ENABLE ACCESS ON AND OFF CAMPUS. THE PROJECT WILL PROVIDE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT AND CAPACITY BUILDING OPPORTUNITIES FOR RLNC IT STAFF, CYBERSECURITY TRAINING FOR RLNC FACULTY, STAFF, AND STUDENTS, AND DRONE TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION FOR COMMUNITY MEMBERS, INCLUDING A YOUTH DRONE CAMP. OUTCOMES: OVERALL, THIS PROJECT WILL RESULT IN UPDATES TO THE RED LAKE NATION COLLEGE CAMPUS BUILDINGS, DEVICES, AND SOFTWARE AND STAFF AND STUDENTS BETTER ABLE TO HANDLE CURRENT DIGITAL AND BROADBAND EDUCATIONAL NEEDS. THE COLLEGE AND ITS COMMUNITY WILL BE BETTER PREPARED FOR FUTURE INNOVATIONS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE UNINTERRUPTED ONLINE EVENTS, ENHANCED FUTURE-PROOF INFRASTRUCTURE, IMPROVED ACCESS TO UPDATED EQUIPMENT, AND ULTIMATELY IMPROVED DELIVERY OF COURSES AND INTERACTIONS WITH STUDENTS.  RLNC STUDENTS WILL HAVE EASIER ACCESS TO RELIABLE DEVICES FOR LEARNING AND ASSIGNMENTS, AND STUDENTS WILL PARTICIPATE MORE IN ONLINE COMPONENTS OF LEARNING.  THE IT STAFF PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT AND RLNC STAFF CYBERSECURITY TRAINING WILL RESULT IN MORE KNOWLEDGEABLE AND SKILLED IT STAFF, BETTER IT SUPPORT AND SECURITY FOR RLNC STAFF AND STUDENTS, AND MORE SECURE PERSONAL AND SHARED DIGITAL USE AND DATA AT RLNC.  PARTICIPANTS IN DRONE TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION WILL BECOME LICENSED DRONE PILOTS LEADING TO A POOL OF LOCAL AND REGIONAL COMMUNITY MEMBERS WITH DRONE KNOWLEDGE, CERTIFICATION, AND CONTACTS AROUND THE COUNTRY. BENEFICIARIES: STUDENTS, FACULTY, AND STAFF OF RED LAKE NATION COLLEGE WILL BENEFIT FROM THE BROADBAND UPGRADES AND IMPROVED ACCESS ON CAMPUS, AVAILABILITY OF DEVICES AND SOFTWARE, AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING. COMMUNITY MEMBERS ON THE RED LAKE NATION INDIAN RESERVATION IN MINNESOTA, WHERE THE COLLEGE IS LOCATED, WILL ALSO BENEFIT FROM THE DRONE TRAINING OPPORTUNITIES. SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES (IF APPLICABLE): THE RECIPIENT DOES NOT INTEND TO SUBAWARD FUNDS.

awards.Description: PURPOSE: LARGE MASSES OF DERELICT FISHING GEAR (DFG) ACCUMULATE ON CORAL REEFS AND SHORELINES ACROSS THE HAWAIIAN ARCHIPELAGO AND AROUND THE WORLD. INDIVIDUAL NET MASSES HAVE BEEN KNOWN TO WEIGH UP TO 11 TONS EACH AND POSE A DANGEROUS AND LETHAL THREAT TO ALL MANNER OF WILDLIFE, ENACT SEVERE HARM TO CORAL REEFS AND OTHER MARINE AND COASTAL SUBSTRATES, AND POSE A HAZARD TO NAVIGATION. THE LARGE VOLUME AND MASS OF MANY OF THESE DERELICT FISHING NETS AND OTHER GHOST GEAR MAKES WHOLESALE REMOVAL IN ONE PIECE VERY DIFFICULT AND OFTEN IMPOSSIBLE TO ACHIEVE SAFELY. THE TEAM PROPOSES TO CONCEPTUALIZE, DEVELOP, FABRICATE, AND TEST INNOVATIVE SOLUTIONS TO ACHIEVE ADVANCEMENTS IN DFG REMOVAL AND SHARE AND TRANSFER TECHNOLOGIES DEVELOPED TO ORGANIZATIONS WORLDWIDE TO ASSIST IN REMOVING DFG. IN 2024, SC STATE UNIVERSITY ADVANCED ITS MISSION OF TEACHING, RESEARCH, AND OUTREACH BY DELIVERING IMPACTFUL PROGRAMS ACROSS 32 SOUTH CAROLINA COUNTIES. THE UNIVERSITY FOCUSED ON FIVE CRITICAL ISSUES: AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, ENVIRONMENTAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES CONSERVATION, FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, AND FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION. THESE INITIATIVES AIMED TO IMPROVE THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR CITIZENS THROUGH EDUCATION, INNOVATION, AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT. UNDER AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, SC STATE ADDRESSED CHALLENGES SUCH AS UNEMPLOYMENT, POVERTY, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS. STAFF FACILITATED 20 WORKSHOPS, 7 IN-PERSON, 7 HYBRID, AND 6 VIRTUAL, REACHING 9,269 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,948 HOURS AND 37,727 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,402 HOURS. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS REPORTED KNOWLEDGE GAINS, AND SIX JOBS WERE CREATED THROUGH A RURAL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE GRANT. IN AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, THE SMALL FARMS TEAM CONDUCTED 58 WORKSHOPS AND WORKED WITH OVER 577 SMALL FARMERS. THEY LOGGED 3,969 DIRECT CONTACT HOURS WITH 16,269 INDIVIDUALS AND 1,516 INDIRECT HOURS WITH 9,678 CONTACTS. PROGRAMMING COVERED ANIMAL PRODUCTION, SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE, AND RISK MANAGEMENT. TOPICS INCLUDED BEEF CATTLE HERD HEA LTH, COLLABORATIVE MARKETING, AGRICULTURAL TAXES, AND NRCS SERVICES. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS HAD ACRES IMPACTED BY INTEGRATED CROP MANAGEMENT, REFLECTING A 56% INCREASE FROM THE PREVIOUS YEAR. A TOTAL OF 117 FARMERS REPORTED PROFIT GAINS, AND 650 PRODUCERS ADOPTED RECOMMENDED PRACTICES. FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS INCLUDED 400 WORKSHOPS FOCUSED ON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FINANCIAL LITERACY, S.T.R.E.A.M. (SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, READING, ENGINEERING, AGRICULTURE/ARTS, AND MATHEMATICS), AND THE AGRICULTURE INNOVATION SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM. STAFF MADE 43,896 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 4 ,360 HOURS AND REACHED 1,484,408 INDIVIDUALS INDIRECTLY OVER 2,004 HOURS. YOUTH PARTICIPATED IN HANDS-ON LEARNING SUCH AS CODING, DRONE EXPLORATION, AND ROBOTICS, FOSTERING LIFELONG SKILLS AND CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY. FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION INITIATIVES WERE DELIVERED THROUGH 1,181 WORKSHOPS PROMOTING HEALTHY BEHAVIORS AND DISEASE PREVENTION. STAFF COLLABORATED WITH ADVISORY COUNCILS AND STAKEHOLDERS TO DESIGN EVIDENCE-BASED NUTRITION EDUCATION PROGRAMS. THEY MADE 22,720 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 2,969 HOURS AND 332,595 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,289 HOURS. SIXTY-EIGHT PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS GAINED NUTRITION KNOWLEDGE. EIGHTEEN MEMORANDUMS OF UNDERSTANDING WERE SIGNED WITH EXTERNAL AGENCIES. STAFF EARNED SERVSAFE AND UTAH STATE NUTRITION CERTIFICATIONS. THE EFNEP EXPLORERS CAMP GRADUATED 636 YOUTH, ALL OF WHOM REPORTED INTENTIONS TO DRINK MORE WATER AND EAT MORE FRUITS. SC STATE'S RESEARCH PORTFOLIO INCLUDED SIX PROJECTS: AI AND DATA ANALYSIS, PHYTOCHEMICALS FROM SWEET POTATOES, FOOD HUB NETWORKS, AIENABLED SMART FARMING IOT SYSTEMS, POLYCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBONS IN SOIL, AND ATMO SPHERIC WATER GENERATION FROM RECYCLED PLASTICS. THREE PROJECTS CONCLUDED IN JULY 2025 AND PREPARED BULLETINS FOR PUBLICATION; THREE REMAIN ACTIVE. THESE RESEARCH EFFORTS HAVE BROAD IMPLICATIONS FOR SUSTAINABILITY, INNOVATION, AND AGRICULTURAL ADVANCEMENT. COLLECTIVELY, SC STATE UNIVERSITY'S PROGRAMS AND RESEARCH INITIATIVES DEMONSTRATE A STRONG COMMITMENT TO COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT, SCIENTIFIC PROGRESS, AND INCLUSIVE EDUCATION, SERVING AS A CATALYST FOR POSITIVE CHANGE ACROSS SOUTH CAROLINA.
